BULLOCK, FREDERICK SHORE, C.I.E., late Indian C.S., Berar.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 7th Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as mag. and collr., joint mag., asst. supt. of Dehra Dun, and small cause court judge; mag. and collr., April, 1889; jud. commr., Berar, April, 1892; commr., Hyderabad assigned dists., Oct., 1895; C.I.E., June, 1897; ret'd., April, 1899.

BULLOCK, ROBERT, Col., I.S.C., late Berar Comm.—First commn., Madras infantry, 11th Dec., 1858; from July, 1862, served in the Berar commn., as asst. commr., offg. asst. rest., and dep. commr. for demarcation of the Dhangs frontier, 1886; jud. commr., Aug., 1888; civil and sessions judge, July, 1889; ret'd., April, 1891.

BULMAN, ALBERT ROBINSON, late Indian C.S., Punjab.—Educ. at Richmond Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. sec. to finl. commr.; jud. asst., March, 1878; dep. commr., Dec., 1882; offg. commr. and supt., Lahore, Aug., 1884; and Ambala, Jan., 1887; ret'd., April, 1889.

BUNBURY, CECIL EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (judl. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 21st Nov., 1885, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., July, 1896; judl. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901.

BURBRIDGE, DAVID JOHN, late Finl. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, Feb., 1868; served in Fin. Dept., Burma, as asst. compt., Feb., 1890; asst. acct.-gen., Bombay, Jan., 1893; ret'd., Jan., 1899; author of 'Guide for investors in and holders of government securities.'

BURGESS, JAMES, C.I.E., LL.D., Hon. A.R.I.B.A., M. Soc. As. Paris, late Archaeological Survey of India.—Educ. at Dumfries, Glasgow, and Edinburgh; apptd. archaeological surveyor and reporter for W. India, 15th Jan., 1874; also archaeological surveyor for S. India, Nov., 1881; dir.-gen. of the archaeological survey of India, March, 1886; fellow of the univ. of Bombay; ret'd., 1889; in 1894, attended the Geneva oriental congress as representative of India; editor of 'The Indian Antiquary,' 1872-84; author of 'Views of Architecture and Scenery in Gujarat and Rajputana,' 'The Temples of Satrunjaya,' 'Somanath, Girnar, &c.,' 'Reports of the Archaeological Survey of W. and S. India,' 'Notes on the Rock Temples of Ajanta, their Paintings and Sculptures,' 'Elephanta,' 'Epigraphia Indica,' &c., and, conjointly with the late J. Ferguson, 'The Rock Temples of India.'

BURKE, CHARLES TOLER, B.E., M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1876; under sec., May, 1892; chairman, Bombay Port Trust, May, 1898; addl. member of govrs. council; suptg. engr., July, 1898; author of articles on various waterworks schemes; ret'd. Sept., 1900.

BURKE, JOHN, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept., 2nd June, 1864; supt., Aug., 1883; offg. dir. of traffic, Feb., June, 1886; has held charge of various divns.; ret'd., 1898.

BURKE, WILLIAM HENRY, M.B., Major, Indian M.S. (surgn., Gokaldas Tejpal Hosp., Bombay).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1882, and was placed on gen. duty; with Upper Burma Field Force, 1886; asst. civil surgn., Poona, Aug., 1889; medl. officer to Kathiawar pol. agency, March, 1897; surgn., Gokaldas Tejpal Hosp., April, 1901.

BURKITT, HAROLD HAMILTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Galway Grammar Sch. and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BURKITT, WILLIAM JOHN DWYER, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 1st Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. and mag. collr., and asst. settl. officer; joint mag. and settl. officer, March, 1900.

BURKITT, WILLIAM ROBERT, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (judge, High Court, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 11th Oct., 1861;

from Oct., 1862, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1887; addl. jud. commr. in Oudh, March, 1891; jud. commr. in Oudh, July, 1891; acting puisne judge, high court, N.W. Prov., Nov., 1892; confid., June, 1895.

BURLS EDWIN GRANT (dir.-gen. of stores, India Office).—Temporary clerk, India office, 20th Oct., 1863; junr. clerk, store dept., Dec., 1865; senr. clerk, Jan., 1875; asst. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1891; designation changed to dep. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1895; dir.-gen. of stores, August, 1896.

BURLTON, CHARLES HENRY BEAUMONT, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875; exec. engr., March, 1887; dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt., public works dept., April, 1894.

BURLTON, HENRY MONTGOMERY BARTON, Col., I. S. C.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, Aug., 1855; served in the Mutiny (medal and clasp); served subsequently in the Bhutan campaign, 1862, and the Abyssinian expedn., 1867–68 (medal); asst. cantonmt. mag., Morar, 1870; cantonmt. mag., Mhow, 1881, Morar, Dec., 1884, and Ambala, Nov., 1886; reverted to mil. duty, 1891.

BURLTON, PHILIP SYKES MURPHY, Captain, Indian S.C. (dist. judge, Punjab).—In mil. employ from 11th Jan., 1886; asst. commr., Oct., 1893; dist. judge, May, 1901.

BURN, JAMES MONTAGUE, Capt., R.E., Survey of India (dep. supt.).—First commn., 17th Feb., 1886; asst. engr., mily. works dept., April, 1888; apptd. to survey of India as asst. supt., May, 1891; dep. supt., Dec., 1896.

BURN, JOHN GUNN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Inverness Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 24th Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BURN, RICHARD, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Liverpool Inst., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., June, 1896; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1897; supt. of census operations, April, 1900.

BURN, ROBERT NATHANIEL, India Public Works Dept. (acctt.-genl., P.W.D., and dep. sec. to govt. of India).—Apptd. to the dept., Aug., 1870; dep. examr., Jan., 1880; examr., Jan., 1882; auditor, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, April, 1886; govt. examr. of accts., E. Indian railway, Feb., 1889; examr. of telegraph accts., April, 1890; dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, Jan., 1895; offg. under sec., public works dept., March, 1895; confid. Dec., 1897; acctt.-genl., P.W. dept., and dep. sec. to govt. of India, 1901.

BURN-MURDOCH, JOHN, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 14th Feb., 1876, and employed on state railways; served in the Afghan war, 1878–80 (medal with two clasps); employed in mil. works dept., 1880; offg. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Dec., 1881; on service with the Egyptian expedn., 1882 (medal with two clasps, star, and 5th class Medjidie); dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lahore, Nov., 1882; in same

capacity at Bombay, March, 1890; exec. engr., Jan., 1887; in 1890–92 offd. as under sec. to govt. of Bombay, railway branch; services lent to Assam-Bengal railway co., Sept., 1892; and to S. Mahratta railway co., Dec., 1894; suptg. engr., May, 1899; ret'd. May, 1900.

BURNÉ, HENRY KNIGHTLEY, C.B., Gen., I.S.C.—Ensign, Dec., 1843; arrived, 25th May, 1844, and served throughout the Sutlej campaign, 1845–46 (medal and clasp), and during the Burmese war of 1852–53 (medal and clasp); acted as cantonmt. joint mag., Ambala, Nov., 1849; also as brigade major, Ambala, 1849–51, and as dep. asst. adjutant-gen., Sirhind divn., 1851–52; dep. judge advocate-gen., April, 1852; 2nd asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., July, 1856; 1st asst. sec., Jan., 1858; dep. sec., March, 1862; sec., June, 1870, to Dec., 1881; C.B., May, 1875.

BURNE, KNIGHTLEY GREY (dep. commr., Burma).—Served in the Bengal police as asst. supt., from 26th Oct., 1865 to May, 1872; from the latter date served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., and acted as town mag. and supt. of jail at Maulmain, 1872–75; offg. dir. of public instrn., 1882; dep. commr., July, 1886; acting addl. sessions judge, March, 1895, and January, 1896; offg. judge, Moumein, Jan., 1898, and offg. commr., Feb., 1898.

BURNE, SIR OWEN TUDOR, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., Major-Gen., late of the Council of India.—Ensign, 20th foot, 15th April, 1855; served in the Crimea; also in the Mutiny of 1856–58, and was present in fifteen actions and received two steps of rank for gallantry in the field; apptd. mil. sec. to the commander-in-chief in India (Lord Strathnairn), 1861–62, and afterwards A.D.C., and at times offg. mil. sec., to that gen. officer when commander of the forces in Ireland, 1865–68; private sec. to viceroy of India (Earl of Mayo), 1869–72; pol. A.D.C. to sec. of state for India, 1872; asst. sec. in pol. and secret dept., India office, 1873; sec. in that dept., 1874; on special duty in India as private sec. to the viceroy (Lord Lytton), 1876–78; member of the council of India, Jan., 1887 to Dec., 1896; G.C.I.E., Jan. 1897; was a royal commr. of the Health and the Colonial and Indian exhibits. of 1882 and 1886; vice-prest. of the society of arts, 1888–94; apptd. by H. M. the Queen a govr. of the Imperial Institute, 1891; and a royal commr. for the World's Exposition in Chicago, 1891–93; vice-prest. of the council of India, 1891–92; ret'd. from the army, Jan., 1894; chairman of the society of arts, 1896–97; a knight of grace of the order of St. John of Jerusalem; author of 'Clyde and Strathnairn,' &c.

BURRARD, SIDNEY GERALD, Major, R.E., Survey of India (supt. trigonol. survey).—First commn., 6th April, 1879; attached to mil. works dept., Baluchistan, March, 1883; asst. supt. trigonol. survey, Sept., 1884; dep. supt., June, 1889; supt., Oct., 1899.

BURRIDGE, CHARLES EDWARD (2nd class clerk, Store Dept., India Office).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 16th March, 1888.

BURROWS, LEONARD ROBERT, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Lincoln Diocesan Sch., St. Nicholas Coll., Lancing, and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1863; asst. collr. and mag., Dec., 1865; prest. of municipal commn., Madras, 1875–80; sub-collr. and joint mag., March, 1882; acting collr. of sea customs, Madras, and protector of emigrants,

Sept., 1883; collr. and mag. and addl. sessions judge, Nilgiris, Sept., 1884; retd., Aug., 1889.

BURT, HENRY PARSELL, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. manager, N.W. Rly.).—Apptd. from Royal Indian Engrg. Coll. as asst. engr., Sept., 1878; employed at Liverpool on works of L. and N.W. railway; posted to state railways, July, 1879; exec. engr., Nov., 1892; offd. as under sec. to govt. of India, March, 1895; dep. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., Nov., 1896; dep. manager, N.W. Rly., March, 1901.

BURTON, FRANCIS HELY, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-sec. to govt., Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 21st Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1900.

BURTON, WILLIAM HENRY, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 26th July, 1857; was apptd. to the dept., May, 1858, and served in Oudh and Hyderabad; exec. engr., July, 1865; exchanged for temporary service out of India, and apptd. to Portsmouth district, and charge of Needles divn., Aug., 1867; proceeded to China, Nov., 1875, where he acted in 1876-77, as commanding royal engrs.; returned to India, Nov., 1877; member of defence committee on coast defences, Dec., 1877; exec. engr., Madras, Jan., 1878; suptg. engr., April, 1889; retd., Dec., 1890.

BUSTEED, HENRY ELMSLEY, C.I.E., M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Mint Dept., Calcutta.—First commn., Indian med. service, Aug., 1855; arrived, March, 1856, and served through the Mutiny campaign (medal with clasp); civil surgn., Cuddalore, Jan., 1861; asst. assay master, Madras mint, Feb., 1865; acted as assay master for various periods, and also as mint master and commr. of paper currency; dep. assay master, Bombay, April, 1868; dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, Nov., 1870; assay master, Sept., 1872; also acted as mint master in 1873 and 1875; retd., June, 1886; C.I.E., Jan., 1887; author of 'Echoes from Old Calcutta.'

BUTLER, JAMES, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C.—Ensign, 19th foot, Feb., 1866; arrived in Madras, 24th May, 1866; served in the Burma commn. from Oct., 1868, as asst. commr. and town mag.; dep. commr., July, 1881; addl. sess. judge, Bassein; placed on unemployed superny. list, March, 1901; author of the 'Mergui Gazetteer.'

BUTLER, MONTAGU SHERARD DAWES, B.A., Indian C.S. (junr. sec. to finl. commr.).—Educ. at Haileybury and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 26th Nov., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Nov., 1900.

BUTLER, SPENCER HARCOURT, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (settlt. offr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 29th Oct., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. sec., board of rev., March, 1892; asst. settlt. officer, Dec., 1893; settlt. officer, Dec., 1894; C.I.E., Nov., 1901.

BUTLER, THEOBALD, B.A., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879; exec. engr., Sept., 1891; inspr. of works, Behar divn., Feb., 1898.

BUTLER, WILLIAM GEORGE (dep.-dir.-gen., Store Dept., India Office).—Educ. at Bradford and Eton; junr. clerk, store dept., India office,

31st Jan., 1867; asst. private sec. and précis writer to sec. of state, 1874-78; senr. clerk, Dec., 1889; dep. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1900.

BUTTERWORTH, ALAN, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 15th Dec., 1885, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec., rev. dept., Aug., 1893; judge, and sessions judge, March, 1897; divl. judge, Feb., 1898; sub-collr. and joint mag. Aug., 1898.

BUTTS, HENRY HILL, B.L., late Oudh Commn.—Extra asst. commr., 3rd June, 1863; from Nov., 1865, served as asst. settlt. officer and asst. commr.; subord. judge, Faizabad, Aug., 1879; district judge, Faizabad, Jan., 1882; dep. commr., Jan., 1882; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1892; retd., Aug., 1893; author of the Lucknow settlt. report.

BUYERS, JOHN WALKER, C.I.E., M.A., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. exec. engr., N.W. Prov., 18th Nov., 1869; engr.-in-chief, Garo hills survey, Dec., 1881; suptg. engr., March, 1887; C.I.E., May, 1889; engr.-in-chief, Mu valley railway, July, 1889; retd., 1891.

BYATT, JOHN (supervisor, Store Dept., India Office).—Asst. clerk, educn. dept., May, 1869; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, May, 1873; supervisor, 2nd grade, Nov., 1880; 1st grade, July, 1888.

BYRNE, JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Royal Univ. of Ireland and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BYTHELL, WILLIAM JOHN, Major, R.E., Survey of India (supt.).—First commn., 25th July, 1882; served as survey officer in Bechuanaland in 1884-85; apptd. asst. engr. Sind-Pishin railway, July, 1887; joined survey of India as asst. supt., May, 1889; dep. supt., April, 1894; supt., Oct., 1900; served as survey officer in the Chin-Lushai expedition, 1894-95, and with the Chitral Relief Force, 1895.

CABELL, WILLIAM HENRY LAWSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Eton and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 30th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Oct., 1900.

CADELL, ALAN, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., Guetersloh Gymnasium, Westphalia, and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; from April, 1867, to March, 1881, served in the settlt. dept.; mag. and collr., May, 1880; offg. commr. of excise and stamps and inspr.-gen. of registrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1883; offg. commr., May, 1889; junr. member, board of rev., April, 1893; senr. member, March, 1894; also member of provl. legisl. council, Nov., 1893; lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan. to Nov., 1895; C.S.I., May, 1895; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Oct., 1895; member of govr.-gen.'s council, Feb.-May, 1896; member board of rev., May, 1896; addl. member of legisl. council, Dec., 1896-April, 1897; retd., Oct., 1897.

CADELL, ALEXANDER, Lieut.-Gen., late R.E.—2nd lieut., Bengal engrs., 13th June, 1845; joined the public works dept., April, 1848; exec. engr., Rawal Pindi, 1850; Nagpur, 1856; Oudh road dept., Dec., 1858; suptg. engr., Punjab, 1864; from 1867 to 1878 served in Indore, as suptg. engr., and chief engr., and sec. to the govr.-gen.'s agent for Central India; also offd. as chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., 1874-75; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1879-80; dep. inspr.-gen. of mil. works, 1881; inspr.-gen., mil. works, 1881-82; ret'd., March, 1882.

CADELL, PATRICK ROBERT, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 7th Dec., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and administrator of Sachin state; 2nd asst., May, 1897; chairman, Karachi plague commn., July, 1898; under-sec. to govt., rev., etc., depts., Sept., 1900, to April, 1901.

CADELL, THOMAS, V.C., Col., I.S.C.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, April, 1854; arrived, July, 1854; served during the Mutiny (medal, clasp, and Victoria cross); second in command, Malwa Bhil corps, dep. Bhil agent, and pol. asst., April, 1861; pol. agent, E. states, Rajputana, April, 1870; pol. agent, Alwar, Nov., 1870; pol. agent, Marwar, Nov., 1877, and in Mewar, April, 1878; commandant, Erinpura force, and pol. agent, W. Rajputana, Oct., 1879; chief commr. and supt., Port Blair and the Nicobars, Dec., 1879; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1892.

CALCRAFT-KENNEDY, BENNET CHRISTIAN HUNTINGDON, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., Queen's Coll., Oxford, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., July, 1897.

CALLAGHAN, THOMAS (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept., India Office).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 25th March, 1885.

CALNAN, DENIS, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at French Coll., Blackrock, Ireland, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 13th Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag.; joint mag., Oct., 1891; dep. commr., March, 1897; mag. and collr., Nov., 1899.

CALVERT, HUBERT, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 28th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CAMA, ARDESHIR KAIKHOSHRO, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. in India and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CAMERON, CHARLES, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 27th July, 1859; arrived, 28th Jan., 1860; was employed under the mil. dept., 1860-66, and served in the Bhutan campaign (medal); from April, 1866, served in Oudh as civil surgn. and supt. of jail; ret'd., Sept., 1892.

CAMERON, DUNCAN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Aberdeen

Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 30th Nov., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1887; in 1889 served in the Punjab as offg. divl. judge; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1894.

CAMERON, LEWIS, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1866; from Feb., 1873, served as civil asst. surgn. and surgn. in Bengal; ret'd., Aug., 1891.

CAMERON, WILLIAM LOCHIEL, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Educ. at Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; entered the service as asst. engr., Sept., 1877; exec. engr., Sept., 1889.

CAMMIADE, PAUL EUGENE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Madras Univ., Caius Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CAMPBELL, ALEXANDER WILLIAM DENNISTOWN, Lt.-Col., I.S.C., late Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1871; served under the mil. dept. to April, 1882, when he was apptd. cantonmt. mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD EDWARDES, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Assam Commn.—First commn., Jan., 1851; served in the Sonthal campaign, 1855-56, and in Central India, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); from May, 1862, served in the Assam commn.; dep. commr., Dec., 1866; inspr. of labourers, Sibsagar, Feb., 1882; reverted to mil. dept., 1890.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD YOUNG GIPPS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

CAMPBELL, ARTHUR FREDERICK TRAVERS (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 18th March, 1893.

CAMPBELL, CHARLES STEWART, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 25th Nov., 1899 and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CAMPBELL, COLIN GEORGE (asst. sec., Pol. Dept., India Office).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; junr. clerk, mil. dept., 13th Jan., 1876; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, 1877-92; senr. clerk, Feb., 1889; reading clerk to council, Aug., 1890, to Aug., 1897; private sec. to sec. of state (Right Hon. H. H. Fowler), March, 1894, to July, 1895; sec. to royal commn. on Indian expenditure, Aug., 1895; asst. sec., pol. dept., Aug., 1897.

CAMPBELL, DUNCAN JOHN ALFRED, Indian C.S. (div. judge, Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 24th Nov., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.; transfed. to Burma, Aug., 1878, and served as asst. commr. and boundary officer; cantonmt. mag., and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, 1883; on special duty in Upper Burma, Feb., 1886; dep. commr., May, 1888; addl. sessions judge, June-Oct., 1894; transfed. to Cent. Prov. as dep. commr., Nov., 1895; div. judge, May, 1896.

CAMPBELL, DUNCAN McNEIL, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 8th April, 1869, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; under sec. to govt., public works dept., 1885-86; suptg. engr., July, 1890; chief engr. and sec. to govt., public works dept., Mysore, July, 1892, to July, 1894; joint sec. to govt. of Madras, gen. buildings and roads branch, Jan., 1896; chief engr., Jan., 1896; ret'd., April, 1898.

CAMPBELL, FRANCIS JOHNSTONE GRAHAM, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 20th Jan., 1864, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and district and sessions judge; ret'd., Oct., 1890.

CAMPBELL, GEORGE ALLEN, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Joined the service, 5th Aug., 1873, as asst. engr., and was employed in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh on provl. railways and other duties; exec. engr., May, 1883; services lent to Indian Midland railway co., 1887-88; transf'd. to state railways, March, 1891; supt. of works, Cawnpore-Gogra connection, Dec., 1894; suptg. engr., July, 1898.

CAMPBELL, GERALD EDWARD LYON, late Indian C.S., Madras.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 11th Dec., 1883, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.; sub. collr. and joint mag., Mar., 1896; ret'd., May, 1898.

CAMPBELL, HENRY WILLIAM, Captain Indian Staff Corps (late Burma Police).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; served with the Zhoab valley expdn., 1890; apptd. asst. comdt. Burma mil. police battn., Oct., 1893; comdt., May, 1894; dep. inspr.-gen. of police for supply and clothing, Dec., 1896; reverted to mil. duty with 18th B. Lancers, Nov., 1898.

CAMPBELL, SIR JAMES McNABB, K.C.I.E., M.A., LL.D., late Indian C.S., Bombay.—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, April-Aug., 1877; acting municipal commr., Bombay, April, 1880; acting under sec. to govt. in the rev., finl., and gen. depts., Oct., 1880; acted as collr. of Bombay and supt. of stamps and stationery in 1881; C.I.E., Jan., 1885; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1890; collr. of land rev., customs and opium, and supt. of stamps and stationery, Nov., 1891; senr. collr., Aug., 1893; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; compiler of 'Bombay Gazetteer.'; ret'd., April, 1900.

CAMPBELL, JOHN, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

CAMPBELL, JOHN STRATHEDEN, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 14th Dec., 1883, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1890; dep. commr., Feb., 1896; dist. and sessions judge, July, 1896; mag. and collr., April, 1898.

CAMPBELL, RICHARD HAMILTON, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Edinburgh Univ.;

apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.; acting under sec., rev. dept., in 1892 and 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., Aug., 1897.

CAMPBELL, ROBERT NEIL, M.B., Lt. Col., Indian Medical Service (civil surg., Khulna).—Joined the service, Oct., 1877; in military employ till Aug., 1883; since which date he has been civil surgeon at various stations in Bengal.

CAMPBELL, WILLIAM EDWARD MARMADUKE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

CAMPBELL-WALKER, INCHES, Col., I.S.C., late Forest Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 27th Dec., 1859; from May, 1865, served in the Madras forest dept. as asst. and dep. conservator; apptd. a commr. for the Vienna exhibn., 1873; knight commander of the order of Francis Joseph of Austria for services there; offg. inspr. of forests, Feb., 1874, to Sept., 1875; on duty at the forest conference, Simla, Sept., 1875; on special duty to New Zealand, 1876-77, and in the Nilgiris, S. Coimbatore, and Tinnevell, Dec., 1877; conservator of forests, 1st grade, Dec., 1884; ret'd., Jan., 1892; author of reports on forest management in Germany, Austria, and Great Britain (1872), and on the forests of New Zealand.

CAMPION, JOHN MONTRIOU, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Apptd. to the dept., 1st July, 1869, as asst. engr., and employed on state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transf'd. to Punjab provl. establ., Aug., 1881; served temporarily in mil. works dept., 1881, and on state railways, 1884-85; suptg. engr., July, 1896; offg. sec. to govt., Punjab, July-Oct., 1899; chief engr. and sec. to govt., Punjab, July, 1900.

CANDY, CHARLES HARRISON, B.A., LL.M., late Indian Educn. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st March, 1875, as acting vice-principal, Elphinstone high sch.; head master, Maharaja's coll., Mysore, June, 1876; principal and prof'r. of English literature, Rajaram coll., Kolhapur, June, 1880; principal, Jan., 1886; ret'd., April, 1899.

CANDY, EDWARD TOWNSHEND, B.L., Indian C.S. (judge, High Court, Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge and sessions judge; jud. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1878; acting jud. commr. and judge of the sadar court in Sind, May, 1883; judge and sessions judge, Aug., 1883; acting remembrancer of legal affairs, July-Nov., 1885; judge of high court, Bombay, April, 1892.

CANDY, RICHARD EDWARD, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acting dir. of public instrn., Berar, June, 1872, to Dec., 1873; first asst. collr. and mag., Oct., 1877; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1885; senr. collr., Sept., 1888; ret'd., 1899.

CANTOPHER, BERNARD WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 29th Aug., 1873, and posted to state

railways; transf. to Bengal, Aug., 1881; again employed on railways, from Nov., 1883; exec. engr., May, 1884; services lent to Mahratta railway co., 1888-89; offg. engr. in-chief, Burma state railway, Sept.-Dec., 1893; retd., Oct., 1901.

CAPPEL, SIR ALBERT JAMES LEPPOC, K.C.I.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Appdt. to the dept., 10th Feb., 1837; 1st grade supt. and personal asst. to dir.-gen., Dec., 1866; dir. of traffic, May, 1869; dep. dir.-gen., Feb., 1879; dir.-gen. of telegraphs, June, 1883; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; retd., 1889.

CAPPEL, EDWARD LOUIS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (senr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 28th Oct., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from May, 1882, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; dir. of land records and agric., June, 1891, to April, 1893; junr. collr. and mag., June, 1899; senr. collr., July, 1897; C.I.E., Jan., 1900.

CARD, HENRY CHARLES (supervisor, Store Dept., India Office).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp.; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 22nd Jan., 1875; supervisor, 2nd grade, Aug., 1900.

CARDEW, ALEXANDER GORDON, M.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at the Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 7th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to the Malabar land tenures committee, Dec., 1885; asst. sec., jud. and legisl. dept., Nov., 1888; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Aug., 1892; collr. and mag., Nov., 1899.

CARDEW, CORNELIUS EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept. (locomotive supt.).—Appdt. to the superior rev. establ., state railways, locomotive dept., March, 1876; locomotive supt., April, 1881; served on the Rajputana, Nagpur-Chhattisgarh, Rajputana-Malwa, Burma, and other railways.

CARDEW, HENRY MASTERS, India Public Works Dept. (locomotive supt.).—Appdt. to the dept. May, 1879; dep. locomotive supt., N.W. Rly., Nov., 1897; locomotive supt. Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, July, 1898.

CARDOZO, HENRY O'CONNELL, C.I.E., late Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Served in the dept. from 21st March, 1859, and was promoted to dep. supt., 1st class, March, 1883, and to supt., July, 1887; C.I.E., 1896; elected Fellow of the Madras Univ., 1898; retd., Feb., 1899.

CAREY, ARTHUR DOUGLAS, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at the City of London Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. collr. of salt rev.; collr. of salt rev., April, 1881; from Feb., 1882, served under the govt. of India as offg. commr., inland customs; commr., salt rev., N. India, Dec., 1882; commr. of customs, salt, opium and abbkari, and reporter-gen. of external commerce for Bombay, May, 1891; on special duty in Lisbon for Goa treaty negotiations, 1891-92; retd., March, 1893.

CAREY, BERTRAM SAUSMAREZ, C.I.E. (dep. commr., Burma).—Entered the service Sept. 1886; apptd. to Burma commn. as asst. commr., Jan., 1887; dep. commr., April, 1900.

CAREY, HIRZEL DENIS DE MUSSENDEN, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dept. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived

25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1897.

CAREY, HORACE RICHARD LE MARCHANT, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 15th Jan., 1861 served in the Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal with clasp); apptd. to the dept., 24th Dec., 1870, as asst. engr., and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; transf. to superior accts. dept., Sept., 1874; examr., Jan., 1882; employed on public works accts. in Burma, April, 1883; and in Bengal, June, 1888; placed on unemployed supy. list, June, 1897.

CAREY, LIONEL SLADE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, June to Dec., 1885; settlt. officer, Oct., 1886; dep. commr. and rev. sec., Dec., 1891; commr. of settlts. and agric., Jan., 1895.

CARGILL, JAMES DUDLEY, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Brighton Gr. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 13th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1897.

CARLESS, GEORGE PROCTOR, A.M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Feb., 1866; exec. engr., Oct., 1888; retired, Oct., 1898.

CARLIN, DANIEL, India Office (second class clerk, India store depôt).—Educ. at Glasgow Athenæum; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 22nd Nov., 1901.

CARLYLE, ROBERT WARRAND, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Dec., 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1890; has offd. also as under sec. to the govt. of India, home dept.; mag. and collr., June, 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1898.

CARMICHAEL, DAVID FREMANTLE, late Madras C.S.—Appdt. from Haileybury, 1851; asst. collr. and mag., Dec., 1851; head asst. registrar, court of Sadar and Foujdari Adalat, Feb., 1855; dep. sec. to govt., Oct., 1856; sec. to board of examrs., Oct., 1856; private sec. to govr. of Madras, March, 1859; fellow of Madras univ., Oct., 1859; collr. and mag. and agent, July, 1863; acting judge, high court, Madras, April to July, 1869; employed in preparation of Vizagapatam district manual; acting member, board of rev., Aug., 1871; inam commr., Aug., 1871; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1872; member of legisl. council, Feb., 1874; on special duty at imperial assemblage, Delhi, Dec., 1876; chief sec. to govt., June, 1877; ordinary member of council, Madras, Dec., 1878; on tour as special commr. through hill tracts of Ganjam, Vizagapatam, and Godavari, Feb., 1881; retd., 1886.

CARMICHAEL, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Univ. of Aberdeen, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; arrived, 4th Dec., 1836, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer;

transfd. to Burma as asst. commr., Aug., 1889; returned to Bombay as 2nd asst., Oct., 1894; 1st asst., Feb., 1897.

CARNDUFF, HERBERT WILLIAM CAMERON, Indian C.S. (dep. sec. to govt. of India, Legisl. Dept.).—Educ. at Edinburgh Coll. Sch., Univ. of Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 30th Nov., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, May, 1888; registrar, appellate side, high court, Calcutta, Oct., 1890; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1895; dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Dec., 1896.

CARNEGAY, HARRY GEORGE, Captain, I.S.C. (asst. to resdt., Baroda).—First commn., 30th Jan., 1886; joined the Bombay Staff Corps, 22 Jan., 1888; asst. pol. supt., Kathiawer, Sept., 1894; asst. pol. resdt., Baroda, July, 1900.

CARR, REGINALD CHILDERS CULLING, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Cheam Sch., the Charterhouse, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 7th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; addl. special asst. to district mag. and collr., March, 1889; asst. sec., jud. and legisl. depts., Sept., 1891; acted as private sec. to govr. in 1891, 1893, and 1895; under sec., rev. dept., Aug., 1892; under sec., in depts. under the chief sec., May, 1894; sec. to the commrs. land rev., Oct., 1896; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1900; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1900.

CARR, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Lancaster Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 14th Nov., 1893, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

CARR-CALTHROP, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, M.D., Colonel, I.M.S. (principal med. officer, Assam).—Joined Bengal medl. service, 1st April, 1869; on mil. duty as medl. officer of 4th B. Cav. till 1897; apptd. princ. medl. offir., presy. dist., Oct., 1897; princ. medl. offir. and sany. commr., Assam, May, 1899.

CARSON, WALTER PETER, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Belgaum).—First commn., 30th Sept., 1878; on mil. duty till Sept., 1894; port surg., Aden, Sept., 1894; civil, Belgaum, Jan., 1900.

CARSTAIRS, ROBERT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and Edinburgh Coll. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 16th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1886; dep. commr., Aug., 1888; author of 'British Work in India.'

CARTER, ALBERT MICHAEL (seur. clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; temporary clerk, India office, 4th Nov., 1861; junr. clerk, March, 1866; senr. clerk, Aug., 1882; assessor of income tax, 1892.

CARTER, GEORGE MARKHAM STRANGE, late Burma Commn.—Apptd. chief inspr. of police, June, 1875; asst. district supt., Feb., 1880, and district supt., Oct., 1882; apptd. asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., Dec., 1890; acted as prest., Rangoon municipality, Dec., 1893, and Jan., 1894; retired, 1898.

CARTER, PHILIP JOHN, late Burma Forest Dept.—Arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1875, and was apptd. to Burma as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, April, 1881; on duty at Port Blair, Nov., 1886; rejoined his Burma

appt., June, 1889; conservator, Feb., 1892; retired, July, 1897.

CARTER, RODERICK EDMUND, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the Dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Mar., 1892.

CASEY, ARTHUR EDWIN COMERFORD, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Portora Royal Sch., and Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Kumaon, May, 1882; ret'd., Oct., 1889.

CASPERSZ, CHARLES PETER, B.L., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 21st Dec., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and settlt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1889; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1894.

CASELS, WALTER SEYTOUN, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Provs. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

CASSON, HERBERT ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Punjab).—Educ. at Newton Abbot Proprietary Coll., Marlborough Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 15th Nov., 1889, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn., 1894-95; dep. commr., Oct., 1897; judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Feb., 1900.

CAVE-BROWNE, EDWARD RABAN, C.S.I., late acctnt.-gen., India Office.—Educ. at the Coll. Sch., Taunton; junr. clerk in the treasury, East India house, 12th April, 1854, and from 1858 in the India office; acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 1860; senr. clerk, June, 1867; asst. in the accts. branch of the finl. dept., Jan., 1872; assessor of income tax, 1872; dep. acctnt.-gen., Feb., 1879; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; acctnt.-gen., June, 1893; ret'd., May, 1900.

CAWLEY, GEORGE JOHN, late Police Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1862, as asst. supt. of police, Assam; district supt., Dec., 1876; transfd. to Bengal, Dec., 1883; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

CAYLEY, HENRY, Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1857; from April, 1872, served as civil surgn. and offg. supt. of vaccn., supt. of royal botanical gardens, ophthalmic surgn. and profir. of ophthalmic surgery, med. coll., Calcutta, supt. of the eye infirmary, and marine surgn.; med. board, India office, 1885; ret'd., April, 1887.

CHADWICK, DAVID THOMAS, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at De Aston Sch., Market Rasen, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

CHADWICK, WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Apptd. from Royal Indian Engrg. Coll. as asst. engr., Sept., 1878, and posted to State Rlys.; exec. engr., Oct., 1892.

CHALMERS, MACKENZIE DALZELL, C.S.I., M.A.—Educ. at King's Coll., London, and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. law member of the govr.-gen'l's council, 1896; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; resigned,

March, 1899; asst. parly. counsel to the Treasury, April, 1899; parly. counsel to ditto, Feb., 1901.

CHAMBERLAIN, JAMES THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

CHAMBERLAIN, SIR NEVILLE BOWLES, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., Field-Marshal.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, 1837; served through the Afghan campaigns of 1839-42 (medal); present with the govr.-gen.'s body-guard at the battle of Maharajpur, Dec., 1843 (bronze star); dep. asst. quartermaster-gen. to the army, 1843; hoA. A.D.C. to Lord Dalhousie, 1848; served in the Punjab campaign of 1848-49, including the actions of Chilianwala and Gujrat (medal and two clasps); mil. sec. to the chief commr. for affairs of the Punjab, March, 1853; commandant, Punjab irregular force, Dec., 1854; adjutant-gen. of the Bengal army, July, 1857; C.B., 1857; A.D.C. to the Queen for services in the Mutiny; commanded the force on the N.W. frontier of India in various expeds. against the hill tribes (medal and clasp); commander-in-chief, Madras, and member of the govr.'s council, Feb., 1876, to 1881; was apptd. head of the special mission to Kabul, Aug., 1878, which, however, was not allowed to enter the Amir's territory.

CHAMIER, EDWARD MAYNARD DES CHAMPS, B.L. (govt. advocate, high court, N.W.P.).—Entered the service as govt. advocate in the High Court, N.W.P., Jan., 1896.

CHAMIER, FRANCIS EDWARD ARCHIBALD, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, as ensign, 7th April, 1850; A.D.C. to Gen. Outram throughout the Oudh campaign; commanded the Kapurthala contingent during its service in Oudh, in 1858 (medal and two clasps); from March, 1858, served in Oudh as asst. commr., dep. commr., and offg. commr.; district judge, Rae Bareli, Feb., 1882, and afterwards at Faizabad; returned to Europe, 1888.

CHANNER, OSBORNE HENRY, M.B., C.M., Lt.-Col., Indian Med. Service (dep. sany. commr., southern reg. dist., Bombay).—Entered the service, Sept., 1876; apptd. to mil. duty; served in charge of Indian expedy. force, Malta and Cyprus, May-Sept., 1878; dep. sany. commr., Konkan dist., Mar., 1881; S. Deccan, Sept., 1881; southern reg. dist., Dec., 1888.

CHANNING, FRANCIS CHORLEY, late Indian C.S., Punjab.—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 25th Nov., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and settlt. officer; settlt. sec. to finl. commr., Jan., 1878; senr. sec. to the finl. commr., March, 1882; dep. commr., March, 1886; district judge, Jan., 1888, but to continue to act as divl. judge; divl. judge, May, 1890; acting judge, chief court, May-Dec., 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1895.

CHAPMAN, CHARLES (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 6th Aug., 1884.

CHAPMAN, EDMUND PELLY, B.A., Indian C.S. (registrar, high court, Calcutta).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 12th Nov., 1889, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and collr., Dec., 1895; asst.

sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Mar., 1896; registrar, high court, appellate side, Mar., 1899.

CHAPMAN, REGINALD CORRIE, Fin. and Commerce Dept., Govt. of India (dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1874, and was employed at Nagpur, and afterwards at Rangoon; asst. comptr.-gen. at Calcutta, Feb., 1879; transf. to Madras as acting asst. acctnt.-gen., March, 1879; confid., July, 1880; in addn., inspr. of local fund accts., April, 1882; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1891; ditto, Punjab, Feb., 1901; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, March, 1901.

CHAPMAN, ROBERT ALEXANDER BOSWELL, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 15th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., May, 1896.

CHAPMAN, ROBERT BARCLAY, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1850; arrived, 6th March, 1850; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; under sec. to govt. of India, home, finl., and foreign depts., April, 1856, to April, 1860; called to the bar (Inner Temple), Jan., 1863; junr. sec., board of rev., Lower Prov., June, 1863; acting commr., Nuddea divn., Nov., 1866, to Jan., 1869; served subsequently as sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., and govt. dir. of Bank of Bengal; ret'd., 1881.

CHARLES, ALBERT PENDRILL, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Trin. Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 6th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.

CHARLES, FENDALL LOWIS, late Indian C.S., Bombay.—Educ. at Repton Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty, demarcation of forests in Nasik, July, 1875; forest settlt. officer, Dharwar, Nov., 1881; acting municipal commr., Bombay, Oct., 1886; junr. collr. and mag., April, 1890; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1892; ret'd., Jan., 1898.

CHARLES, JOHN GEORGE, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 15th Dec., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under sec.; member of cattle plague commn., June, 1870; offg. dep. collr. of customs, Calcutta, July, 1871; offg. dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, March, 1876; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1876; acted as presidency and as chief presidency mag., Calcutta; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1885; ret'd., Jan., 1893.

CHARLES, RICHARD HAVELOCK, M.D., M.Ch., F.R.C.S.I., Major, Indian Med. Service (prof., Calcutta Med. Coll.).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1882, and served as prof. of anatomy, Lahore med. coll.; apptd. in similar capacity to Calcutta med. coll., April, 1894.

CHARLES, THOMAS GWTYHER (dist. supt., Bengal Police).—Entered the service 20th Mar., 1866, and served as asst. supt. and dist. supt. of police.

CHARLTON, ALFRED (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 8th April, 1882.

CHASE, WILLIAM HACKETT, Indian Public Works Dept. (dep. locom. supt.).—Joined the service 1st Dec., 1877, in the State Ry. rev. estab.; asst.-locom. supt., Feb., 1883; dist. locom. supt., July, 1889; dep. locom. supt., May, 1900.

CHATFIELD, GEORGE ERNLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Dec., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CHATFIELD, KYRLE MITFORD, M.A., late Educ. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; served from 29th March, 1866, as principal and prof. of logic and moral philosophy at the Elphinstone coll., Bombay; dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1874; ret'd., April, 1897.

CHATTERTON, ALFRED, B.Sc. (prof., Madras Engrg. Coll.).—Apptd. prof. of engrg., Mad. Engrg. Coll., July, 1888.

CHATTERTON, FRANK WILLIAM, Col., Bengal Infantry.—Arrived in India as ensign, 4th Nov., 1859; employed under the mil. dept. till Feb., 1869, from which date he served in Jhansi and Kumaon as offg. asst. commr.; from May, 1873, served as cantonmt. mag. of Rurki; also small cause court judge, Rurki, March, 1879; cantonmt. mag., Agra, Dec., 1880, to Nov., 1886, when he was apptd. commandant of administrative battalion Bengal presidency volunteers; hon. A.D.C. to viceroy, Dec., 1888.

CHENEVIX-TRENCH, GEORGE FREDERICK, Major, I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (gov.-gen.'s agent in Khorasan and Seistan).—First commn., 22nd Jan., 1879; served in the Burma expdn., 1886; offg. asst. to gov.-gen.'s agent in Rajputana, June, 1888; on depn. in Kotah and Jhallawar, 1888-89; acting asst. district supt. of police, Quetta, Dec., 1889; also asst. commr., April, 1890; offg. first asst. to pol. rest., Persian Gulf, Aug., 1890; acting pol. agent, Basra, April, 1891; British joint commr. at Leh, Dec., 1891; asst. rest. for Leh, Kashmir, Dec., 1894; pol. offr. with Sirdar Ayub Khan, May, 1899; gov.-gen.'s agent in Khorasan and Seistan, May, 1901.

CHERRY, JOHN WILLIAM, late Forest Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 5th Feb. 1869, as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, Jan., 1872; conservator, Oct., 1891; ret'd., July, 1900.

CHESNEY, HAROLD FRANK, Major, R.E., Indian Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—First commn., 31st Jan., 1878; apptd. to the mily. works dept. as asst. engr., March, 1882; exec. engr., March 1890; to public works dept., Punjab, Nov., 1895.

CHESTER, EDWARD G. (dep. conservator, Bengal Forest Dept.).—Apptd. to Forest Dept., Dec., 1875; dep. conservator, Sept., 1882.

CHEVIS, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Lancing Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 4th Dec., 1885, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, April, 1893; dep. commr., Jan., 1899; divl. judge, Nov., 1900.

CHILDE, LETTERSTEDT FREDERICK, M.B., Major, I.M.S. (prof., Grant Med. Coll., Bombay).—First commn., 31 March, 1887; on mily. duty till Dec., 1889, when he was appointed physician J. J. Hospital, and prof., Grant Med. Coll., Bombay.

CHIPPER, ERNEST (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 22d class, 3rd Dec., 1875.

CHISHOLM, JAMES WRIGHT, late Central Prov. Commn.—Served in the Central Prov. from 24th Oct., 1856, as dep. collr., asst. commr., dep. commr., and settl. officer; inspr.-gen. of registrn., supt. of stamps, and commr. of excise, Oct., 1871; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, Oct., 1876; sec. to the chief commr., June, 1880; commr. of the Chhattisgarh divn., March, 1883; ret'd., 1889.

CHITTY, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., B.L. (chief judge, Small Cause Court, Bombay).—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 17th Nov., 1884; acting chief judge, Bombay small cause court, Feb.-May, 1891, and again from Oct., 1891; confd., July, 1892.

CHOLMELEY, NORMAN GOODFORD, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1884, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Oct., 1890.

CHOTZNER, ALFRED JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow and St. John's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CHRISTIE, CHARLES HOWARD PEREGRINE, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., April, 1866; joined the dept., March, 1869; served as asst. engr. and asst. consulting engr. for railways; dep. controller, public works accts., Madras, irrign. branch, Jan., 1873; examr., Jan., 1880; transfld. to Central India, Sept., 1880; examr. of guaranteed railway accts., Calcutta, April, 1881; examr., public works accts., Madras, March, 1887; N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1899.

CHRISTIE, GEORGE FRANCIS STEPHEN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 14th Dec., 1891, and served in Burma as asst. commr., cantonmt. mag., and small cause court judge.

CHRYSTIE, GEORGE, Col., late Police Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 12th Dec., 1857; arrived, 12th Feb., 1858, and served in the suppression of the Mutiny, &c.; from Aug., 1863, served in Madras as asst. supt. of police; supt., April, 1873; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1891; vacated, Dec., 1895.

CHUCKERBUTTY, ALFRED WILLIAM GOOD-EVE, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 30th Dec., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Jan., 1893.

CHUNDER MADHUB GHOSE (judge, High Court, Calcutta).—Member of the legisl. council of Bengal, 1883-85; fellow, Calcutta univ., 1885; prest., faculty of law, 1886-88; prest., board of examrs. for pleaders, &c., 1892; puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, 12th Jan., 1885.

CHUNDER NARAYAN SINGH, M.A. (collr. of stamp rev., Calcutta).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1866, and served as dep. mag. and dep. collr.; collr. of stamp rev., Calcutta, Feb., 1901.

CHURCH, WILLIAM THOMAS, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rugby and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 30th Oct., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; supt., Dehra Dun, Jan., 1882; mag. and collr., July, 1885; offd. as commr., Rohilkhand, in 1889; ret'd., April, 1890.

CLARK, CHARLES COSLEY STEWART, Indian Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1882, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Nov., 1890.

CLARK, JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Old Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1890; mag. and collr., Nov., 1897; dep. commr., Aug., 1900.

CLARK, WILLIAM OVENS, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (chief judge, Chief Court, Punjab).—Educ. at Portora Sch., Enniskillen, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 11th Nov., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on famine duty, Bengal, Feb., 1874; on special duty in the public works dept., March, 1882, and in the secretariat, March, 1885; dep. commr., Jan., 1888; district judge, Sept., 1892; divl. judge, Nov., 1892; judge, chief court, Oct., 1898; chief judge, Dec., 1898.

CLARKE, SIR ANDREW, G.C.M.G., C.B., C.I.E., Lieut.-Gen.—First commn., royal engineers, June, 1844; in 1848 was employed on special service in New Zealand, and was A.D.C. to lieut.-govr., Tasmania, 1849-53; govr. and commander-in-chief, Straits Settlements, 1873-75; member of council of govr.-gen. of India in charge of public works, June, 1875, to 1880; commandant, sch. of mil. engrg., Chatham, 1881-82; inspr.-gen. of fortifications and dir. of works, ordnance dept., war office, 1882-86; ret'd. July, 1886.

CLARKE, CHARLES AGACY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's School, London, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 17th Dec., 1896, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

CLARKE, C.B., M.A., late Educl. Service, Assam.—Apptd. to the Bengal edul. dept., 8th Dec., 1865; served as profr. at the presidency coll., and as inspr. of schools; offg. supt. of the Calcutta botanical garden, May, 1869; offg. dir. of public instrn., Dec., 1884; transf. to Assam as inspr. of schools, March, 1885; ret'd., June, 1887.

CLARKE, SIR CHARLES MANSFIELD, K.C.B., Lieut.-Gen. (quarter-master-gen., War Office).—First commn., 1st March, 1856; served in the New Zealand wars, 1861-66 (medal), and S. African war, 1879 (medal and C.B.), and the subsequent operations in Basutoland; commandant-gen. of the colonial forces, Cape of Good Hope, 1880-82; asst. adjutant-gen., southern district, 1882, Aldershot, 1882-84, headquarters, 1884-86; dep. adjutant-gen., Ireland, 1886-88; in command of 3rd infantry brigade at Aldershot, 1889-92; dep. adjutant-gen., headquarters, 1892; commander-in-chief, Madras, and second member

of council, Oct., 1893, till the abolition of that post, April, 1895; lieut.-gen. commanding the forces in Madras, April, 1895, to Nov., 1898; quarter-master-gen., war office, Sept., 1899.

CLARKE, EDWARD HENRY SCAMANDER, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Held various non-gazetted appts. from 20th Sept., 1875; was apptd. extra asst. commr. in the Punjab, Feb., 1880; served on the staff of Sir Lepel Griffin at Kabul, till the withdrawal of the forces in eastern Afghanistan (medal); accompanied Afghan boundary commn. to Herat frontier, Aug., 1884, to Nov., 1886; and on duty in connection therewith in London and Petersburg, Dec., 1886, to Sept., 1887; asst. pol. sec. with Kabul mission, 1893; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Oct., 1894, to Sept., 1899; offg. under sec., Nov., 1898.

CLARKE, GEOFFREY ROTHE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 5th Dec., 1895, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as asst. mag. and collr.

CLARKE, HENRY, Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (examr. mil. works).—First commn., Jan., 1869; apptd. to public works dept., Sept., 1873, and posted to Poona dist.; reverted to Imperial estabt., July, 1883; rejoined Aug., 1885, and served in the accts. estabt. as dep. examiner and examiner.

CLARKE, HERVEY MORRIS STANLEY, Col., I.S.C.—Arrived in India as ensign. Madras native infantry, 13th March, 1854; served with the Central India horse, 1858-60; from Sept., 1861, served in the N.W. Prov. police dept.; district supt., April, 1863; personal asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, Dec., 1871; dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1885; inspr.-gen. of prisons, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1886; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

CLARKE, LEYCESTER ANNAND GREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

CLARKE, LOFTUS OTWAY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 14th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., April, 1899.

CLARKE, ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. and supt., Punjab).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Raphoe, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 8th Dec., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. registrar, chief court, Punjab, May, 1881; offg. divl. judge, Oct., 1886; dep. commr., Nov., 1886; commr. and supt., March, 1896.

CLARKE, ROBERT LUCAS HYRAPIEF, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 25th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., July, 1898.

CLARKE, ROBERT THOMAS, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Raphoe Royal School and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 14th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; employed under the Kashmir Durbar as settl. offr. in Baltistan, July, 1898.

CLARKE, SYDENHAM CUBBON, Col., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 9th Jan., 1862; apptd. asst. engr., Mysore, Oct., 1862; exec. engr., Sept., 1867; served at Chatham, March to Sept., 1874; acting prof. of surveying, Cooper's Hill Coll., Sept., 1874; returned to Madras, Nov., 1876; served under the govt. of India on the Peshawar-Jalalabad state railway, Nov., 1879, and in Baluchistan; temporary supt. of works, Madras, April, 1883; offd. for various periods as suptg. engr., and in May, 1889, was in charge of public works, stores, and workshops; ret'd., March, 1891.

CLARKE, TREDWAR GEORGE, Col., I.S.C.—Ensign, Madras infantry, 1853; served in the Mysore commn. from Oct., 1857; dep. supt., Nov., 1862; dep. commr., 1873; sec. to chief commr., 1877; commr., Nagar divn., May, 1879; commr. of Coorg, 1881; offg. rest. in Mysore and chief commr. of Coorg, Dec., 1884, to March, 1885; and afterwards commr.; reverted to mil. duty, Jan., 1891.

CLARKE, WILLOUGHBY CHARLES STANLEY, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Assam Commn.—First commn. as ensign, June, 1851; arrived, 15th Oct., 1851; was employed on mil. duty till 1862, including service in the Sonthal expedn., 1855, and in the China expedn., 1860-61; also served as supt. of police and mag., govr.-gen.'s camp, 1859-60; from June, 1862, served in the Assam commn.; dep. commr., July, 1867; returned to Europe, 1889.

CLARKSON, JOHN WILKINS, Lieut.-Colonel, I.M.S. (sanitary commr., Bombay).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1875; arrived, 17th May, 1876; dep. sanitary commr., May, 1881; sanitary commr., April, 1896.

CLAY, ARTHURLLOYD, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Cholmeley Gr. Sch., Highgate, at Cologne, and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 29th Nov., 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; from Sept., 1874, to June, 1877, served in Assam as dep. commr., and district and sessions judge of Sylhet; mag. and collr., July, 1879; dep. commr., Manbhum, Aug., 1879; mag. and collr., Bakarganj, March, 1886; offd. as commr. of various divns.; ret'd., July, 1890.

CLAY, WILLIAM MCKINLEY, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 23th Oct., 1862; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine relief duty, 1873-74; mag. and collr., 1st grade, March, 1887; ret'd., 1888.

CLAYTON, ALFRED HARE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CLAYTON, HAROLD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Marlborough and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 10th Dec., 1897, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. offr.

CLEGG, ROBERT BAILEY, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 12th Jan., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and

head asst.; sub-collr. and joint mag. and principal asst. collr., July, 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1899; collr. and mag., Oct., 1900.

CLEGHORN, JAMES, M.D., Surgn.-Gen., late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., March, 1865; arrived, 26th Sept., 1865; held various mil. med. charges, and offd. as civil surgn. at several stations; from Feb., 1870, served in the N.W. Prov., as civil surgn., at various stations; offg. surgn. supt., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1885, to March, 1886, when he was apptd. civil surgn. of Lucknow; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Punjab, Sept., 1891; in 1893 was employed on special duty in connection with the cantonmts. act; dir. gen., Indian Med. Serv., and sanitary commr. with govt. of India, March, 1895; ret'd., Oct., 1898.

CLEMENTS, ERNEST, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Aug., 1900.

CLEMENTSON, EDWARD HENRY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Apptd. from Royal Indian Engrg. Coll., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Mysore as asst. engr.; transf. to State Railways, July, 1880; exec. engr., Feb., 1890.

CLENCH, ROBERT CHERRETT, Registry and Record Dept., India Office (supplemental clerk).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

CLERK, HUGH EDWARD, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

CLEVELAND, CHARLES RAITT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Finchley, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1887, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; asst. settlt. officer, Jan., 1890; asst. to commr. of settlts. and agric., Nov., 1890; se'tlt. officer, Oct., 1891; commr. of excise, &c., Nov., 1894; dep. commr., April, 1897.

CLIBBORN, JOHN, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C. (principal, Thomason College).—First commn., July, 1869; asst. engr., Oudh irrigation, March, 1872, exec. engr., Nov., 1881; principal Thomason Civil Engrg. Coll., Roorkee, Aug., 1892; is the author of several works on Indian irrigation.

CLIFFORD, SAMUEL LE POER TRENCH, Punjab Commn. (divl. judge).—From 1st April, 1861, served in the subord. jud. dept.; in Jan., 1872, was apptd. registrar of the jud. commr.'s court in Oudh; dep. registrar of chief court, Punjab, Oct., 1872; offd. for various periods as judge, small cause court; jud. asst., Jan., 1884; dist. judge, Mar., 1895; divl. judge, Aug., 1898; divl. judge, Aug., 1898; author of 'Notes on the Code of Criminal Procedure.'

CLIFFORD, WILLIAM WIGRAM, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the dept., 19th Sept., 1868, as acting asst. district supt.; confd., Feb., 1871; district supt., March, 1875; ret'd., Jan., 1900.

CLIFT, HENRY WILKINS, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. 8th Oct., 1868, and employed in buildings and roads branch in Mysore; exec. engr., Sept., 1871; served also in irrig. branch, and on famine relief works; transf. to state railways, July, 1879, and employed on Mysore and S. Mahratta lines, and

on Bengal-Assam railway survey; E. Coast railway, Nov., 1889; ret'd., Dec., 1895.

CLIFTON, CHARLES NAPIER, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1886.

CLINTON (BARON), RIGHT HON. CHARLES HENRY ROLLE HEPBURN STUART TREFUSIS.—Educ. at Eton, and at Christ Church, Oxford; M.P. for N. Devon from 1857 to 1866; partly under sec. of state for India, July, 1867, to Dec., 1868; apptd. a charity commr. for England and Wales, 1874; lord-lieut. of Devon since 1887.

CLOETE, HENRY NEWMAN CUNNINGHAM, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Apptd. asst. engr., and posted to Bombay, 10th Aug., 1868; exec. engr., March, 1873; transf'd. to state railways, Dec., 1873, and employed on W. Rajputana railway survey, and on the Punjab N. and Kandahar state railways; transf'd. to Bengal, May, 1881; suptg. \frac{1}{2} engr., May 1893.

CLOETE, JOSIAS GORDON, Lieut.-Gen., late Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Feb., 1857; arrived, May, 1857; served in the Madras police as asst. supt., June, 1861, to June, 1863, and from the latter date in the rev. survey; dep. supt., Jan., 1872; ret'd. from the dept., 1888.

CLOUGH, JOHN WILLIAM, Acctnt. Gen.'s Dept., India Office (2nd class clerk).—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th July, 1892.

COATES, WILLIAM, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, Punjab (prof'r., Lahore Med. Coll.).—Joined the service March, 1877; in mil. employ. till Oct., 1881, since when he has held various civil surgeoncies; prof'r., Lahore med. coll., April, 1893.

COBB, HENRY VENN, C.I.E., LL.B., Indian C.S. (resdt., Jaipur).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Nov., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. collr., Feb., 1892; acting asst. rest., Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, Aug.—Nov., 1893, and from April, 1895; in 1894 acted as dep. dir., land records, and as dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev., pol. agent, Ajmere, Nov., 1896; asst. resdt., Kashmir, Feb., 1899; resdt. Jaipur, Dec., 1900; C.I.E., Nov., 1901.

COBB, ROBERT, M.D., Lt.-Col., Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Backergunge).—First commn., 31st Mar., 1876; civil surgn. at various stations from April, 1877; civil surgn., Backergunge, Nov., 1900.

COBB, WILLIAM HEY, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., York, Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 25th Dec., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; city mag., Lucknow, July, 1889; also wasika officer, Oct., 1891; joint mag., Aug., 1893; dep. commr., Jan., 1896; mag. and collr., Nov., 1896.

COBDEN-RAMSAY, LOUIS EVELIGH BAWTREE, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt. of Bengal in gen., rev., and stat. depts.).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst.

mag. and collr.; asst. settlt. offr. and supt. of survey, Mar., 1900; under sec. to govt. of Bengal in gen., rev., and stat. depts., April, 1901.

COBDEN-RAMSAY, VERE BAWTREE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 9th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

COCKERELL, HORACE ABEL, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 31st Oct., 1853; served as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr., also as supt. of survey, Sept., 1859, offg. chairman, justices of the peace, Calcutta, and commr. of police, March, 1869, and Dec., 1872; commr. of divn., June, 1872; sec. to the govt. of Bengal, jud. and pol. depts., April, 1878; member of Bengal legisl. council, 1878, 1880, and 1882; board of rev., March, 1883; offg. lieut.-govr. of Bengal, Aug., 1885; ret'd., Aug., 1887.

CODRINGTON, GEORGE HENRY FORBES, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 4th May, 1857; served on mil. duty till Dec., 1865, and subsequently in the Bombay police dept. as asst. supt. and district supt.; 1st grade supt., April, 1883; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

COGHLAN, ROBERT NESBIT, late Customs Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 23rd April, 1871; from March, 1872, served as gen. manager of the customs bonded warehouses, Bombay; from March, 1874, served as asst. collr. of salt rev.; collr. of sea customs, Karachi, and asst. commr. of salt rev. in Sind, Sept., 1882; collr. of customs and salt rev. in Sind, March, 1891; retired, Feb., 1900.

COHEN, SAMUEL, India Audit Office (2nd class clerk).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 12th March, 1900.

COLDSTREAM, WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., July, 1876; dep. commr., March, 1877; acted as commr. in various divns. from 1880; ret'd., June, 1894; author of a work on the grasses of the Southern Punjab.

COLE, ALFRED WILLIAM RICHARDSON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 21st Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

COLE, CHARLES JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (under sec. to govt., railway branch).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., March, 1887; under sec. to govt. railway branch, April, 1899.

COLE, HENRY WALTER GEORGE, Capt. Indian S.C. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and R. M. Coll., Sandhurst; first commn., 29th Aug., 1885; joined the Assam commn., July, 1891, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1901.

COLE, WILLIAM HAMMOND, M.A., late Survey of India Dept.—Served in the educn. dept., N.W. Prov., April, 1862, to March, 1864, as prof'r. of mathematics at the govt. coll., Agrā; from Jan., 1867, served in the great trigon. survey; asst. supt. of surveys, Sept., 1869; dep.

supt., July, 1882; apptd. to charge of headquarters office, Dehra Dun, and survey detachment in Kashmir, Jan., 1884; retd., April, 1892; author of various contributions to the survey publs., and of transls. relating to geodesy.

COLE, WILLIAM HENRY, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1876, and posted as asst. engr. to state railways; asst. supt. of way and works, N.W. Railway, Jan., 1888; exec. engr., Feb., 1888; retd. March, 1898.

COLEBROOK, HENRY WILLIAM VAUGHAN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; transf. to Burma, April, 1894, and placed in charge of central irrig. divn.

COLERIDGE, FRANCIS ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 5th Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

COLES, FRANK ELLIS, Cent. Provs. Police (dist. supt.).—Apptd. to the dept. as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police, April, 1874; confd., April, 1878; dist. supt., July, 1880.

COLES, GEORGE EDWARD, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrig. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883; suptg. engr., Oct., 1898.

COLLEN, SIR EDWIN HENRY HAYTER, G.C.I.E., C.B., Major-Gen., I.S.C.—First comm., royal artillery, July, 1863; served with the Abyssinian expedn., 1868 (medal); commissary of ordnance, India, 1869-70; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, May, 1873; acted as asst. mil. sec. to govt. of India, 1870 and 1873-74; sec. to Indian ordnance comm., 1874-75; 3rd asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., March, 1875; 2nd asst. sec., April, 1876; 1st asst. sec., Dec., 1876; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., imperial assemblage, Delhi, 1876-77; on special duty in intelligence branch, London, 1877-79; sec. to Indian army organisation comm., 1879-80; served during the Afghan war as asst. controller-gen. of supply and transport, 1880-81 (medal); dep. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., April, 1884; served in Egypt with Suakim field force and as mil. sec. to Sir G. Graham, 1885 (medal with clasp, and Khedive's star); acclnt.-gen., mil. dept., govt. of India, July, 1886; sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., Nov., 1887; C.I.E., Jan., 1889; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1893; mil. member of govr.-gen.'s council, April, 1896, to April, 1901; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1901.

COLLETT, CHARLES MORDEN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as asst. commr.

COLLIER, FRANK ROBERT STANLEY, Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at King's Coll. and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 6th Nov., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, March, 1874; offg. collr. of customs, Calcutta, May, 1884; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1886; mag. and collr., April, 1889; member of Bengal legisl. council, July, 1893; commr., April, 1898; publ.

an annotated edition of the Bengal municipal act, 1884, another of the Bengal local self-govt. act and rules, 1886, and the 'Bengal Municipal Manual,' 1887.

COLLIER, GEORGE HERMAN (senior clerk, store dept., India office).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch.; junr. clerk, India office, 17th Feb., 1876, senr. clerk, Aug., 1896.

COLLIN, EDWARD WOODFIELD, B.A., Indian C.S. (sec., board of rev., Bengal).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; private sec. to finl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Jan., 1880; served under the foreign dept., Jan. to July, 1881, and in the finl. dept., March to Sept., 1885; settltd. officer, Muzaffarpur, Jan., 1886; offd. as dir. of land records, Bengal, April, 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1889; mag. and collr., Jan., 1894, mag. of 24-pergunnabs, Dec., 1895; sec., rev. board, June, 1900.

COLLINS, SIR ARTHUR JOHN HAMMOND, Kt., Q.C., B.L., late Madras High Court.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge, of which he was fellow; called to the bar (Gray's Inn), 1860; Q.C. and bencher of his inn, 1877; recorder of Poole, 1873-79, and of Exeter from 1879, until he was apptd. chief justice of the high court, Madras, 28th Nov., 1885; vice-chancellor, Madras univ., 1889; retd., Aug., 1899.

COLLINS, ARTHUR HERBERT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Bengal).—Apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 1st Jan., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug. 1880; dept. commr., Dec., 1896.

COLLINS, GEORGE GOWER, Burma Commn. (dep. commr.).—Held subord. appts. from 25th July, 1879; apptd. to the Burma police dept. as asst. district supt., Oct., 1882; joined the commn. as supernumerary asst. commr., March, 1884; asst. supt., Rangoon jail, Oct., 1884; asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., June, 1898.

COLLINS, SIDNEY ARTHUR (2nd class clerk, Store Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, India store dept., 4th Dec., 1885.

COLOMB, RICHARD PASLEY, Major, Indian S.C. (dep. commr., Haidarabad admn.).—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1879; in mil. employ till July, 1890; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Aug., 1890, to Nov., 1891; asst. pol. agent, Mahikantha, Jan. to July, 1892; asst. commr., Haidarabad admn., Sept., 1892; dep. commr., Aug., 1897.

COLQUHOUN, ARCHIBALD ROSS, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Entered the dept. in Oct., 1871, and served as asst. and exec. engr. in Burma; on special duty as personal asst. to Major Street on his mission to Siam, Jan.-May, 1879; posted to special duty in Upper Burma as civil officer and apptd. a dep. commr., Feb., 1886; retransfd. to public works dept., and posted to Baluchistan, Aug., 1889; services lent to British S. Africa Co., 1891-92; retd., Nov., 1893; author of 'Across Chryse,' 'Among the Shans,' 'The Truth about Tonquin,' 'The Opening of China,' 'Burma and the Burmans.'

COLQUHOUN, HENRY ARCHIBALD COLEBROOKE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Merton Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 18th Dec., 1895, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

COLSTON, EDWARD JOHN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Chesterfield Gr. Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

COLVIN, SIR AUCKLAND, K.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Eton; apptd. from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 17th Jan., 1858, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. officer; offg. under sec. to the govt. of India in the home dept., May, 1864, and in the foreign dept., Jan. to Nov., 1865; settl. officer, July, 1869; sec., board of rev., N.W. Prov., March, 1870; on special duty for revision of Allahabad settl., June, 1870; offg. sec. to govt., N.W.
