DE BROE, VICTOR EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand Rly.).—Apptd. asst. engr., 22nd Jan., 1874, and posted to State Railways; exec. engr., June, 1892; dep. manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand Rly., July, 1900.

DE FABECK, WILLIAM FREDERICK, M.D., Surgn.-Major-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Entered the army med. dept., June, 1855 joined the Madras med. dept., June, 1857, and served under the mil. dept. to May, 1871, also holding various civil surgeoncies; from Nov., 1873; served in British Burma as civil surgn. and supt. of lock hospital, Maulmain; also health officer, Maulmain, Feb., 1878; dep. surgn.-gen., Nagpur force, and Central India regts. March, 1886; surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Madras, May, 1890; ret'd. May, 1894; served in the Crimean campaign, 1855-56 (medal and clasp) and in the Mutiny, 1859 (medal).

DEIGHTON, KENNETH, B.A., late Educ. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as prof'r., Agra coll., 19th Feb., 1862; principal, Agra coll., March, 1864; inspr. of

schs., Rohilkhand divn., April, 1885; ret'd., 1890.

DE LA COURNEUVE, STEWARD HOWARD THOMAS (dep. commr., Burma).—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1873; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1880; asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., March, 1891.

DE LAESSOE, ALBERT FREDERICK, C.M.G., C.I.E., Capt., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (in charge of the ex-Amir).—Served in the Danish army from April, 1867; attached to foreign legion of French army, 1870-71; chevalier of legion of honour, 1871; from 1873 to 1880 served as instructor in Persian army and chief of staff coll. in Persia; apptd. translr. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Feb., 1881; attached to Afghan boundary commn., Sept., 1884; on special duty with Sir West Ridge-way in St. Petersburg and London; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, Nov., 1887; asst. commr., Merwara, July, 1889; Ajmir, May, 1892; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1893; pol. officer in charge of the ex-Amir of Kabul, April, 1899.

DELAWAR HOSAIN AHMED, Khan Bahadur, B.A., late Bengal Registrn. Dept.—Educ. at Calcutta Univ.; joined the service, 27th Feb., 1861, as dep mag., and dep. collr.; apptd. inspr.-gen. of registrn., Bengal, April, 1895; ret'd., 1900.

DELEVINGNE, WALTER NORMAN, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the City of London Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1899.

DE MARSAC, FREDERICK ROOME, late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 8th March, 1864; asst. supt., Jan., 1866; supt., April, 1880; in charge, store dept., May, 1891; ret'd., March, 1892.

DE MELLO, CHARLES HARVEY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason College; apptd. to dept. as apprentice engr., 21st Nov., 1876; asst.-engr., April, 1879; exec. engr., May, 1894; has received the thanks of the Bengal govt.

DE MONTMORENCY, GEOFFREY FITZHERVEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 6th Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

DE MORGAN, WALTER CAMPBELL, M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1885; suptg. engr., Feb., 1901.

DEMPSTER, FRANCIS ERSKINE, C.I.E., M.I.E.E. (supt., India Telegraph Dept.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., Oct., 1878, as asst. supt.; supt., Feb., 1893; was in charge of field telegraphs with Chitral relief force, March, 1895; has received the war medal with 'Chin-Lushai 1889-90' clasp.

DEMPSTER, JAMES INNES ROTHNEY, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 2nd Nov., 1870, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; services lent to Egyptian govt., 1885-89; suptg. engr., April, 1893; ret'd., April, 1899.

DENMAN, JOHN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after

exam. of 1878; arrived, 4th Dec., 1880, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1891; small cause court judge, Aug., 1893; district and sessions judge, March, 1894.

DENNYS, CHARLES JOHN, Major, I.S.C., Jud. Dept., Punjab (cantonmt. mag.).—First commr., 26th Mar., 1874; services placed at disposal of Punjab govt., July, 1881; offg. asst. cantonmt. mag., Aug., 1881; confd., Oct., 1882; cantonmt. mag., March, 1886.

DENNYS, EDWARD AUGUSTUS, Indian Public Works Dept. (examr. of accounts, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1875, as accont.; asst. examr., April, 1876; dep. examr., Dec., 1881; examr., Dec., 1886.

DENNYS, GEORGE WILLIAM PATRICK, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Peshawar).—Joined the service, 31st Oct., 1879; in mil. employ till April, 1882, since when he has held several civil surgenies.

DENTITH, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DE PALEZIEUX-FALCONNET, GRENVILLE PULTENEY, Major-Gen., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras engrs., 1852; joined the Madras public works dept., Aug., 1854, as asst. engr.; from Dec., 1854, served in the Bengal public works dept. as exec. engr., Pegu; Mhow divn., Rajputana, Nov., 1864; offg. suptg. engr., mil. works, Allahabad, 1872; suptg. engr., Meerut, April, 1874, and afterwards at Moradabad and Rawalpindi; offd. as inspr.-gen. of mil. works, 1883-84; ret'd., 1886.

DE RENZY, ANNESLEY CHARLES CASTRIOT, C.B., Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; entered the Bengal med. service, 1851; served through the Burmese war, 1852-53 (medal and clasp), and the Mutiny, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); apptd. first sanitary commr., Punjab, 1868; sanitary commr., Assam, 1878; served in the expdn. against the Nagas, 1879 (clasp and C.B.); dep. surgn.-gen. Punjab frontier force, 1880; ret'd., Dec., 1882; author of sanitary reports to govt. on the Punjab and Assam, and of a paper on the sanitary state of British troops in N. India.

DE ROZARIO, VICTOR PHILIP, Madras Jud. Dept. (judge, Madras small causes court).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1858, and served in various subord. posts; district munsif, Nov., 1864; sub-judge, Oct., 1875; judge, Madras small causes court, Dec., 1892.

DE SOUZA, FRANCIS XAVIER, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 20th Jan., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1899; acting joint judge, Mar., 1900; 2nd asst., July, 1900.

DESVOEUX, HERBERT, Captain, I.S.C. (settl. offr., Burma commn.).—First commn., 28th Sept., 1887; asst. cmdt. Burma Police Battn., Jan., 1890; asst. commr., Dec., 1890; settl. officer, Aug., 1897.

DEUCHARS, GEORGE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst.

engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to state railways ;
exec. engr., Jan., 1894.

DEVONSHIRE (DUKE OF), HIS GRACE SPENCER COMPTON CAVENDISH, K.G., P.C., D.C.L., LL.D. (Lord President of the Council).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge ; attached to Earl Granville's special mission to Russia in 1856 ; M.P. for N. Lancashire from 1857 to 1868, when he was returned for the Radnor boroughs ; LL.D., Cambridge, 1862 ; apptd. a lord of the admiralty, March, 1862 ; under sec. for war, April, 1863 ; sec. of state for war, Feb.—July, 1866 ; postmr.-gen., 1868-71 ; chief sec. for Ireland, 1871-74 ; lord rector of the univ. of Edinburgh, 1879 ; was returned for N.E. Lancashire in April, 1880, and became sec. of state for India ; sec. of state for war, 1882-85 ; a royal commr. for the exhibn. of 1886 ; chairman of the royal commn. on the labour question, 1891 ; chancellor of Cambridge univ., 1892 ; K.G., 1892 ; lord prest. of the council in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895.

DEWAR, DOUGLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at foreign schools and Jesus Coll. Cambridge ; apptd. after exam. of 1897 ; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in the N.W. Provs. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.

DEWAR, FRANCIS, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1896 ; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

DEWHURST, ROBERT PAGET, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Clitheroe Gr. Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1892 ; arrived, 28th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr. ; joint mag., March, 1899 ; under sec. to govt., April to Nov., 1900.

DE WINTON, THOMAS WALTER, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll. ; appointed to the dept. as asst. engr., Nov., 1879 ; exec. engr., April, 1891.

DE WINTON, WALTER BERNARD, Madras Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr. ; exec. engr., May, 1883 ; in 1885-86 served temporarily on Madras state railway surveys, and in 1890-91 was employed under the inspr.-gen. of mil. works ; chief engr. and sec. to govt., April, 1898.

DEY, GEORGE GOODAIR, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at North London Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London ; apptd. after exam. of 1867 ; arrived, 25th Oct., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. ; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1880 ; also served in Assam, in 1880, as offg. judge of Sylhet and Cachar ; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1887 ; ret'd., April, 1895.

DHANJISHA NAVROJI PARAKH, L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bombay Med. Service.—Joined the service, Sept., 1874 ; arrived, 5th May, 1875 ; employed under the mil. dept. till 1881, including service in the Persian expdn., 1856-57 (medal and clasp) ; served subsequently as asst. civil surgn., Poona, and in various acting appts. ; surgn. to the Gokuldas Tejpal hosp., Jan., 1887 ; ret'd., Mar., 1898.

DHARMA RAO NAYUDU, REDNAM, Dewan Bahadur, late Madras Salt Dept.—Joined the service, 15th May, 1861, and was employed in the police dept. until April, 1869, when he was transf'd. to the rev. dept. as dep. collr. ; personal asst. to salt commr., July, 1878 ; asst. commr. of salt rev., Feb., 1880 ; dep. commr., Dec., 1892 ; ret'd., April, 1896.

DIACK, ALEXANDER HENDERSON, Indian C.S. (rev. and finl. sec. to govt., Punjab).—Educ. at Univ. of Aberdeen, and King's College, Cambridge ; apptd. after exam. of 1879 ; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1882, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and settl. officer ; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., Nov., 1891, to July 1892 ; dep. commr. and settl. officer, Dec., 1894 ; rev. and finl. sec. to govt., Punjab, Aug., 1899.

DICKENS, FREDERIC JOHN, India Office (second class clerk, store dept.).—Apptd. clerk, second class, store dept., 6th Dec., 1900.

DICKINSON, FRANCIS BRADLEY, Forest Dept., N.W. Prov. (conservator of forests).—Joined the dept., 22nd Dec., 1872, and served in Coorg, Madras, Central Prov. and Burma as asst. and dep. conservator ; conservator, Burma, April, 1894 ; conservator, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1899.

DIMMÖCK, HENRY PEERS, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Major, Indian Med. Service, Bombay (prof., Grant med. coll.).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880 ; civil surgn., Sept., 1887 ; supt., Mahabaleshwar, Sept., 1889 ; obstetric physician and prof. of midwifery, Grant med. coll., June, 1891 ; also presidency surgn., April, 1893 ; member of plague committee, March, 1897.

DINGWALL-FORDYCE, CHARLES GRAHAM, Forest Dept., Assam (conservator).—Joined the service, 21st Jan., 1880, as asst. consr. ; dep. consr., March, 1888 ; consr., June, 1901.

DIXON, FREDERIC PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1894 ; arrived, 19th Dec., 1895 ; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DIXON, FREDERICK THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at London Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge ; apptd. after exam. of 1893 ; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

DIXON, JAMES, Burma Police Dept. (dep.-inspr.-gen. of police).—Apptd. inspr. of police, Sept., 1870 ; asst. district supt., Oct., 1877, and district supt., Jan., 1883 ; dep.-inspr.-gen. of civil police, July, 1889.

DOBBIE, WILLIAM HERBERT, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (acnt.-gen., Madras).—Educ. at the Merchant Taylors' Sch. ; joined the dept., 23rd May, 1872 ; on famine duty, 1874-75 ; asst. acnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1875 ; from Jan., 1876, served in Bengal as acting dep.-acnt.-gen., and as asst. comptr.-gen. ; served in Madras, 1878-83, and in the Punjab, 1883-84 ; asst. acnt.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1884 ; Punjab, April, 1885 ; dep. acnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., March, 1886 ; comptr., Assam, April, 1887 ; dep. acnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1890 ; offg. comptr., Haidarabad, July, 1891 ; dep. comptr.-gen., April, 1896 ; acnt.-gen., Madras, Jan., 1901.

DOBBS, HENRY ROBERT CONWAY, Indian C.S. (asst. pol. agent, Zhoib).—Educ. at Winchester

and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 6th Dec., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, March, 1895; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., June, 1896; joint mag., April, 1897; asst. to rest., Mysore, Oct., 1899; asst. pol. agent, Zohb, March, 1901.

DOBIE, STANLEY LOCKER, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; supt. of lunatic asylum, Madras, Jan., 1882; served in Afghanistan and Egypt; med. officer, Utakamand, April, 1888, to Nov., 1889; supt. of lunatic asylum, Madras, Jan., 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1892; ret'd., July, 1897.

DODD, CHARLES, late Educ. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as offg. profr. Agra coll., 4th Aug., 1868; head master, Bareilly coll., Dec., 1868; profr. of English literature, Agra and Benares colls., Dec., 1874; inspr. of schools, Sept., 1884; ret'd., March, 1894.

DODD, CHARLES ALBERT, Col., I.S.C.—Arrived as ensign, 12th Nov., 1857, and served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); from Feb., 1859, served in the police dept.; personal asst. to inspr.-gen., N.W. Prov., March, 1861; district supt., Aug., 1861; from April, 1870, served as supt., govt. press, and curator of govt. books, N.W. Prov.; also supt. of emigration, Jan., 1883; reverted to mil. dept., May, 1893.

DODERET, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (junr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 7th Nov., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; forest settlt. officer, May, 1888; 2nd asst., April, 1890; special under sec. to govt. in the dept. of famine relief, Feb.—May, 1892; 1st asst., May, 1893; junr. collr., July, 1897; pol. agent, Bangs, March, 1901.

DOIG, SCROPE BERDMORE, M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 16th July, 1867, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1872; on special duty, Ahmadnagar water-supply scheme, in 1891, and again as member of Sind irrign. commn.; sanitary engr., and member of sanitary board, Jan., 1892; returned to public works dept., as suptg. engr., Oct., 1893; chief engr., Dec., 1896; sec. to govt., June, 1899; ret'd., Aug., 1899.

DOIG, WILLIAM AITKENHEAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Dublin High Sch. and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

DONALD, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub-divl. officer, Assam).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfid. to Assam as asst. commr., May, 1898; sub-divl. offr., April, 1899.

DONALD, JOHN STUART, C.I.E. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Joined the service, April, 1882; extra asst. commr., May, 1884; asst. commr., Nov., 1890; accompanied mission to Kabul under Sir H. M. Durand, 1893; on special duty on the N.W. Frontier, 1894; was British commr. for demarcation of Kurram-Afghan boundary, May-Dec., 1894; dep. commr., Aug., 1900.

DONALD, WILLIAM, late Finl. Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 26th Oct., 1856; from 23rd

May, 1859, served in the central public works office, and the civil paymaster's office, Madras, and was apptd. to finl. dept., Aug., 1862; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Aug., 1869; dir. of incorporated bank of Madras, March, 1871; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1874, and at Madras, June, 1875; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Dec., 1884; compiled the manual of rules and regulations for the use of junior members of Madras civil service; ret'd., 1889.

DONOVAN, CHARLES, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Kingstown Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 11th Jan., 1863; from Feb., 1864, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; served in Assam, Sept., 1879, to June, 1882, as dep. commr.; judge, Benares, Jan., 1885; ret'd., June, 1889.

DORAN, EDWARD ANTHONY, Postal Dept. (Postmaster-Gen., Bengal).—Joined the service, 2nd Feb., 1880, and served in the postal dept. as clerk, supdt., and asst. dir.-gen.; postmr.-gen. and dep. dir.-gen., April, 1901.

DOSABHAI FRAMJI, C.S.I., late Presidency Mag., Bombay.—Served from 6th Oct., 1860, as income tax assessor; police mag., July, 1866; special officer for the imperial licence tax, May, 1867; collr. of income tax, and certificate tax, May, 1869; sheriff of Bombay, Dec., 1871; from June, 1874, served as second mag. of police, Bombay, and also held the posts of chairman of the municipal corporation, chairman of the justices of the peace, member of legisl. council, joint commr. of income tax, &c.; C.S.I., Jan., 1877; author of 'Moral and Miscellaneous Essays,' 'The British Raj,' 'History of the Parsees,' and 'Travels in Great Britain'; ret'd., 1888.

DOUGLAS, EDWARD WILLIAM SPENCER, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 4th Nov., 1871, and posted to state railways; asst. engr., Sept., 1872; transfid. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, irrign. branch, Nov., 1873; exec. engr., May, 1883; served on state railways in 1885-86 and 1886-87.

DOUGLAS, MONTAGUE WILLIAM, Captain, Indian S.C. (dep. commr., Punjab).—In mil. employ from 21st March, 1887; asst. commr., April, 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1899.

DOUGLAS, SHOLTO JAMES, B.L., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 6th Jan., 1878, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and inspr. of labourers; from April, 1885, served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1889; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1894.

DOUIE, JAMES MCCRONE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the High Sch., Edinburgh, Univ. of Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 7th Dec., 1876, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., sec. to the finl. commr. and settlt. officer; senr. sec. to finl. commr., April, 1889; dep. commr., Nov., 1891; offg. rev. sec., April, 1893, to Oct., 1894, and from Nov., 1895, to April, 1896; offg. commr. and supt., April, 1898; offg. chief sec. to govt., Punjab, March, 1900; member of legisl. council, Punjab, July, 1900; has publ'd. a transl. of the Biluchinama, with a Biloch grammar.

DOVETON, JOHN CHADWICK, Col., I.S.C., late Forest Dept., Central Prov.—First commn., Oct., 1858; asst. conservator of forests, Central Prov., 8th April, 1864; dep. conservator, May, 1865; conservator, Sept., 1868; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1894.

DOWDEN, THOMAS FREEMAN, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Dec., 1856; joined the Bombay public works dept., Aug., 1859; under sec. to govt., 1865; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, 1872; manager, Dhond and Manmad state railway, Dec., 1878; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); offg. sec. to agent, Baluchistan, April, 1880; acting senr. dep. consulting engr., and under sec. to govt., Bombay, Nov., 1881; asst. to dir.-gen. of railways, 1884; suptg. engr., July, 1886; dep. consulting engr., Calcutta, April, 1886; consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lucknow, Feb., 1887; reverted to mil. duty, June, 1892; ret'd. from the army, May, 1893.

DOWN, CECIL PATTON, Captain, Indian S.C. (pol. officer, Tochi).—In mil. employ from 5th Nov., 1888; asst. commr., Dec., 1893; pol. officer, Tochi, Aug., 1900.

DOWN, JAMES ERSKINE, Bombay Police Dept. (inspr.-gen. of police).—Joined the service, 24th May, 1872; asst. supt., Aug., 1875; supt., April, 1876; acting inspr.-gen., Feb., 1898; confd. Jan., 1899.

DOYLE, BERNARD, B.A., M.B., M.Ch., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, Punjab (civil surgn., Karnal).—Apptd. to the Indian med. dept., and arrived, 21st Nov., 1876; served in mil. dept. to April, 1881, and afterwards in the Punjab as civil surgn. and dep. sanitary commr.; served in Burma, 1886-87; med. adviser to Maharaja of Jhind, Sept., 1887; civil surgn., Ambala, March, 1890; civil surgn. and supt. of lunatic asylum, Delhi, June, 1890; civil surgn., Firozpur, Oct., 1892; Peshawar, July, 1893; Karnal, Oct., 1900.

D'OYLY, WARREN HASTINGS, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 28th May, 1858, and served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts.; inspr.-gen. of jails, May, 1876; mag. and collr., 1st grade, July, 1876; offg. opium agent, Behar, 1878, 1885-87, and 1889-90; offg. commr., Bhagalpur, 1884-85, and 1889; ret'd., Nov., 1891.

DRAKE, FRANCIS COURTNEY, India Office (senior clerk).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; junr. clerk, record office, 23rd Feb., 1892; junr. clerk, India office, 22nd Aug., 1892; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, Feb., 1894; senr. clerk, July, 1898.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, EDWARD FORSTER, F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 1st Oct., 1866; arrived, 23rd April, 1867, and served under the mil. dept., Madras, and also in acting civil appts.; rest. asst. surgn., gen. hosp., and prof'r., med. coll., Madras, Oct., 1870; ophthalmic surgn., gen. hosp., and prof'r. of ophthalmic surgery and physiology, med. coll., March, 1875; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1885; ret'd., April, 1894; member of India Office med. board, Aug., 1897, to June, 1900.

DRAKE - BROCKMAN, ELLIOT GEORGE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal)

—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1895; mag. and collr., Oct., 1899; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1900.

DRAKE - BROCKMAN, HENRY VERNON, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Central Prov.).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 10th Dec., 1886, and served in the Central Prov. as acting under sec. and asst. commr.; commr. of excise, Aug., 1894; dep. commr., Oct., 1894; divl. judge, April, 1897.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, HERBERT EDWARD, F.R.C.S.E., Capt., Indian Med. Service (agency surgn., Bhartpore).—Entered the service, Sept., 1888; in mil. employ till Nov., 1891, when he was apptd. civil surgn. in the N.W. Prov.; surgn. to the Bhartpore State, Nov., 1895.

DRAKE - BROCKMAN, RALPH RHENIUS EVANS, A.I.C.E., Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 1st March, 1864, and joined sappers and miners; from Dec., 1864, served in the public works dept.; exec. engr., March, 1870; suptg. engr., Nov., 1888; acted in 1889 and 1892-93 as joint sec. to govt. and chief engr., gen. buildings and roads branch; chief engr. for irrign., and joint sec. to govt., irrign. branch, April, 1893; chief engr., 1st class, Jan., 1896; ret'd., Oct., 1896.

DRAKE - BROCKMAN, WILLIAM DRAKE, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Served in the Punjab as sub. asst. rev. surveyor, 1852-55; joined Thomason Coll., and thence was apptd. to the public works dept., April, 1858, and served in the N.W. Prov., in the buildings and roads branch; transf'd. temporarily to railway dept., 1858-60; employed on famine relief works in 1861, and 1877-78; exec. engr., March, 1874; suptg. engr., March, 1886; ret'd., March, 1892.

DREW, WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (dep. manager, state rlys.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to Bombay; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; dep. manager, state railways, March, 1898.

DREW, WILLIAM WILSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (senr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 14th Nov., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from May, 1882, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag., and was permanently transf'd., June, 1885; inspr. of factories, town of Bombay, May, 1887; 2nd asst. collr., Nov., 1888; provl. supt. of census operations, April, 1890; 1st asst. collr. and mag., May, 1891; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1896; senr. collr., July, 1897.

DRIBERG, JOHN JAMES STREET, late Assam Commn.—Educ. at St. Nicholas Coll., Lancing; joined the service as acting dep. mag. and dep. collr. in Bengal, Nov., 1861; confd., 16th April, 1862; from Dec., 1863, served in Assam as extra asst. commr.; asst. civil and pol. officer with the Bhutan Duars field force, 1864-65 (medal and clasp); dep. supt. of census in Assam, Oct., 1880; supt. of emigration, June, 1882; dep. commr., May, 1886; inspr.-gen. of jails, &c., Nov., 1891; in 1893 acted as commr., Assam valley districts; ret'd., April, 1896.

DRUMMOND, JAMES RAMSAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at

Edinburgh Acad., Univ. of Glasgow, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 27th Nov., 1874, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, March, 1890; dep. commr., Aug., 1890.

DRURY, FRANCIS JAMES, B.A., M.B., Major, I.M.S., Bengal Med. Dept. (prof. Calcutta med. coll.).—Joined the service April, 1885; in miliary employ till June, 1889; asst.-surgeon, Eden Hospital, Calcutta, April, 1891; resdt. physician and prof., Calcutta Med. Coll., Oct., 1893; ditto, and govt. bacteriologist, May, 1899.

DRURY, GEORGE MARSTON, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. traffic supt., 30th Jan., 1878, and served chiefly on the E. Bengal state railway; traffic supt., E. Coast railway, Nov., 1891; ret'd., Dec., 1892.

DRYSDALE, ALEXANDER TOVEY, late Forest Dept., Hyderabad Administration.—Arrived, Dec., 1864; from April, 1865, served in the Central Prov. as head master of govt. sch.; from June, 1868, served in the forest dept., Berar, as asst. conservator, dep. conservator, and conservator; ret'd., Jan., 1887.

DRYSDALE, THEODORE, late Central Prov. Commn.—Asst. master, govt. school, Saugor, April, 1861; served in the Central Prov. commn. from 19th Jan., 1863; apptd. extra asst. commr., April, 1864; supt. of the jud. commr.'s office, June, 1864; registrar, May, 1869; dep. supt. of census operations, Central Prov., April, 1880, and wrote the provl. report; judge, small cause court, Nagpur, Nov., 1884; dep. commr., April, 1885; cantonmt. mag., Kampti, 1885-89; divl. judge, June, 1895; ret'd., March, 1897.

DU BOULAY, JAMES HOUSSEMAINNE, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1837; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd assist., May, 1896; 1st asst., April, 1899.

DUCAT, WALTER MARDEN, Col., R.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service as second lieut., 13th June, 1856; arrived, May, 1858; asst. engr., Aug., 1859; exec. engr., Aug., 1863; suptg. engr., Nov., 1886; ret'd., April, 1890.

DUCKWORTH, EDWARD DYCE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Dec., 1899, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

DUFFERIN AND AVA (MARQUIS OF), RIGHT HON. FREDERICK TEMPLE HAMILTON BLACKWOOD, K.P., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., P.C., D.C.L., LL.D.—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; specially attached to Lord John Russell's mission to Vienna in 1855; in 1860 was apptd. British commr. in Syria to enquire into the massacre of Christians, and for his services was nominated a K.C.B.; under sec. of state for India, 1864-66; under sec. for war, 1866; chancellor of the duchy of Lancaster, 1868-72; govr.-gen. of Canada, 1872-78; ambassador at St. Petersburg, Feb., 1879; ambassador at Constantinople, May, 1881; from Nov., 1882, to April, 1883, was engaged in Egypt in the settlement of the various questions arising out of Arabi's rebellion; viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, 13th Dec., 1884; left India to take up post of ambassador at Rome, Dec., 1888; lord warden

of the cinque ports, 1891-95; ambassador at Paris, Dec., 1891 to Oct., 1896; K.P. in 1863; privy councillor, Dec., 1868; earl of the United Kingdom, 1871, and marquis, 1888, taking the style of Ava in addn. to Dufferin by special command of Her Majesty; prest. of the royal geographical society, May, 1878; LL.D., Harvard, 1878; LL.D., Dublin, 1879; D.C.L., Oxford, 1879; G.C.B., 1883; chancellor of the royal univ., Ireland, 1885; author of 'Letters from High Latitudes,' and other works.

DUKE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Arbroath High Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 11th Dec., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1892; mag. and collr., Dec., 1895.

DUKE, JOSHUA, Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resdy. surgn., Kashmir).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and was employed on mil. duty and as civil surgn. at Amritsar (1881-82) and in Kashmir (1884); served in the Afghan war, Marri expedn., and with the Gilgit mission; med. officer, Malwa Bhil corps and Bhopawar pol. agency, May, 1891; acted as residency surgn., Kashmir, 1889-91, 1895, and 1898; confd. Sept., 1900; author of 'Recollections of the Kabul campaign,' and 'Banting in India.'

DUMERGUE, JOHN WILLOUGHBY FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. rest. in Travancore; Malayalam translr. to govt., March, 1884; head asst., Nilgiris, Feb., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1889; district and sessions judge, Madura, Aug., 1893; collr. and mag., Chingleput, Nov., 1896; dist. and sess. judge, Tinnevelly, Jan., 1899; ditto, Canara, Oct., 1899.

DUNCAN, DAVID, M.A., D.Sc., LL.D. (Edinburgh), late Educn. Dept., Madras.—Served in the dept. from 20th May, 1870, as prof. of logic and moral philosophy at the presidency coll., and was registrar of the Madras univ., 1875-79 and 1881-86; fellow of the Madras univ., March, 1877; principal and prof., presidency coll., from March, 1884; dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1892; member of legisl. council, Oct., 1894; ret'd., Nov., 1899.

DUNDAS, CHARLES LAWRENCE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Eton, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 30th Dec., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

DUNLOP, ALEXANDER JOHNSTON, C.I.E. (dep. commr., Hyderabad).—Apptd. asst. to cotton commr., Central Prov. and Berar, 1st May, 1869, and asst. commr., Feb., 1876; from 1869 to 1874 held charge in addn. to other duties of a govt. experimental farm; inspr.-gen., rev. dept., Nizam's govt., April, 1885, and survey and settlt. commr., Nizam's govt., April, 1887; rank of dep. commr., Hyderabad, July, 1889; C.I.E., Nov., 1901.

DUNLOP, JOHN GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 16th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DUNLOP-SMITH, JAMES ROBERT, C.I.E., M.A., Maj. I.S.C. (dir. of land records and agriculture, Punjab).—Arrived, 24th Oct., 1879; from March, 1883, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; private sec. to lieut.-govr., Dec., 1883; asst. commr. and settl. collr., April, 1888; dep. commr., April, 1895; dir. of land records and agriculture, Oct., 1897; C.I.E., Jan., 1901.

DUNN, CHARLES WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

DUNN, GEORGE OWEN WILLIAM, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1886; under sec. to govt., public works dept., May, 1894.

DUPERNEX, HUBERT EMILE LEONCE PAUL, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 31st Oct., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., May, 1897; joint mag., July, 1897; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1901; author of 'The Enhanced Excise Duties' and 'Tenancies in Oudh.'

DURAND, ALGERNON GEORGE ARNOLD, C.B., C.I.E., Col., I.S.C.—First commn., 21st Dec., 1872; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal with clasp, and bronze star); A.D.C. to the viceroy, 1881-82; on special duty on N.W. frontier, 1883; British agent, Gilgit, July, 1889; in 1891 commanded Hunza-Nagar field force (medal with clasp; C.B.); mil. sec. to viceroy, Jan., 1894; C.I.E., June, 1897; retired, Jan., 1901.

DURAND, SIR EDWARD LAW, Bart., C.B., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 10th Oct., 1865; arrived, June, 1866; served with the 69th regt., and was transf. to Bengal staff corps, Oct., 1868; A.D.C. and private sec. to lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, June, 1870; from Feb., 1871, served in the foreign dept. in Rajputana and Central India; acting pol. agent, Manipur, Feb.-Oct., 1877; acting asst. rest., Bushire, April, 1878; in charge of ex-Amir of Kabul, March, 1881; asst. commr., Afghan boundary commn., Oct., 1885; rest., Nepal, May, 1889; ret'd., Oct., 1893; author of 'Report on Fars and Sketch Map of part of the Country,' and 'Report on Pearl Fisheries.'

DURAND, SIR HENRY MORTIMER, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.L., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Blackheath Proprietary Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 3rd March, 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, 1873; from June, 1874, served in the foreign dept. of the govt. of India, and was apptd. offg. 1st asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, July, 1876; offg. asst. sec., foreign dept., Nov., 1877; pol. sec. to major-gen. Sir F. Roberts with the Kabul field force, Sept., 1879 (medal and two clasps); under sec., foreign dept., March, 1880; C.S.I., Feb., 1881; offg. private sec. to the viceroy, June, 1880; sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Feb., 1885; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1889; sent on a special mission to Afghanistan, Aug., 1893; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1894; envoy and minister plenipotentiary in Persia, May, 1894; ret'd. from the Indian civil service, Aug., 1897; apptd. ambassador

extraordinary and plenipotentiary to the Court of Spain, 1900; G.C.M.G., May, 1900; has publ. a work on the first Afghan war, 1871, and a life of Sir H. M. Durand, 1883.

DURRANT, WILLIAM SCOTT, M.A. (senior clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Educ. at Whitgift, and Lycée St. Omer; B.A., London (with honours in French), 1888; M.A. (Classics), 1891; junr. clerk, India office, 18th Sept., 1879; senr. clerk, Oct., 1897.

DUTHIE, JOHN FIRMINGER, B.A., F.L.S., Indian Botanical Survey (director, botanical dept., Saharanpur).—Apptd. supt., botanical gardens, Saharanpur, Dec., 1876; director, botanical dept., Northern India, April, 1887; author of 'Fodder Grasses of Northern India.'

DUTHOIT, WILLIAM, D.C.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford; called to the bar at the Inner Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 17th Dec., 1857; served from Sept., 1858, in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and dep. supt., family domains, Maharaja of Benares; offg. insp.-gen. of registrn. and commr. of excise and stamps, March, 1875; mag. and collr., Shah-jahanpur, April, 1877; offg. puisne judge, high court, June, 1881; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1881; offg. jud. commr., Oudh, May, 1882; employed on the opium commn., under govt. of Bengal, 1883; jud. commr. of Oudh, March, 1884; ret'd., Aug., 1886.

DUTT, CHARU CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 7th Dec., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

DUTT, PRATAP CHANDRA, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 25th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

DUTT, ROMESH CHUNDER, C.I.E., B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Colootola Branch Sch., Calcutta, Presidency Coll., Calcutta Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 28th Sept., 1871; served in rev. and jud. depts., Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., March, 1888; acted as commr., Bardwan, 1894-95; member of provl. legisl. council, Jan.-Oct., 1895; has publ. 'Three Years in Europe,' 1872; 'Peasantry of Bengal,' 1876; 'Literature of Bengal,' 1877; a History of Civilisation in Ancient India,' 1888-89; a 'Brief History of Ancient and Modern India,' 1891; 'Lays of Ancient India,' 1894; &c., &c.; ret'd., Oct., 1897.

DUTT, RUSSICK LALL, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; served in the civil dept. from 30th Nov., 1872, in acting appts., and as civil surgn. at various stations; ret'd., Oct., 1899.

DUVAL, HERBERT PHILIP, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Caius College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 9th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DWARKA NATH MITTER, B.L., Bengal Jud. Dept. (subordinate judge).—Joined the service as Munsif, March, 1869; subordinate judge, Nov., 1882.

DYMOTT, DONALD FREDERICK, M.B., Surgn.-Major, late Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the ser-

vice, 31st March, 1880; asst. physician, gen. hosp., July, 1884; rest. physician, March, 1885; inspr. of vaccn., and dep. sanitary commr., June, 1888; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1893; acting med. and sanitary officer, Salem, Oct., 1894; offg. jail supt., Feb., 1895; acting surgn., 2nd district, May, 1895; ret'd., May, 1897.

DYSON, HERBERT JEKYL, F.R.C.S., Major, Indian Med. Service (sanitary commr., Bengal).—First commr., 1st April, 1885; acted as civil surgn. in various stations from Jan., 1889; acting dep. sanitary commr., Punjab, Nov., 1889; confd., Oct., 1890; sanitary commr., Bengal, Aug., 1895.

DYSON, JOHN, late Oudh Commn.—Joined the service, 8th June, 1858, as dep. mag. and dep. collr. in Bengal; from Dec., 1861, served in Oudh as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; small cause court judge, Lucknow, 1869; district judge, Faizabad, Aug., 1886; offg. commr., Sept., 1886; offg. jud. commr., Oudh, Aug., 1889; addl. jud. commr., July, 1890; ret'd., March, 1891.

DYSON, ROBERT CHARLES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Dec., 1891.

EADES, FREDERICK TALBOT (junior clerk, Store Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Jan., 1875; staff clerk, July, 1896; junior clerk, March, 1901.

EALES, CHARLES LINDSTEDT MODELLIER, B.L., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 4th Dec., 1878, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1887; small cause court judge, March, 1893; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1893.

EALES, HERBERT LOVELY, Indian C.S. (commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 30th Oct., 1879, and served in British Burma as asst. commr.; on special duty in Upper Burma, Dec., 1885; dep. commr., May, 1888; offg. sec. to chief commr., April-May, 1890; supt. of provl. census, 1890-92; offd. as prest. Rangoon municipality, April-Dec., 1896; and again from April, 1897; commr., Aug., 1901.

EARDLEY-WILMOT, SAINT-HILL, India Forest Dept. (conservator of forests).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1873, as asst. conservator in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; dep. conservator, Nov., 1883; conservator, Oct., 1891; transferred to Burma, Jan., 1900.

EARLE, ARCHDALE, Indian C.S. (commr. of excise and salt, Bengal).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 18th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. private sec. to the lieut.-govr., Feb., 1886; offg. under sec. to govt., jud., &c., depts., Feb., 1889; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1891; mag. and collr., Jan., 1895; commr. of excise and salt, May, 1901.

EAST, WILLIAM ALBERT, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 26th Nov., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. registrar, dep. registrar and sealer of the high court, and asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of Panchganj, Nov., 1876; junr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent;

Dharwar, June, 1881; senr. collr., June, 1885; ret'd., May, 1891.

EBDEN, EDWARD JAMES, B.A., late Indian C.S., Bombay.—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Sch., Ipswich, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 6th Dec., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from Sept., 1872, served in the forest dept. as acting dep. conservator; on famine duty, Dharwar, 1877, and in Mysore, 1877-78; from July, 1878, served as asst. collr., railway mag., and forest settl. officer; first asst., Aug., 1881; junr. collr. and mag., June, 1888; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1890; offg. commr., southern divn., Sept., 1895; commr., May, 1897; ret. Feb., 1900.

ECCLES, JOHN, M.A., Survey of India (supt.).—Joined the service as asst. supt. May, 1885; dep. supt., Dec., 1890; supt., May, 1898.

EDGAR, SIR JOHN WARE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; served in Cachar from Dec., 1863, as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; on special duty with the Lushai expdn., 1871; mag. and collr., Shahabad, Feb., 1878; commr. of Chittagong, Dec., 1885; chief sec. to govt. of Bengal, Aug., 1887; K.C.I.E., May, 1889; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Jan., 1892; ret'd., July, 1892.

EDGE, SIR JOHN, Kt., Q.C., B.A., LL.B., LL.D. (hon.), B.L. (member of the Council of India).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1866; Q.C., 1886; went the northern and north-eastern circuit, and was apptd. chief justice, N.W. Prov., Aug., 1886; vice-chancellor, Allahabad univ., 1887-93; apptd. member of the Council of India, March, 1868.

EDGERLEY, STEYNING WILLIAM, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., pol. dept., Bombay).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 20th Nov., 1879, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; from May, 1881, served in acting appts. in Sind; 2nd asst. collr. in Sind, 1885 and 1886; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct.-Dec., 1888; private sec. to the govr. of Bombay, March, 1889, to July, 1895; C.I.E., May, 1895; jun. collr. and mag., July, 1896; sec. to govt., pol., jud., and legisl. depts., March, 1897; and acting chief sec., July, 1898; joint compiler of a manual of village accts. for Sind, 1887, which he also transld. into Sindhi.

EDGINGTON, ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 21st Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

EDMONSTONE-MONTGOMERIE, CHARLES WATERTON, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Uppingham and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 7th Dec., 1887, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; acting commr. of settlts. and agric., May, 1898; 2nd sec. to chief commr., April, 1899.

EDWARDES, STEPHEN MEREDYTH.—Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1895, and served in

Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. to collr. of land rev., May, 1898; acting under sec. rev. and finl. depts., Aug., 1898.

EDWARDS, ALFRED CLARKE, M.A., B.L., Bengal Educ. Service (principal, Presy. College).—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd., 24th April, 1878; arrived, 28th Sept., 1878, and served as profir. in the presidency coll. and principal, Rajshahye coll.; principal, Dacca coll., Nov., 1887; principal, Patna coll., April, 1897; principal, Presidency Coll., Jan., 1900.

EDWARDS, ARTHUR RICHARD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 29th Nov., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

EDWARDS, THOMAS CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 29th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., April, 1899; city mag., Lucknow, July, 1899; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Feb., 1901.

EDWARDS, WILLIAM RICE, M.D., Major, Indian Medical Service.—First commr., April, 1886; civil surgeon, Quetta, April, 1892; on Field Service, South Africa, 1900.

EGERTON, CHARLES PHILIP, Capt., I.S.C. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Entered the service, Feb., 1884; in mil. employ till March, 1888; apptd. to the Punjab commn. as asst. commr., March, 1888; dep. commr., July, 1897; dist. judge, Sept., 1897; dep. commr., Jan., 1898.

EGERTON, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Lt.-Col., late I.S.C.—Joined the service, Aug., 1873; was in mil. employ till April, 1879; served in Punjab as asst. commr. from April, 1879; dep. commr., June, 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1901.

EGERTON, SIR ROBERT EYLES, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Edinburgh Acad., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. from Haileybury, in 1850; served in the Punjab as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer, and was in charge of the Lahore settlt., 1854-58; dep. commr., 1856; addl. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1863; commr., Nagpur, Central Prov., March, 1864; offd. as jud. commr., Central Prov., at various times from 1865 to 1867; finl. commr. of the Punjab, March, 1868; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, 1871, and again in 1872 and 1877; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, April, 1877, to April, 1882, when he retired from the service.

EGERTON, ROBERT WALTER, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. as asst. engr., Sept., 1898, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1890; under-sec. to govt. of Madras, railway branch, Nov., 1890.

EGERTON, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 5th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl., &c., depts., April, 1895; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1897.

EGGAR, H. C. (solicitor to govt. of India).—Apptd. offg. solicitor to govt. of India, Dec., 1898; confirmed, March, 1900.

ELGIN AND KINCARDINE (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. VICTOR ALEXANDER BRUCE, K.G.,

G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., P.C., LL.D.—Born, 16th May, 1849; succeeded his father (viceroi and govr.-gen. of India, 1862-63) in the earldom in 1863; educ. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A., 1877); received the hon. degree of LL.D. from the Univ. of St. Andrews in 1886; was treasurer of Her Majesty's household and first commr. of works in Mr. Gladstone's third administration (1886); sworn of the privy council, Feb., 1886; apptd. lord lieut. of Fifeshire, Aug., 1886; and is also a univ. commr. for Scotland; assumed office as viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, 27th Jan., 1894; resigned, Jan., 1890.

ELIOT, JOHN, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.S. (Meteorological reporter to the Govt. of India).—Joined the service, June, 1869; from 27th Dec., 1869, to Feb., 1876, served in the N.W. Prov. as profir. at Thomason and Muir colls., supt. of the meteorological observatory, Allahabad, and meteorological reporter to govt.; from Feb., 1876, served in Bengal, as profir., presidency coll., and meteorological reporter; meteorological reporter to govt. of India, May, 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1897.

ELIOTT, FRANCIS HARDINGE, Major, I.S.C. (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., Oct., 1881; on military duty till July, 1888, when he joined the Burma commn. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., July, 1900.

ELIOTT, LAWRENCE EDWARD, Major, I.S.C. (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., Aug., 1880; employed in Burma as asst. commr., July, 1886; offg. supt., N. Shan states, July-Nov., 1890, and from May, 1894; dep. commr., Jan., 1896.

ELLENOR, THOMAS RALPH (staff clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; staff clerk, May, 1901.

ELLERCAMPT, HERBERT McDONALD (second-class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, Oct. 10th, 1879.

ELLIOT, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS HUGH, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 21st Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from June, 1873, to Nov., 1875, served in Berar as educt. inspr., and acting dir. of public instrn.; principal of the Raja's sch., and tutor and govr. of the Gaekwar of Baroda, Dec., 1875; C.I.E., May, 1883; on duty to England with the Gaekwar of Baroda, 1884; survey and settlt. officer, Baroda, March, 1890; ret'd., June, 1896.

ELLIOT, FREDERICK EDEN, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1858; arrived, 16th March, 1858; served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., April, 1877; district and sessions judge, March, 1881; ret'd., March, 1894.

ELLIOTT, ALGERNON, Haidarabad Admn. (judicial commr.).—Educ. at Repton Sch.; joined the service, 16th March, 1869, as acting asst. district supt. of police, Central Prov.; transf. to Berar as acting asst. commr., June, 1869; confd., April, 1874; dep. commr., July, 1889; civil and sess. judge, Oct., 1895; judicial commr., Feb., 1900.

ELLIOTT, SIR CHARLES ALFRED, K.C.S.I., LL.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1856; arrived, 30th Nov., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint

mag., and in Oudh as asst. commr.; from Feb., 1863, to Oct., 1866, served in the Central Prov. as settlt. officer, Hoshangabad; served subsequently as settlt. officer, Farakhabad, and as sec. to govt. N.W. Prov., 1870-77; commr., Meerut, Oct., 1877; famine commr., Mysore, Sept., 1877; addl. sec. to govt. of India, famine branch, May, 1878; sec. to famine commn., June, 1878; census commr., Oct., 1880; chief commr. of Assam, March, 1881; prest. of the public expenditure committee, Feb., 1886; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1887; ordinary member of govt.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1887; lieut.-govr. of Bengal, Dec., 1890, to Dec., 1895; author of 'Chronicles of Unao' and of the reports of the famine commn., 1879, and finance commn., 1887.

ELLIOTT, CHARLES FREDERIC, Punjab Forest Dept. (conservator of forests).—Joined the service, Jan., 1868; asst. conservator, Jan., 1869; dep. consr., July, 1880; conservator, Dec., 1896.

ELLIOTT, ROBERT ERNEST ALGERNON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 24th Nov., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

ELLIS, LOVEYS EDWARD (second-class clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th Sept., 1894.

ELLIS, THOMAS PETER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Oswestry High Sch. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 28th Feb., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ELLISON, THOMAS EDWARD, late Bengal C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Coll., Bradford; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 19th Oct., 1864; asst. commr., Delhi, Aug., 1865; transf. to Central Prov., Oct., 1868; judge, small cause court, Nagpur, Sept., 1883; dep. commr., Nov., 1884; ret'd., Dec., 1890.

ELPHINSTONE, GRAHAM WARBURTON, Indian C.S. (sub-coll. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived in Madras, 19th Nov., 1887, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., July, 1894; sub-coll. and joint mag., Dec., 1899.

ELSMIE, GEORGE ROBERT, C.S.I., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 12th Feb., 1858, and was apptd. asst. commr., Punjab; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, Oct., 1868; dep. commr., May, 1870; from 1871 to 1877 offd. as addl. commr.; civil and sessions judge, March, 1877; commr. and supt., Lahore, 1880-82, and 1885-87; judge, chief court, Punjab, 1882-85; 2nd finl. commr., May, 1887; also member of govt.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1888; 1st finl. commr., March, 1889; on special duty at Rawal Pindi durbar, March, 1885; C.S.I., Jan., 1893; ret'd., Feb., 1893; author of 'Notes on Peshawur Crime.'

ELSTON, JOSEPH, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 16th Oct., 1871, and posted to state railways; asst. engr., Sept., 1872; exec. engr., May, 1883; engr.-in-chief, Gauhati railway survey, March, 1898; ret'd., March, 1901.

ELWIN, EDGAR ALFRED, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Dover Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 7th Nov., 1883, and served in Madras

as asst. collr. and mag.; manager, Jaipur estate, July-Oct., 1890; senr. asst. collr., Vizagapatam, Feb., 1891; sub.-collr. and joint mag., June, 1896; collr. and mag., Dec., 1898.

ELWIN, ERNEST BARNES, Indian C.S. (special asst., Madras).—Educ. at Dover Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 5th Jan., 1891, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; special asst., Nov., 1896.

EMANUEL, ALFRED EDWARD LEWIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 19th Nov., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and mag.

EMERSON, THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Coll., the Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., March, 1898; personal asst. to chief commr., May, 1900.

EMSLIE, HAROLD HEATHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Felsted Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 17th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

ENGLISH, ALEXANDER EMANUEL, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Rugby, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ENTHOVEN, REGINALD EDWARD, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and inspr. of factories; 2nd asst., June, 1896; 1st asst. and under-sec. to govt., gen. dept., census, July, 1900; author of 'Cotton Fabric of the Bombay Presidency.'

ERSKINE, CLAUDE FRANCIS, Survey of India (dep. supt.).—Joined the service, June, 1880; asst. supt., Dec., 1881; dep. supt. Jan., 1896.

ERSKINE, GEORGE ELPHINSTONE, Maj.-Gen. late Kumaun Commn.—Arrived as cornet Bombay light cavalry, 9th Dec., 1857; employed under the mil. dept., including service with the Rajputana field force, till Feb., 1863, when he joined the Oudh commn. as asst. commr.; asst. settlt. officer, May, 1865; personal asst. to chief commr., Oudh, July, 1871, to April, 1878; offg. sec. to chief commr., May to Aug., 1876; sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. in forest branch, and Oudh rev. dept., April, 1878; commr., Jan., 1882; on special duty to inquire into condition of tenantry in Oudh, 1882-83; wrote a report on that subject, and a digest of Oudh rev. circulars; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1895.

ERSKINE, KEITH DAVID, Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (reslt. in Western Rajputana States).—1st commn., 6th Feb., 1884; pol. asst., May, 1889; pol. agent, May, 1897; in Haraoti and Tonk, Nov., 1900; resdt. in Western Rajputana States, April, 1901.

ESTCOURT, ERNEST ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 29th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ETESON, ALFRED, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Assam.—Joined the Indian med. dept. as asst. surgn., May, 1854; arrived, 30th June, 1854; held various mil. med. charges (medal for services during the Mutiny); sec. to surgn.-gen., Indian med. dept., March, 1879; dep. surgn.-gen., eastern frontier district, and sanitary commr., Assam, Jan., 1884; ret'd., Jan., 1889.

EUAN-SMITH, SIR CHARLES BEAN, K.C.B., C.S.I., D.C.L., Col.—Ensign, Madras infantry, March., 1859; served in Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (medal); sec. to Sir F. Goldsmid's mission to Persia, 1870-72; C.S.I., Nov., 1872; private sec. to Sir Bartle Frere, on special mission to Zanzibar and Muscat, 1872-73; on special duty in India office, 1873-74; pol. agent and consul-gen., Zanzibar, May, 1875; on special duty, foreign dept., on occasion of Prince of Wales's visit to Calcutta, 1875; first asst. rest., Haidarabad, March, 1876; pol. agent, and consul-gen., Muscat, July, 1879; during the Afghan war, 1879-80, was on special pol. duty on the staff of Lieut.-Gen. Sir D. Stewart, and subsequently with Gen. Hill's divn., and with the Kabul-Kandahar force under Gen. Roberts (medal with two clasps, and bronze star); acted as rest., Mewar, in 1882 and 1884; asst. pol. agent, Banswara, Dec., 1882; from May, 1883, served as addl. pol. agent, Bhartpur and Karauli; apptd. consul-gen. at Zanzibar, 1888; ret'd. from the army, March, 1889; K.C.B., Nov., 1890; envoy extraordinary and minister plenipotentiary at Tangiers, 1891-93; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1893.

EVANS, ARTHUR CLEMENT, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.)—Trained at Thomason Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Jan., 1876; asst. engr., July, 1876; exec. engr., Nov., 1887.

EVANS, CHARLES THOMAS, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; from Aug., 1860, served in subord. offices; apptd. to the dept., Sept., 1869, as asst. engr., and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., June, 1876; ret'd., Nov., 1896.

EVANS, ERNEST SYDNEY (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office)—Clerk, 2nd class, 11th March, 1895.

EVANS, FREDERICK BUISSON, Indian C.S. (spcl. asst. settl. officer, Madras)—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spcl. asst. settl. officer, March, 1901.

EVANS, GEORGE HENRY. Veterinary Capt. (supt., civil veterinary dept., Burma)—Joined the (home) service, 27th Aug., 1834; supt. civil veterinary dept., Burma, April, 1892; served in Chin-Lushai expedn., 1889-90; author of a book on Materia Medica in Burmese.

EVANS, HENRY FARRINGTON, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (member, Board of Revenue, N.W. Prov. and Oudh)—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and settl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1880; special judge, Jhansi, July, 1882; joint mag., 1st grade, Oct., 1882; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1887; on special duty Main dras and Bombay, 1890-91, and as

member of Deccan agriculturists relief commn., 1891-92; offg. jud. commr., Oudh, May-July, 1893; commr. and agent, Benares, Dec., 1893; commr., Agra, Nov., 1895; chief sec. to govt., Dec., 1896; junr. member, rev. board, Nov., 1898; C.S.I., June, 1899; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Dec., 1899; author of 'Farukhabad and Agra settl. reports,' &c.

EVANS, LOFTUS GEORGE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh)—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 13th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Sept., 1886; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, March, 1891; district and sessions judge, April, 1891.

EVANS-GORDON, WILLIAM EDEN, Maj., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 15th July, 1876; from Feb., 1883, served under the foreign dept. with the Central India horse; boundary settl. officer, Central India, 1883-84; attaché, foreign dept., Oct., 1885; also translr. of foreign European languages, Aug., 1887; asst. sec., Nov., 1887; pol. officer in charge of Sardar Ayub Khan, March, 1888; asst. rest., Kashmir, Nov., 1890; joint commr., Ladakh, March, 1889; pol. officer in attendance on the Gackwar in Europe, 1894; offg. pol. agent, Jhaisiwar, March, 1895; ret'd., May, 1897.

EVERETT, STEPHEN HENRY (senr. clerk, India Audit Office)—Educ. at Dulwich Coll.; writer, India office, May, 1872; junr. examr., India audit office, Dec., 1873; designation changed to junr. clerk, Oct., 1892; senr. clerk, Mar., 1897.

EVERS, BENJAMIN, M.D., C.M., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the service, April, 1868; arrived, 27th Oct., 1868, served as civil surgn. and supt. of jail at various stations from April, 1876; ret'd., April, 1896.

EWBANK, ALFRED LLOYD VANDYKE, M.A., late Educ. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd., 16th Jan., 1868; arrived, 28th Feb., 1868, and served as prof. Patna coll., and principal, Dacca coll.; prof., Patna coll., Oct. 1879; principal, Oct., 1881; ret'd., April, 1897.

EYRE, GERVAS SELWYN, B.L., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C. (commr., Burma)—First commn., 12th June, 1869; served in mil. dept. until July, 1879, when he was apptd. asst. commr. in Burma; on duty with the Burma field force, 1887; and with the force operating against the Chins, 1888; dep. commr., May, 1888; offg. inspr.-gen. of police, 1893, and from March, 1894, to Oct., 1895; commr., April, 1900; royal humane society's medal for saving lives of two brother officers.

EZECHIEL, JOSEPH ALOYSIUS, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal)—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and sub-divl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1899.

FABRE-TONNERRE, LOUIS EUSEBE, Bengal Police dept. (dist. supt., Assam)—Joined the service, 26th Feb., 1874, and served as asst. supt. and dist. supt.

FAGAN, PATRICK JAMES, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab)—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 26th Nov., 1887, and served in the Punjab as asst.

commr.; settlt. officer, Bikan'r, 1892-93; dep. commr., Aug., 1896.

FAGAN, CHRISTOPHER GEORGE FORBES, Maj., Indian Staff Corps (pol. agent, Kota).—Educ. at Harrow; first commission, 63rd Foot, 11th Feb., 1875; joined the Bengal Staff Corps, Feb., 1879; served in the Afghan war of 1879-80 (medal); served in the commissariat dept., June, 1881, to June, 1882; squadron offr. 10th B. Lancers, June, 1882; asst. dist. supt. of police, Punjab, Sept., 1885; dist. supt., March, 1887; asst. residnt. at Hyderabad, Nov., 1892; asst. pol. agent, Bussorah, Feb., 1897; pol. agent, Maskat, April, 1898; pol. agent, Kota, Feb., 1900.

FAICHNIE, ALEXANDER GEORGE, late Postal Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1858; served for some time as supt. of the Rawal Pindi mil. horse van and bullock train; dep. postmr.-gen., Central Prov. and Berar, Nov., 1879; retd., Oct., 1894.

FAIRWEATHER, JAMES, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Arrived, 20th March, 1856; in civil charge of Rawal Pindi and its jail, April, 1869; civil surgn. at Gurdaspur, April, 1874, and subsequently at other stations in the Punjab; offd. as sanitary commr., surgn.-gen., Punjab, and inspr.-gen. of prisons; also acted for various periods as med. adviser to the Maharaja of Patiala; retd., 1886; author of several papers on professional subjects.

FALLE, PHILIP JOHN, Comdr. R.I.M., Bengal Marine Dept. (port officer, Calcutta).—Entered the Royal Indian Marine, Jan., 1868; apptd. port officer, Calcutta, April, 1898.

FANSHAW, ARTHUR UPTON, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (dir.-gen. of Post Office in India).—Educ. at Repton; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, Nov., 1871, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and inspr.-gen. of registrn.; asst. sec. to the chief commr., July, 1880; in charge of model farm, Nagpur, 1880-81; from April, 1881, served in the postal dept.; postmr.-gen., Bombay, March, 1882; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Dec., 1888; on special duty under home dept., Jan., 1889; dir.-gen. of the post office of India, May, 1889; member of the royal commn. on opium, 1893-95; C.S.I., Jan., 1896.

FANSHAW, HERBERT CHARLES, C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Bedford, Tonbridge, and Repton; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 6th Nov., 1873, and was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; on famine duty in Bengal, 1874; offg. settlt. officer, Rohtak, 1875-76 and 1877; asst. to the officer on special duty in Kashmir, June to Sept., 1879; junr. sec. to govt. Punjab, Nov., 1888; rev. sec., March, 1890; offd. for various periods as chief sec.; dep. commr., April, 1892; chief sec. to govt., Punjab, Sept., 1895, to Nov., 1898; commr. and supt., Jan., 1899; C.S.I., May, 1900; retd., May, 1901; author of the Rohtak settlt. report, and 'Punjab Famine Code.'

FARDUNJI KUVARJI TARAPURVALLA, C.I.E., B.A., L.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service 28th March, 1876, and served as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1888; C.I.E., Jan., 1900.

FARLEY, FRANK, Indian Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept. 11th Oct., 1879, and posted

to the Punjab; asst. engr., July, 1880; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

FARMER, HENRY RICHARD, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Loughborough, Bromsgrove, and Uppingham Gr. Schs.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 4th Dec., 1868, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1887; retd., June, 1896.

FARQUHAR, WILLIAM, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 28th May, 1858; arrived, 31st Dec., 1858, and served under the mil. dept. in Madras, Burma, and the Straits Settlements; also held civil med. charge, Tounghoo, in 1859; med. officer, Utakamand, Nov., 1882; dep. surgn.-gen., Belgaum and W. districts, July, 1885; retd., July, 1890.

FARRAN, GEORGE HOUGHTON, M.A., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Served from 17th May, 1866, as head asst. in the prothonotary's office, high court, Bombay; dep. registrar, commr. for taking affidavits, and judge's clerk, Nov., 1873; asst. commr. for taking accts. and asst. taxing master, July, 1876; master and registrar in equity, commr. for taking accts., and taxing officer, Sept., 1893; retd., Jan. 1899.

FARRANT, JAMES THOMAS, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Oct., 1877; and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., March, 1878; exec. engr., Dec., 1892.

FAULDER, CECIL JOSEPH SALKELD, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived 9th Dec., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1887; mag. and collr., March, 1892.

FAULKNER, ERNEST TRVELYAN, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

FAUNTHORPE, JOHN CHAMPION, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rossall Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 26th Oct., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1897; under sec. to govt., Jan. to Oct., 1899.

FAWCETT, CHARLES GORDON HILL, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 9th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. and asst. judge, Feb., 1897; under-sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., June, 1898; acting remembrancer of legal affairs, March, 1899.

FAWCETT, EDWARD PINDER, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Dean Close Sch., Cheltenham, Ley's Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1897, arrived 7th Dec., 1898; and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag., and collr.

FAYRER, SIR JOSEPH, Bart., K.C.S.I., LL.D., M.D., F.R.S., F.R.C.P., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Gen.—Acting asst. surgn., R.N., Aug., 1847; asst. surgn., royal artillery, 1849; joined the East India Company's service, April, 1850, and served under the mil. dept., also holding civil

surgery. of Cherra Punji, 1850; served in the Burma campaign, and ofd. as civil surg., Rangoon, 1853; apptd. residency surg., Lucknow, by Lord Dalhousie, for services during war in Burma; hon. asst. rest. at Lucknow, Sept., 1854; civil surg., Lucknow, March, 1856; brevet rank of surg. for services in defence of Lucknow residency during the siege, Sept., 1858; profir. of surgery and surg. of med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1860; med. charge of Mysore princes, June, 1860; fellow of univ. of Calcutta, March, 1861, and served for two years as prest. of the med. faculty; prest. of the Asiatic Society of Bengal, 1867; offg. surg. to viceroy, 1869; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Edinburgh in his tour through India, 1870; hon. physician to the Queen, July, 1871; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales to India, 1875-76, in med. charge of his party; ret'd., 1874; prest. of med. board, India office, 1874-95; also member of army sanitary commn., and of senate of army med. sch. at Netley; bart., Jan., 1896; author of 'Clinical Surgery in India', 'Clinical and Pathological Observations in India', 'Thanatophidia of India,' and other works; retired from India Office Medical Board, Jan., 1895.

FELL, GEORGE BUTLER HUNTER, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., Burma).—Educ. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 7th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; personal asst. to chief commr., April, 1897; private sec. to lieut.-govr., May, 1897; acting asst. sec., March, 1899; confd., Oct., 1899; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1900.

FENNER, HENRY ALGERNON SHRAPNEL, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 20th June, 1861; exec. engr., Sept., 1868; suptg. engr., March, 1887; offg. chief engr. and joint sec. to govt. of the Punjab, Jan.-Feb., 1892; ret'd., May, 1893.

FENTON, LAYARD LIVINGSTONE, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C. (1st asst., Kathiawar).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1870, and after serving under the mil. dept. was transfid. to the rev. survey dept. as asst. supt., Nov., 1873; asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, March, 1880; supt. of surveys under the rajasthanik court, Dec., 1890; prest. rajasthanik court, April, 1896; 1st asst. Kathiawar, April, 1899.

FENTON, MICHAEL WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 18th Nov., 1883, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., under sec. to govt., Oct., 1887, to Nov., 1891; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., July, 1892; rev. sec. to govt., Oct., 1896; dep. commr., Oct., 1899; author of 'Punjab Municipal Manual.'

FENTON, THOMAS CHARLES, B.L., Registry and Record Dept., India Office (staff clerk).—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd May, 1873; attached to the store dept. till Aug., 1879, and subsequently to the registry dept.; called to the Bar (Middle Temple), 17th Nov., 1898; staff clerk, Oct., 1901.

FERARD, HENRY CECIL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Eton, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 17th Nov., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.,

joint mag., April, 1893; dep. commr., July, 1896; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; mag. and collr., July, 1898.

FERARD, JOHN EDWARD, India Office (senior clerk, pub. works dept.).—Educ. at Eton, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; upper divn. clerk, local govt. board, 2nd Feb., 1892; junr. clerk, India office, 7th April, 1893; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state, Feb., 1894; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, July, 1898, to Feb., 1901; senior clerk, pub. works dept., March, 1901.

FERGUSSON, RT. HON. SIR JAMES, Bart., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., C.I.E., M.P.—Born in 1832; educ. at Rugby, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; entered the grenadier guards, and served in the Crimean campaign, 1854-55 (medal and three clasps); ret'd. from the army in 1855; M.P. for Ayrshire, 1854-57, and 1859-68, and for N.E. Manchester, from 1885; under sec. of state for India, June, 1866; under sec. for the home dept., July, 1867; govr. of S. Australia, 1868-73; govr. of New Zealand, 1873-74; govr. of Bombay, April, 1880, to March, 1885; parly. under sec. of state for foreign affairs, Aug., 1886; royal commr. for the Adelaide exhibn. of 1887; postmr.-gen., Sept., 1891, to Aug., 1892.

FERGUSSON, JOHN CARLISLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 8th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. as asst. commr., May, 1901.

FERNANDEZ, EDWARD ELIAS, Indian Forest Dept., Cent. Provs. (conservator of forests).—Entered the service Oct., 1873, as asst. conservator; dep. dir. Forest School, Dec., 1888; dep. conservator, Feb. 1891; conservator, Aug., 1900; author of several works on forestry.

FERRAR, MICHAEL LLOYD, B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Belfast Acad. Instn., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 21st Jan., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfid. to Oudh, Sept., 1865, as asst. commr.; from July, 1868, to April, 1875, served in the settl. dept.; dep. commr., April, 1883; mag. and collr., March, 1884; commr. in Oudh, Aug., 1889; commr., Gorakhpur, July, 1891; author of Sitapur settl. report, 1873; ret'd., April, 1896.

FERRERS, VVYVYAN MACLEOD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Bath Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 10th Dec., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

FERRIS, WILLIAM BUTLER, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C. (pol. agent, Mahikantha).—First commn., 18th Oct., 1869; in pol. employ from May, 1874; rest. and pol. agent, Somali coast, March, 1896; pol. agent, Mahikantha, Feb., 1897.

FFINCH, BENJAMIN TRAILL, C.I.E., India Office (dir.-in-Chief, Indo-European Telegraph, Public Works Dept.).—Apptd. to the Indian telegraph dept., 5th Nov., 1857; transfid. to Indo-European telegraph dept. as dep. dir. of the Persian Gulf section, April, 1875; officially designated dir., March, 1884; in pol. charge, Mekran coast, Aug., 1879; justice of the peace for Mekran, Aug., 1880; dir.-in-chief, Indo-European telegraph dept., 1st April, 1893; C.I.E., June, 1897.

FFORDF, ARTHUR BROWNLOW, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1800; arrived, 31st Oct., 1802, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1808.

FFRENCH-MULLEN, DOUGLAS, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resdy. surgn. and chief med. offr., Rajputana).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1877; arrived, 25th Oct., 1877, and served from April, 1878, under the foreign dept. in med. charge of the Mewar Bhil corps, and offg. residency surgn., Mewar; confd. in latter appt., Nov., 1886; civil surgn., Ajmir, and med. officer of Merwara battalion, March, 1893; resdy. surgn. and chief med. offr., Rajputana, May, 1901.

FFRENCH-MULLEN, JARLATH, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Bengal Med. Dept. (civil surgn., Rajshahye).—Joined the service, March, 1878; in mil. employ till Aug., 1878; since when he has held civil surgeonies in various places in Bengal; and has been resdt. surgn., Calcutta Med. Coll.

FIDDIAN, WILLIAM, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Huddersfield Coll., Shrewsbury Gr. Sch., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 14th Nov., 1868, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, May, 1874; offg. commr., Sundarbans, Jan., 1879; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1880; mag. and collr., April, 1886; ret'd., June, 1894.

FIELD, CHARLES DICKENSON, M.A., LL.D., B.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 11th Oct., 1860; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and registrar, high court; from Dec., 1870, served in the jud. dept.; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1879; member of provl. legisl. council, Dec., 1879; judge, high court, Oct., 1880; ret'd., Sept., 1886; author of 'Essays on Indian Topics,' 'Chronological Table of, and Index to, the Indian Statute Book,' and other legal works.

FIELD, GEORGE MOYLE ROBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 16th Oct., 1872, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; services lent to Patiala state, 1887-93; suptg. engr., Jan., 1899.

FILGATE, ALEXANDER JEROME, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 1857; served in the China war, 1860 (medal and clasps); joined the Madras public works dept., Oct., 1863; dep. compt., public works accts., July, 1865; dep. consulting engr. for railways, and examr. of accts., Feb., 1866; from March, 1867, served in the India public works dept. as asst. acctnt.-gen.; dep. acctnt.-gen., and under sec. to govt., March, 1872; acctnt.-gen. and dep. sec., Sept., 1881; member of fin. committee, 1886-87; ret'd., Oct., 1890.

FILGATE, TOWNLEY MACARTNEY, late Registrn. Depts. and Jail, Bombay.—Arrived, 1st March, 1867, and served as mil. sec. to govr. of Bombay, and registrar-gen. of assurances; from July, 1871, served as inspr.-gen. of registrn.; inspr.-gen. of prisons, in addn., April, 1887; also registrar-gen. of

births, deaths, and marriages, Sept., 1888; ret'd., April, 1901.

FINCH, CHARLES THOMAS, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office (2nd class clerk).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872.

FINDLAY, CHARLES STEWART, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Provs.).—Educ. at Aberdeen and Edinburgh Univs. and at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 5th Dec., 1898, and served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr.

FINIMORE, BENJAMIN KINGTON, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1881, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.
