Prov., and chemical examr., Aug., 1866; supt. of new central prison, Farakhabad, Dec., 1866; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons and civil hosp., N.W. Prov., in 1877, 1879, and 1882; supt., Bareilly jails, Dec., 1877; dep. sanitary commr., 1st circle, Dec., 1883; ret'd., Oct., 1885.

GRANT, GEORGE FRANCIS MYTTON, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and Brighton; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Dec., 1864, and served as asst. collr. and mag., branch registrar-gen., and educ. inspr. in Sind; in charge of the Sachin state, May, 1870; junr. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Dec., 1882; junr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Thana, March, 1885; senr. collr., Sept., 1886; commr., N. divn., April, 1890; ret'd., Nov., 1890.

GRANT, GREGORY COLQUHOUN, Col., I.S.C., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 4th Nov., 1853; on mil. duty till Nov., 1860, including service with the Okhamandal field force in 1859; served subsequently as dep. collr. and

mag. in Sind; judge and sessions judge, March, 1865; also acted as jud. commr. in Sind, and judge of the sadar court; reverted to mil. dept., 1890.

GRANT, J. G. GARROW, Dr., late Emigration Dept., Bengal.—Served in the dept. from 22nd May, 1863, as med. inspr. and protector of labourers, Kushtia; protector of emigrants and supt. of labour transports, Calcutta, Feb., 1871; supt. of emigration, Calcutta, Jan., 1874; ret'd., 1889.

GRANT, JOHN HAY, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 6th Feb., 1857, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., railway mag., and asst. rev. and police commr.; junr. collr., Sept., 1877; municipal commr., Bombay, Nov., 1877; collr. of Bombay, and supt. of stamps and stationery, Aug., 1883; special commr. for Bombay for international exhibn. at Calcutta, 1883; ret'd., Jan., 1888.

GRANT, JOHN PYNE, Col., I.S.C., late Rev. Survey of Mysore.—First commn., Bombay infantry, Jan., 1856; joined the rev. survey, 1860; supt., Jan., 1868; ret'd., Jan., 1890.

GRANT, THOMAS (2nd class clerk, Acctt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 28th May, 1875.

GRANT, TREVOR JOHN CHICHELE, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 27th Jan., 1858; served in Bengal as asst. and joint mag.; mag. and collr., May, 1874; offg. sec., board of rev., April, 1874; ret'd., Jan., 1893.

GRANT-BROWN, GEORGE EUSTACE RIOU, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 9th Dec., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Aug., 1901.

GRANT-DUFF, RT. HON. SIR MOUNTSTUART ELPHINSTONE, G.C.S.I., C.I.E., F.R.S.—Born in 1829; educ. at Edinburgh, and at Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A., 1853); called to the bar at the Inner Temple in 1854; represented the Elgin burghs in parliament from 1857 to 1881; under sec. of state for India, Dec., 1868, to Feb., 1874; under sec. of state for the colonies, April, 1880, to Aug., 1881; govt. of Madras, Nov., 1881, to Dec., 1886; was lord rector of the univ. of Aberdeen, 1866-72; author of 'Studies in European Politics,' 'Elgin Speeches,' 'A Political Survey,' 'Memoir of Sir H. S. Maine,' 'Ernest Rénan,' 'Notes from a Diary,' &c.

GRANVILLE, HENRY CHUDLEIGH, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Civil Engrg. Coll.; entered the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1876; exec. engr., Dec., 1887.

GRAVES, SOMERSET HENRY PAUL, Col., I.S.C., late Burma Police Dept.—First commn., 2nd Sept., 1868; served in the 26th native infantry, and in June, 1885, was apptd. supt. of police, Port Blair; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Upper Burma, May, 1887; reverted to mil. duty, 1888.

GRAY, EVELYN, B.L., Indian C.S. (senr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 27th Dec., 1878, and served as asst. collr. and mag. and forest settl. officer; transf'd. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Dec., 1891; dep. commr., Jan., 1892;

re-transfd. to Bombay as junr. collr., Dec., 1896; senr. collr., Jan., 1898.

GRAY, MALCOLM ALEXANDER, B.A., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late dep. commr., Assam.—Ensign, 3rd (Buffs) regt., 7th Nov., 1868; arrived, 1st Feb., 1870; served in the Assam commn., from Aug., 1873, as offg. asst. commr. and asst. inspr. of immigrants; confd. as asst. commr., July, 1877; forest settlt. officer, Oct., 1885; dep. commr., Sept., 1888; ret'd., April, 1891.

GRAY, PERCY, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wellington Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 30th Dec., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; junr. sec., board of rev., April-July, 1885; joint mag., Sept., 1887, and asst. commr., Feb., 1890; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, May, 1891; registrar, high court, March, 1892; dep. commr., March, 1894; mag. and coll., Nov., 1895.

GRAY, ROBERT, M.B., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the Indian med. dept. in 1859; arrived, 11th March, 1860, and served in the Punjab from Feb., 1863, as civil asst. surgn., jail supt. and surgn. at various stations; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Sept., 1884; ret'd., April, 1891.

GRAY, WELLINGTON, L.M., L. Ch., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 31st March, 1866; arrived, 9th Nov., 1866, and served as profir. and principal, Grant med. coll., surgn. at the Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., curator of Victoria and Albert museum, and sec. to the surgn.-gen.; ret'd., April, 1893.

GRAY, WILLIAM BORMAN, Indian Public Works Dept. (examiner of accts).—Apptd. asst. examr., 12th April, 1876, and posted to Punjab Northern State Rly.; dep. examr., Jan., 1882; examr., Dec., 1886.

GREANY, JOHN PHILIP, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Poona).—Joined the service 31st March, 1875, and was employed on mil. duty, and in various acting civil posts; apptd. civil surgn., July, 1883; acted as dep. sanitary commr. in 1888 and 1893.

GREAVES, SIR GEORGE RICHARDS, K.C.B., K.C.M.G., Gen.—First commn., 30th Nov., 1849; served in the Mutiny, the Yusufzai expdn. of 1858 (medal with clasp), and the war in New Zealand, 1862-66 (medal); chief of the staff in the Ashanti expdn. of 1873-74 (medal with clasp and C.B.), and in the Soudan campaign in 1885 (K.C.B., medal with clasp); commander-in-chief of Bombay army, and second member of council of govt. of Bombay, March, 1890; resigned, April, 1893; ret'd., from the army, June, 1896.

GREAVES, RICHARD HERBERT, late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 19th Jan., 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; transfd. to Assam as district and sessions judge, Sylhet, Oct., 1887; ret'd., July, 1896.

GREEN, CHARLES ROBERT MORTIMER, Major, Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Mozufferarpur).—First commn., Sept., 1886; in mil. employ till Oct., 1891; civil surgn., Manbhum, July, 1893; ditto, Durbhanga, May, 1897.

GREEN, GEORGE EDWARD TEMPEST, Major, Indian Staff Corps (dep. commr., Burma).—Entered the service, Feb., 1882; on mil. duty till Dec., 1885, when he joined the Burma

commn. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1900.

GREENE, WALTER JAMES, Finl. Dept., India Office (clerk for non-effective accts.).—Writer, 1st July, 1867; clerk, and class, Aug., 1878; clerk for exam. of army non-effective accts., March, 1885.

GREENSHIELDS, ROBERT SCOTT, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Birkenhead Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 9th Dec., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to Assam, March, 1882, as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to the chief commr., March, 1886; offd. as dir. of land rev. and agric. in 1889; dep. commr., Nov., 1889; mag. and collr., Bengal, Nov., 1897.

GREENSTREET, WILLIAM LEES, Col., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Oct., 1864; arrived, Jan., 1870; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1871, as asst. engr., N.W. Prov.; transfd. to mil. works branch, 1872; exec. engr., Nov., 1874; supt. engr., July, 1885; took part in the Hazara expdn., 1891; chief engr., Sept., 1893; posted to Bombay command, April, 1895; returned to Europe, Jan., 1899.

GREER, RICHARD TOWNSEND, Indian C.S. (chairman of the corporation of Calcutta).—Educ. at Erasmus Coll., Drogheda, and Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 10th Dec., 1877, and served in Assam as asst. commr., and asst. sec. to chief commr.; transfd. to Bengal, Nov., 1888, as joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., March, 1893; insp.-gen. of police, March, 1900; chairman of the corporation of Calcutta, Oct., 1900; member, lieut. govr.'s council, Bengal, Jan., 1901.

GREENEN, RICHARD, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Dulwich and at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 1st Dec., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1891; asst. supt. and small cause judge, Dehra Dun, 1892-93; acted as under sec. to govt. in 1894, and from July, 1895; under sec. to govt., Dec., 1895; dep. commr., April, 1897; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1898; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., April, 1900; author of 'Heroes Five.'

GREGG, WILLIAM HENRY, M.B., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—First commn., 1869; served in the Lushai campaign, 1871-72 (medal and clasp); civil surgn., Dinajpur, 1873, Jessor, 1875, Hugli, 1876-86; lecturer on botany, Hugli coll., 1877-86; offg. protector of emigrants, Calcutta, April, 1888; sanitary commr., Bengal, Jan., 1889; civil surgn., Burdwan, April, 1896; ret'd., Dec., 1899.

GREGORY, GREGORY MARCAR, Opium Dept., Bengal (opium factory supt., Benares).—Entered the dept., April, 1873; head asst., April, 1879; sub. dep. opium agent, March, 1890; factory supt., Benares, March, 1899.

GREIG, GEORGE, late Forest Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived, 20th Sept., 1857, as a lieut., 93rd Sutherland highlanders; ret'd. from the army, June, 1865; joined the forest dept., Dec., 1868, and was apptd. dep. conservator, March, 1871, and conservator, Nov., 1876; ret'd., 1889.

GREY, LEOPOLD JOHN HERBERT, C.S.I., Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 3rd Jan., 1857; asst. commr. in Assam, Feb., 1865; offg.

civil officer with Bhutan Dwar field force, Dec., 1865; from April, 1866, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., but was employed under the govt. of Bengal, 1868-69; pol. agent, Bahawalpur state, 1871-73, and 1877; jud. asst., July, 1876; on special duty in connection with imperial assemblage, 1876-77; dep. commr., Nov., 1876; sec. to board of examrs. (under govt. of India), March, 1882; commr. and supt., Delhi divn., March, 1886; offd. as first finl. commr. in 1891; and as second in 1893; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1894.

GREY, WILLIAM FRANCIS HUNGERFORD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma commn.—First commn., Aug., 1868; served in Burma from April, 1874, as asst. commr., and offg. asst. sec.; boundary officer, Paungde, Aug., 1881; cantonmt. mag., and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, Sept., 1882; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; commr., Oct., 1898; ret'd., March, 1901.

GRIERSON, GEORGE ABRAHAM, C.I.E., Ph.D., Indian C.S. (special duty linguistic survey).—Educ. at St. Bees, Shrewsbury, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 25th Oct., 1873; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and small cause court judge; on famine duty, 1874; on special duty in the rev. dept., Dec., 1882; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1884; mag. and collr., March, 1890; opium agent, Bihar, Oct., 1896; on special duty with Linguistic Survey of India, May, 1898; C.I.E., May, 1894; hon. Ph.D. (Halle), Aug., 1894; has publ. various works in connection with the Behari language and its dialects, translns., collections of folk songs, &c.; also 'Curiosities of Indian Literature,' 1885; 'Vidyapati and his Contemporaries,' 1885; 'Modern Vernacular Literature of Hindustan, 1890; and 'Inscriptions of Piyadasi,' 1891.

GRIERSON, JOHN, Col., I.S.C., late India Public Works Dept.—2nd lieut., Bombay artillery, Dec., 1857; arrived, 24th Jan., 1858; joined the dept., Feb., 1864; exec. engr., Jan., 1871; from May, 1872, served in various parts of India as dep. controller of accts., dep. examr., and examr. of accts.; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1894.

GRIESBACH, CARL LUDOLF, C.I.E., F.G.S., Geological Survey of India (dir. of geol. survey).—Apptd. asst. supt., 26th Sept., 1878; arrived, 11th Nov., 1878; on special duty with troops in S. Afghanistan, 1880; present at the battle of Maiwand and during the siege of Kandahar (medal); employed on Afghan boundary commn., 1884-86; dep. supt., Nov., 1886; on depn. as geologist to the Amir of Kabul, 1888-89; supt., June, 1889; dir., July, 1894; author of numerous contributions to the survey publns.

GRIFFIN, HENRY DALY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at French Coll., Blackrock, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1889; joint mag., July, 1890; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1896.

GRIFFIN, SIR LEPEL HENRY, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 17th Nov., 1860, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Punjab, April, 1870; supt., Kapurthala state, April, 1875; dep. commr., April, 1877; sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1878; on special duty at the Paris exhibn.,

July, 1878; during the occupation of Afghanistan held the special appt. of chief pol. officer in north and west districts of that country (medal); rest. and agent to the govr.-gen., Central India, Feb., 1881; K.C.S.I., May, 1881; royal commr., Indian and colonial exhibn., 1886; ret'd., Jan., 1889; author of 'Punjab Chiefs,' 1865, 'The Law of Inheritance to Chiefs,' 1869, 'The Great Republic,' 1884, and 'Famous Monuments of Central India,' 1886.

GRIFFITH, HUGH, Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Burma.—Joined the Indian med. dept. (Madras establ.), 1859; apptd. civil surgn. and health officer, Rangoon, April, 1871; also supt. of lunatic asylum, June, 1871, and supt. of vaccn., May, 1875; ret'd., May, 1890.

GRIFFITH, RALPH THOMAS HOTCHKIN, C.I.E., M.A., late Educ. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived 1st Dec., 1853, and served as profr. and head master, Benares coll.; principal, Benares coll., Jan., 1861; supt. of observatory, Benares, May, 1866; inspr.-gen. of schs., N.W. Prov., March, 1878; dir. of public instrn., Feb., 1879; ret'd., 1885.

GRIFFITHS, WILLIAM, M.A., B.L., late Educ. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd., 1st July, 1869; arrived, 20th Jan., 1870, and served as asst. profr. and profr., presidency coll.; fellow of Calcutta univ., 1879; principal, Hugli coll., April, 1880; principal, presidency coll., Dec., 1892; ret'd., Sept., 1896.

GRIGG, EDWARD EVANS, Lieut.-Col., Bengal Infantry, late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, as ensign, Dec., 1861; served under the mil. dept. till Jan., 1864, and subsequently as asst. commr. in Assam up to March, 1867, when he was transf. to the Oudh commn. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1855; offg. commr., 1892, and from April, 1894; commr., Kumaon, Nov., 1895; returned to Europe, March, 1899.

GRIMES, HENRY ROBERT, late Jail Dept., Madras.—Asst. dep. commr. of paper currency, Vizagapatam, 29th Nov., 1864; from April, 1866, served as supt. of the central jail, Coimbatore; inspr.-gen. of jails, Madras, July, 1884; ret'd., April, 1889.

GRIMLEY, REGINALD FEARNS, Indian C.S. (spcl. asst. collr., Madras).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 9th Nov., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Cent. Prov., April, 1898; asst. commr., May, 1898; dep. commr., Jan., 1900; spcl. asst. collr., Tinnevelly, July, 1900.

GROVES, GUSTAVUS BARTON, Postal Dept., Govt. of India (dep. postmr.-gen. for Rajputana).—Entered the dept. May, 1869; apptd. dep. postmr.-gen. for Rajputana, Oct., 1894; author of 'The Post Office in British Burma.'

GROVES, HERBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Apptd., 23rd July, 1868, and posted to Mysore; exec. engr., Feb., 1876; transf. to Central India, Oct., 1882, and employed on state railways; transf. to Burma, May, 1887 (medal, with clasp); services lent to Mysore state, July, 1890; under sec. to govt. of Mysore, railway branch, July, 1891; joint sec., July, 1892; transf. to Burma, April, 1895; suptg. engr., Nov., 1895; to Madras, Oct., 1899; chief engr., and sec. to govt. of Burma, Aug., 1900.

GROWSE, EDWARD FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Charter-

house, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 18th Nov., 1881, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1880; mag. and collr., July, 1884; mag. of Cuttack, Dec., 1895.

GRUNDY, JAMES (inspr. of mines).—Apptd. inspr. of mines in India, Nov., 1893.

GRUNING, JOHN FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, Jan., 1899.

GRUZELIER, OCTAVIUS, India Office (2nd class clerk, pol. dept.).—Educ. at Devonport High School; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, Registry and Record Dept., May, 1900; transf. to pol. dept., Jan., 1902.

GUBBAY, MOSES MORDECAI SIMEON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Dec., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

GUBBINS, FREDERICK BEBB, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1835; served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr. of Benares, 1852; civil and sessions judge, Benares, 1857; commr. of rev. and circuit, Benares, agent, and supt. of ex-rajah of Coorg, 1858; ret'd., 1863.

GUITEMAN, CHARLES EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at City of London Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 20th Dec., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

GUN, WILLIAM HENRY MAFFETT, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Greenock Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 17th Nov., 1871, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1883; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1888; ret'd., June, 1897.

GUNGA RAM, Rai Bahadur, M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 2nd Oct., 1873, and posted to Punjab provl. estabt.; exec. engr., May, 1883.

GUNN, WILLIAM DONALD, Veterinary Major (supt., Hissar Cattle Farm).—Joined the (home) service, 13th July, 1878; supt., civil veterinary dept., Bengal, May, 1892; supt., Hissar cattle farm, April, 1899.

GUPTA, INANENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 12th Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1900.

GUPTA, KALI PADAM, M.A., M.B., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Medical Dept.—Joined the service, 13th June, 1872, and served as supt. of vaccn., and on gen. and jail duty; in 1888 acted in addn. as sanitary commr. for Bengal; civil surgn., July, 1891; ret'd., June, 1898.

GURDON, BERTRAND EVELYN MELLISH, C.I.E., D.S.O., Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Chitral).—First commn., 25th Aug., 1886; asst. pol. agent,

Gilgit, April, 1895; ditto, Chitral, Oct., 1895; C.I.E., May, 1900; pol. agent, April, 1901.

GURDON, EVELYN PULTENEY, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 2nd April, 1852; from Aug., 1860, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., dep. commr., and small cause court judge; addl. commr., Nov., 1881; commr. and supt., Aug., 1884; divl. judge, Oct., 1885; ret'd. from civil employment, Jan., 1890.

GURDON, PHILIP RICHARD THORNHAGH, Major, I.S.C. (dep. commr., Assam).—Joined the army, 2nd Sept., 1882; apptd. asst. commr. in Assam, 2nd June, 1886; dep. commr., Feb., 1895.

GÜTERSLOH, FREDERIC NICHOLAS, late Railway Branch, Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., June, 1874, as asst. engr. on the Indus valley state railway; manager, Wardha valley railway, Dec., 1878; locomotive supt., Rajputana railways, March, 1880; ret'd., June, 1891.

GWYTHER, FRANK EDWIN, Indian Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., and posted to Punjab irrigation, 31st May, 1881; asst. engr., Dec., 1881; exec. engr., Feb., 1895.

GWYTHER, HENRY THOMAS, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll., Roorkee; apptd. as apprentice engr., 21st March, 1874, and posted to Bengal; asst. engr., March, 1875; transf. to State Railways, Aug., 1875; exec. engr., Oct., 1889.

GWYTHER, WILLIAM BANKS, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Nov., 1876, and posted to Bengal; asst. engr., April, 1878; exec. engr., March, 1892.

HABIB-ULLAH, SAIVID, Maulvi, Statutory C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov., under the statutory rules, 14th Feb., 1887; joint mag., Sept., 1892; asst. commr., Nov., 1895; dep. commr., March, 1897.

HACKETT, ARTHUR LUKE, Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 1st April, 1867; arrived, 26th Oct., 1867; served under the mil. dept., and in various acting civil appts.; from Oct., 1878, served as civil surgn. at various stations, health officer and supt. of lock hosp., Madras, &c.; ret'd., May, 1895.

HAFIZ MUHAMMAD NAWAZ KHAN, BAHADUR, Lieut.-Colonel.—Apptd. British Agent with the Ameer of Afghanistan, April, 1900.

HAGGER, WILLIAM ROBERT, Veterinary Major (supt., civil veterinary dept., Burma).—Joined the (home) service, 13th July, 1878; supt., civil veterinary dept., Nov., 1898.

HAIG, CHARLES THOMAS, Major-Gen., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—Obtained his first commn. from Addiscombe, June, 1854; lieut., Bombay engrs., May, 1856; served with the Persian expdn., 1856-57 (medal and clasp), and with the Rajputana field force, 1857 (medal and clasp); asst. exec. engr. at Nimach, May, 1858; apptd. as asst. in the great trigonl. survey of India, Sept., 1859; served with the Okhamandal field force in 1859; dep. supt., Oct., 1867; on depn. as commr., Venice geographical exhibn., 1881; dep. surveyor-gen. in charge of trigonl. survey branch, Feb., 1887; ret'd. from civil em-

ployment, 1890; and from the army, April, 1891.

HAILEY, HAMMET REGINALD CLODE, B.A., Indian C.S. (settl. offr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 7th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., May, 1897; joint mag., June, 1899; settl. offr., Dec., 1899.

HAILEY, WILLIAM MALCOLM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., April, 1898, to Oct., 1899.

HAINES, SIR FREDERICK PAUL, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., Field-Marshal.—Entered the army as ensign in 1839; mil. sec. to Sir Hugh Gough in the war on the Sutlej, 1845; was present at the battles of Mudki and Firozshah (medal and clasp); served through the Punjab campaign of 1848 and 1849, and was present at Ramnagar, the passage of the Chenab, and the battles of Chilianwala and Gujrat; served with the 21st fusiliers through the campaign of the Crimea, 1854-55 (medal with four clasps, 5th class of the Medjidie and Turkish medal); K.C.B., 1871; G.C.B., 1877; commander-in-chief of the Madras army, and member of the govr.'s council, May, 1871, to 1875; commander-in-chief of the forces in India, with local rank of gen. and extraordinary member of the govr.-gen.'s council, April, 1876, to 1881, and directed the mil. operations of the Afghan campaigns of 1878-79 and 1879-80 (G.C. S.I.; thanked by both houses of parliament, 1879 and 1881).

HAINES, HERMANN ANDERSON, Pol. Dept., India Office (senr. clerk and reading clerk to council).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 15th Nov., 1881; pol. dept., 1886; précis writer, Dec., 1889, to Dec., 1894; senr. clerk, Feb., 1894; reading clerk to the Council of India, Aug., 1897.

HALL, ARTHUR ALEXANDER, M.A., Madras Educ. Service (princl., Teachers' Coll., Saidapet).—Apptd. principal, Teachers' Coll., Madras, Jan., 1887.

HALL, CHARLES FREDERICK, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Budaun, Feb., 1883; ret'd., Dec., 1886.

HALL, GEOFFREY CRAYTHORNE, Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (inspr.-gen. of prisons, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, as asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ.; served under the mil. dept. until March, 1875, when he entered civil employ as offg. civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Jhansi; supt., central prison, Allahabad, Nov., 1877; inspr.-gen. of prisons, July, 1896; author of three med. works.

HALL, MONTAGU STANLEY, Registry and Record Dept., India Office (staff clerk).—Writer, India office, 21st Jan., 1875; clerk, 2nd class, Oct., 1878; staff clerk, Jan., 1898.

HALL, WILLIAM THOMAS, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma).—Educ. at Rossall, Kingstown Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 5th Dec., 1878, and served in British Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dir. of land records and agric., Oct., 1887; on special duty to Perak tin mines in 1888-89; dep. commr., Nov., 1892; acting prest., Rangoon municipality, March, 1894; offg. commr. and sessions judge, April, 1896; commr., Mar., 1899.

HALLEN, JAMES HERBERT BROKENCOTE, C.I.E., F.R.C.S., Veterinary Lieut.-Col., late Civil Veterinary Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 23rd May, 1850; acted as principal veterinary surgn., Abyssinian field force, Dec., 1867; prest. of the Indian cattle plague commn., Nov., 1869; inspecting veterinary surgn., Bengal army, July, 1872; ditto, horse-breeding operations, May, 1875; inspr.-gen., civil veterinary dept. in India, April, 1892; C.I.E., June, 1893; author of 'Manual of the more deadly forms of Cattle Disease in India,' and the report of the cattle plague commn.; ret'd., May, 1894.

HALLETT, HENRY HONYWOOD HUGHES, Col., I. S. C., late Police Dept., Central Prov.—First commn., July, 1858; arrived, 11th Aug., 1858; served with the Bundelkhand field force, 1859; from 26th Dec., 1868, served in the Central Prov. as personal asst. to chief commr., asst. commr., &c., and was apptd. supt. of the sch. of industry and central jail, Jabalpur, Aug., 1878; also supt. of reformatory sch., June, 1891; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, Oct., 1894, to Aug., 1897.

HALLETT, JOHN REVANS, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Univ. of Melbourne; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 17th Oct., 1864; asst. mag. and collr., Bardwan, May, 1865; inspr. of schs., Aug., 1870; second inspr. of registrn. offices, March, 1876; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1878; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1880; re'd., Feb., 1890.

HALLIDAY, FREDERICK MYTTON, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 30th Oct., 1856, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr.; offd. as opium agent, Benares, in 1876 and 1877; commr., April, 1878; chairman, port commrs., Calcutta, April, 1887; offd. at various periods as addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council; member of board of rev., April, 1880; ret'd., Nov., 1891.

HALLIFAX, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Assam).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 28th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., Nov., 1892; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1898; mag. and collr., June, 1900.

HALLIFAX, CHARLES JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and All Souls' Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 31st Dec., 1888, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; councillor to the Raja of Suket, 1891-93; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Dec., 1896; dep. commr., April, 1898; sec. to plague commr., Oct., 1898.

HALLIFAX, HENRY FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford;

apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Sept., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settl. offr.

HAM, WILLIAM JAMES, late Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, and was attached to the sea post office, Bombay, 6th May, 1868; supernumerary inspr., Oct., 1877; dep. postmr.-gen., Central India, May, 1880, and at Calcutta, attached to the railway mail service, April, 1882; asst. dir.-gen., June, 1887; inspr.-gen., railway mail service, Sept., 1890; has acted as postmr.-gen. at Lucknow, Calcutta, Madras, N.W. Prov. and Oudh; confd. as postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1897; retd., April, 1901.

HAMBLIN, RICHARD EBB, M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and Christ Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 4th Jan., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., June, 1887; joint mag., March, 1890; dep. commr., April, 1893; mag. and collr., Sept., 1894; commr., Kumaon, March, 1899.

HAMILTON, FRANK SUMNER, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 10th Dec., 1885, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1892; mag. and collr., Oct., 1896; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1897.

HAMILTON, JOHN MONTGOMERY, India Office (second class clerk, store dept.).—Educ. at Ayr Acad. : apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 18th Nov., 1901.

HAMILTON, RIGHT HON. LORD GEORGE FRANCIS, M.P. (sec. of state for India).—Born Dec., 1845; educ. at Harrow; ensign, rifle brigade, 1864; transfed. to the Coldstream guards, 1868; was elected member for Middlesex, Dec., 1868, and represented that county in parliament until the election which followed the reform bill of 1885, when he was returned for the Ealing divn.; under sec. of state for India, Feb., 1874; vice-prest. of the committee of council on educn., April, 1878; first lord of the admiralty in the Marquis of Salisbury's first and second administrations, 1885-86, and 1886-92; chairman, London school board, 1894-95; sec. of state for India in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895.

HAMILTON, RICHARD ERRIS, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (acctnt.-gen., Burma).—Joined the dept., 1st Aug., 1868; served as asst. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., and British Burma, asst. comptr.-gen., and asst. commr. of paper currency; offg. asst. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., March, 1882; comptr. and dep. commr. of paper currency, Central Prov., Jan., 1883; comptr., India treasuries, Nov., 1891; acctnt.-gen., Punjab, April, 1894; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1896; acctnt.-gen., Burma, Nov., 1898.

HAMILTON, ROBERT CLIFTON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. on famine duty, Belgaum).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 1st Dec., 1893, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and sub-divl. officer; asst. mag. and collr., July, 1899; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1900; asst. collr. on famine duty, Belgaum, April, 1901.

HAMILTON, THOMAS SHARP, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Old Trafford Sch., Manchester, Chester Coll., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 6th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge, Nov., 1887; judge and sessions judge, April, 1890; retd., Jan., 1897.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM ROBERTS, B.L., late Police Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1869; asst. to the rev. and police commr., Oct., 1869; dep. collr., May, 1874; city mag., Sept., 1874; Huzur dep. commr., July, 1876; presidency mag., Jan., 1888 to 1899; author of a police manual, and works on local self-govt. and village customs.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM STIRLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab, April, 1897; senr. sec., April, 1898, to Nov., 1900.

HAMMICK, MURRAY, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (inspr.-gen. of police, Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 18th Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and in Coorg as asst. commr. and mag.; from Jan., 1883, served in the Madras secretariat as asst. sec., jud. and legisl. depts. and acting under sec. in the rev. dept.; head asst., S. Kanara, April, 1885; acting postmr.-gen., Madras, 1886; sec. to the commrs. of land rev., Oct., 1887; collr. and mag., Malabar, Nov., 1893; inspr.-gen. of Madras police, Dec., 1894; C.I.E., Nov., 1901.

HAMMICK, STEPHEN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and tutor to the Maharaja of Kolhapur; on famine duty, Kaladgi, Sept., 1877; jud. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, Aug., 1883; judge and sessions judge, Shikarpur, Feb., 1885; member of Deccan agriculturists' relief act commn., 1891-92; retd., June, 1894.

HAMMOND, ALFRED JOHN (dist. supt. of Police, Punjab).—Entered the service as asst. dist. supt. of police, July, 1870; dist. supt., July, 1880.

HAMMOND, EGBERT LAURIE LUCAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Newton Coll., S. Devon, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 27th Nov., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HAMNETT, FREDERICK HARPER, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, March, 1885; acting sub-collr., Godavari, April, 1886; head asst., Nov., 1887; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1891; district and sessions judge, Feb., 1895; coll. and mag., Oct., 1899; dist. and sess. judge, Godavari, April, 1901.

HAMNETT, GEORGE, C.I.E., late Registrn. Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 11th March, 1843; uncov. asst. to the consulting engr., Jan., 1859; marriage registrar for town of Madras, Jan., 1866; first uncov. asst., chief sec.'s office, Feb., 1867; receiver of the Carnatic property, Feb., 1875; inspr.-gen. of registrn.,

Nov., 1880; C.I.E., June, 1885; was also registrar-gen. for Mysore, including Bangalore; retd., Sept., 1889; member of legisl. council, 1890-92.

HANCE, JOHN EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 4th Dec., 1900, and served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr.

HANCOCK, GEORGE EDWARD, Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 13th Nov., 1859, and served under the mil. dept.; from Nov., 1863, served as asst. supt. of rev. survey and assessment, Gujarat; acting supt. of the Rajkot and Limbdi states, and acting asst. rest. at Aden, and at Baroda; asst. gen. supt. for thagi and dakaiti, Oct., 1871; asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1872; from Oct., 1873, served as supt. of surveys under rajasthanik court, Kathiawar; acting joint administrator of Gondal state, 1880-81, and was on special duty with the Thakur Saheb in Europe, 1883; on special duty to inquire into administration of Rajpipla state, Nov., 1883; third asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1885, continuing to be supt. of surveys; prest. of the rajasthanik court, Jan., 1886; pol. supt., Savantvadi, May-Oct., 1891; pol. agent, &c., Kolhapur; offd. in Kathiawar, Jan., 1894; and again from March, 1895; returned to Europe, May, 1896.

HANDCOCK, WILLIAM ERSKINE FRASER, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1894.

HANDLEY, ALFRED PERCIVAL, B.L., Jud. Dept., Bengal (chief judge, Calcutta small cause court).—Joined the service, 14th March, 1890, as offg. chief presidency mag. and judge of the court for trial of pilots, Calcutta, and served until Oct., 1891; from Feb., 1892, acted as presidency mag., and was apptd. judge of Calcutta small cause court, May, 1892; chief judge, Oct., 1893; acting judge, high court, May, 1899, and March, 1900.

HANDLEY, FRANCIS FREDERICK, B.L., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Sydenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 7th Oct., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; senr. marriage registrar, Calcutta, Dec., 1880; first inspr. of registrn. offices, Dec., 1881; acting inspr.-gen. of registrn., April, 1885; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1885; district and sessions judge, April, 1889; retd., April, 1900.

HANKEY, HERBERT, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Harrow, and Haileybury; apptd. in 1856; served in the Punjab till 1862, and subsequently in the Lower Prov., and was apptd. mag. and collr., Maldah, 1867, and subsequently at Chittagong; supt. of transport during famine of 1874; inspr.-gen. of police, Lower Prov., 1876; retd., 1878.

HANKIN, EDWARD LEWIS, Lieut.-Gen., I.S.C., late Madras Police Dept.—Ensign, Dec., 1847; arrived, 16th June, 1848; served in the public works dept., Madras, from Aug., 1853, to July, 1859; served subsequently in the police dept. as supt. of police; acting mag. of police for town of Madras, March, 1870; joint mag., Utakamand, Nov., 1870; supt. of family payments and pensions, Oct., 1876; sec. to govt., mil. dept., Madras, Dec., 1882; returned to Europe, 1888.

HANKIN, ERNEST HANBURY, M.A. (chemical exammr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Joined the service, 26th Oct., 1892, as chemical exammr., analyst, and bacteriologist.

HANNAGAN, JOHN SALISBURY, late Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1859, and held various non-gazetted appts. till May, 1880, when he was apptd. sub-judge at Lucknow; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1892; served through the Mutiny (medal); retd., 1896.

HANNAN, JOHN, late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 31st July, 1866, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Dec., 1891; offg. joint sec. to govt., public works dept., April-Nov., 1893; retd., Dec., 1900.

HANNAY, ALEXANDER LIDDERDALE, Indian C.S. (regr., high court, Madras).—Educ. at Uppingham, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 5th Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; regr. of high court of judicature, Feb., 1898.

HANNYNGTON, FRANK, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 30th Jan., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HAPPELL, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Crosshill Sch., Maybole, Glasgow Univ., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 25th Nov., 1864, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; acting commr. of salt rev., Madras, 1881; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1883; collr. and mag., Nov., 1883; retd., Jan., 1892.

HARCOURT, ALFRED FREDERICK POLLOCK, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 15th Oct., 1855; from Sept., 1862, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., March, 1877; dep. commr., April, 1877; retd., March, 1880; author of 'Down by the Drawl,' 'The Royal Umbrella,' and 'Kulu, Lahoul, and Spitti.'

HARCOURT, HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HARDIE, ROBERT, late member of the Council of India.—Apptd. to the council of the sec. of state for India on 17th March, 1887, for ten years; vice-prest., 1892-93; was previously engaged in the profession of banking, and held the office of sec. and treasurer of the Bank of Bengal for 15 years; in 1886 was a member of the finance committee apptd. by the govt. of India for the reduction of expenditure.

HARDIMAN, JOHN THOMAS PERCY, Indian C.S. (asst. acct.-gen., Bombay).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 10th Dec., 1897, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. acct.-gen., Bombay, Aug., 1901.

HARDING, FRANCIS HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1885; district and sessions judge, May, 1890; retd., June, 1900.

HARDING, HERBERT OLIVE DENMAN, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. to mag. and collr.; head asst. collr., Coimbatore, Feb., 1893; special asst., Malabar, Dec., 1894; acting dist. and sess. judge, July, 1897; sub.-collr. and joint mag., March, 1898.

HARDING, HERBERT WILLIAM (senr. clerk, India Audit Office).—Associate of King's Coll., London; writer, India office, 27th April, 1868; junr. examr., India audit office, Nov., 1873; designation changed to junr. clerk, Oct., 1892; senr. clerk, Jan., 1895.

HARDWICKE (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. ALBERT EDWARD YORKE (parly. under sec. of state for India).—Born in 1867; educ. at Eton Coll.; attaché at Vienna, 1886-1891; parly. under sec. of state for India, Nov., 1900.

HARDY, RICHARD GILLIES, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Witney Gr. Sch., and Victoria Coll., Jersey; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; mag. and collr., July, 1886; offd. as commr., 1889, 1895, and from April, 1896; commr., Oct., 1896; settl. commr., Lucknow, Oct., 1898; addl. member viceroy's legisl. council, Dec., 1899, and Dec., 1901; C.S.I., Jan., 1900; acting chief sec. to govt., May, 1900.

HARE, LANCELOT, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint, and City of London Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 5th Nov., 1873; served in Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; on famine duty, July, 1874; asst. sec. to chief commr. of Assam, March, 1879, and offg. sec., March, 1880; offg. inspr.-gen. of police, Assam, July, 1880; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1884; mag. and collr., June, 1890; offg. commr., April, 1896; C.I.E., Jan., 1900; addl. commr., Mar., 1900; commr., Patna, Nov., 1900.

HARE, ROBERT DOUGLAS, Haidarabad admin. (dep. commr.).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr., Chota Nagpur, 14th June, 1869; served on famine duty, May, 1874, and in Berar from Oct., 1875; asst. commr., May, 1878; judge of small cause court, June, 1883; dep. commr., July, 1889; dir. of land records and agric., July, 1890, to Aug., 1891.

HARE, WILLIAM HARRIS MACVICAR, late Indian Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. 9th Nov., 1870, as asst. supt.; supt., Oct., 1887; ret., Mar., 1898.

HARI KISHEN KAUL, Pandit, M.A. (settlt. collr., Punjab commn.).—Probr. under native civil service rules, 17th April, 1890; extra asst. commr., April, 1892; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., Dec., 1895; settlt. collr., Aug., 1898.

HARINGTON, ARTHUR HENRY, M.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862; served in Oudh from Aug., 1863, as asst. commr. and junr. sec. to chief commr.; from Dec., 1866, to Jan., 1868, served under the govt. of India as offg. asst. sec., foreign dept., and offg. under sec., home dept.; offg. supt. of excise and stamps, Oudh, July, 1868; offg. settlt. officer, Hardoi, March, 1870; manager of encumbered estates, Faizabad

and Gonda, 1872-75; dep. commr., Aug., 1879; mag. and collr., Saharanpur, Oct., 1883; commr. in Oudh, June, 1889; commr., Allahabad, Nov., 1889; Meerut, May, 1891; ret'd., June, 1896; author of 'Economic Reform in Rural India.'

HARINGTON, HERBERT BARING, M.A., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, 2nd Feb., 1857, as asst. in telegraph dept.; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); from Feb., 1860, served in the Oudh commn. as asst. commr., and asst. settlt. officer; offg. sec. to chief commr., 1869-73; also offg. dir. of public instrn., 1870; dep. commr., July, 1875; district judge, Lucknow, 1879-81; ret'd., Nov., 1887.

HARINGTON, HERBERT SEPTIMUS, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suprg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; engr.-in-chief, Mari-Attock railway, April, 1893; suptg. engr., Jan., 1898.

HARINGTON, RICHARD, B.L. (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Apptd. judge, Calcutta high court, July, 1899.

HARMAN, JOHN MACDONALD, India Public Works Dept. (suprg. engr.).—Trained at the Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 29th May, 1872, and employed on mil. works in the N.W. Prov.; transfld. to state railways, Jan., 1880; exec. engr., May, 1883; offg. engr.-in-chief, N.W. railway (frontier section), April, 1895; Burma State railway, April, 1896; suptg. engr., Nov., 1899.

HARRIDANCE, HENRY, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office (2nd class clerk).—Clerk, 2nd class, 8th March, 1878.

HARRIOTT, GEORGE MOSS, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Oct., 1878, and posted to Cent. Provs.; asst. engr., May, 1879; exec. engr., June, 1891; C.I.E., Jan., 1900.

HARRIS, EDWARD BRANSON, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Commercial Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 14th Dec., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1888; mag. and collr., June, 1892.

HARRIS, (BARON), RIGHT HON. GEORGE ROBERT CANNING, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.,—Educ. at Eton, and at Christ Church, Oxford, where he graduated B.A. in 1874; is J. P. and D. L. for Kent, and dep. chairman of the East Kent quarter sessions; under sec. for India in Lord Salisbury's first administration, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; under sec. of state for war, Aug., 1886; govr. of Bombay, 12th April, 1890, to 18th Feb., 1895; G.C.I.E., May, 1890; G.C.S.I., May, 1895; apptd. a lord-in-waiting to Her late Majesty, July, 1895.

HARRIS, GEORGE FRANCIS ANGELS, Lieut.-Col., Indian Medl. Service (profr., Calcutta Medl. Coll. and medl. inspr. of emigrants).—1st commn., 30th Sept., 1878; civil surgeon at various stations, May, 1879-Feb., 1900; profr., Calcutta Medl. Coll., Aug., 1900.

HARRIS, GEORGE STEEL TRAVERS, India Public Works Dept. (suprg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Nov., 1881; lent to Rewa state, Dec., 1883, and to Gwalior, 1886-94; consulting architect, Madras, Oct., 1896.

HARRIS, HENRY NEVILLE, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 3rd April, 1866, as asst. supt. of police; employed in Assam in 1870; district supt., Feb., 1874; dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1899; ret'd., Aug., 1901.

HARRIS, LEONARD TATHAM, Indian C.S. (dist. mag. and collr., Madras).—Educ. at Falmouth Gr. Sch., Bath Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 10th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; dist. mag. and collr., Bangalore, May, 1899.

HARRIS, ROBERT LISTON, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 1st Dec., 1877, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; depd. to settlt. dept., 1883-84; district judge, Nov., 1889; divl. judge, July, 1894.

HARRISON, CHARLES HOLMES, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Nov., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HARRISON, CHARLES WILLIAM INGLEBY, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 11th June, 1858; arrived, 9th Sept., 1860; served in the public works dept., irrig. branch, N.W. Prov., from 10th April, 1863, as asst. engr., exec. engr., and asst. to chief engr.; suptg. engr., Dec., 1881; offd. as joint sec. to govt., Bengal, July 1886, and as sec., April-June, 1888; chief engr., Dec., 1889; joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan., 1890; sec., July, 1891; reverted to mil. dept., June, 1894; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

HARRISON, FRANCIS CAPEL, Indian C.S. (acct.-genl., N.W. Provs. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 27th Nov., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, May, 1889; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Sept., 1890; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1892; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1892; acctnt.-gen., Madras, Nov., 1897; acctnt.-gen. N.W. Provs. and Oudh, Oct., 1899.

HARRISON, GEORGE McCULLOCH, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; entered the service, 1st Oct., 1882, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Dec., 1896.

HARRISON, HENRY ANTHONY, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1855; arrived, 3rd April, 1855; served from May, 1856, to Sept., 1858, in Bengal, as asst. mag. and collr., Cuttack, and subsequently in the N.W. Prov.; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, April, 1862; asst., settlt. dept., Feb., 1864; mag. and collr., March, 1872; offg. commr., Jhansi divn., Sept., 1875; civil and sessions judge, March, 1878; ret'd., June, 1887.

HARRISON, MICHAEL HERMANN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Radley Sch. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HARRISON, PERCY, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and settlt. officer, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and

collr.; under sec., March, 1894; joint mag. and settlt. officer, May, 1897.

HARRISON, WILLIAM (2nd class clerk, India Store Dépôt).—Clerk, 2nd class, 21st Oct., 1897.

HARRISON, WILLIAM PRINGLE, Lieut.-Col., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived as ensign, 10th Feb., 1861; employed under the mil. dept. till Oct., 1867, from which date he served in the Oudh commn. as asst. commr., offg. district supt. of police and supt. of encumbered estates in Sultanpur; sub-judge, Partabgarh, Aug., 1879; cantonmt. mag., July, 1881; dep. commr., Feb., 1888; ret'd. from civil employ, May, 1898.

HART, GEORGE HENRY REEVES, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (comptr., Indian treasuries).—Probr. in finl. dept., 20th July, 1869; asst. to comptr.-gen., Dec., 1870; asst. to private sec. to viceroy, 1874-76; asst. to acctnt.-gen., Bombay, 1877; private sec. to govt. of Bombay, March, 1880; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Aug., 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Lahore, Jan., 1889; Bombay, Feb., 1891; acted as comptr., India treasuries, Aug., 1893, and as asst. sec., fin. and commerce dept.; confd. as comptr. Indian treasuries, April, 1898.

HART, JOHN HENRY EUSTACE, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 23rd Nov., 1854; joined the dept., Jan., 1855, and served as civil engr., acting exec. engr. for reclamations, Bombay, architectural exec. engr. and surveyor, Bombay, and acting supt., harbour works, Karachi; acting chief engr. for irrig., Sept., 1877; suptg. engr., March, 1880; joint sec. for irrig., March, 1880; chief engr., Dec., 1885; sec., public works and irrig. dept., Sept., 1887; ret'd., 1888.

HART, SIDNEY GEORGE, Indian C.S. (sub.-divl. offr., Assam).—Educ. at King's Sch., Rochester, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 4th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., June, 1897; sub. divl. offr., May, 1898.

HART, WILLIAM, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. in 1834; served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge and sessions judge; commr. for enquiring into claims, &c., S. Mahratta country, 1847; sec. to govt., gen. and ecclesiast. depts., 1855, also in the public works dept., and railway branch, 1856; puisne judge of the court of sadar diwani, 1860; rev. and police commr., S. divn., 1862; member of Bombay legisl. council, 1864; ret'd. in 1865.

HART-DAVIES, THOMAS, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Marlborough; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from May, 1876, served in Sind as educ. inspr. and manager of encumbered estates; 1st asst. collr., April, 1887; judge and sessions judge, June, 1891; acted in May, 1895, as jud. commr. in Sind; ret'd., Feb., 1897.

HARTNOLL, HENRY SULLIVAN, B.L., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Exeter Gr. Sch. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1883, and served in Burma as asst. commr. (war medal and clasp); dep. commr., July, 1890.

HARTT, WILLIAM EDWARD, India Public Works Dept. (district traffic supt.).—Joined the service, 10th April, 1870; asst. traffic supt., state railways, 1878; district traffic supt., 1882.

HARTWELL, SYDNEY CHARLES ELPHINSTONE, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. traffic supt., Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, 1st Jan., 1889; offg. manager, 1891-92, and from April, 1895; ret'd., 1898.

HARVEY, EDWARD, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 22nd Dec., 1859; served in the dept. from April, 1864; exec. engr., mil. works branch, June, 1871; served in the Lushai expedn., 1871-72 (medal and clasp), in the Jowaki Afridi campaign, 1878-79 (clasp), and in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); suptg. engr., May, 1889; ret'd., Oct., 1892.

HARVEY, JOHN, Educl. Dept. (inspr. of schools, Punjab).—Served as asst. and head master of various schs. from 1st Nov., 1869; asst. inspr. of schs., April, 1877; tutor to the Raja of Chamba, Nov., 1879; inspr. of schs., Nov., 1883.

HARVEY, ROBERT, C.B., D.S.O., M.D., Surgn.-Gen., late Ind. Mcd. Service.—First commn., 31st March, 1865; from 1867 served as civil surgn., offg. sanitary commr., Bengal med. inspr. of emigrants, and supt. of emigration; profr. of midwifery, med. coll., and obstetric physician to the coll. hosp., Sept., 1882; hon. surgn. on personal staff of the viceroy, May, 1883; reverted to mil. dept., March, 1890; inspr.-gen. of civil hosps., Bengal, May, 1894; principal med. officer, Punjab, April, 1895; offg. dir.-gen., Indian Med. Service, Feb.-Oct., 1898; confd., Oct., 1898; C.B., Dec., 1898; died, Dec., 1901.

HARVEY, WILLIAM LEATHEM, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (municipal commr., Bombay).—Educ. at the Royal Acad. Instn., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 28th Dec., 1883, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; under sec. to govt., rev., finl. and gen. depts., Aug., 1891; 1st asst., Aug., 1894; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, July, 1895; municipal commr., Bombay, Dec., 1898; C.I.E., Nov., 1901.

HARWARD, ALFRED EDGAR, Indian C.S. (dist. and sessions judge, Assam).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 12th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1892; transfld. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1894; district and sessions judge, July, 1896.

HASHMAT-ULLA, Maulvi, M.A., Statutory C.S. (joint. mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. a prob. under the native civil service rules, Mar., 1886; confd., 13th June, 1889, as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1894; asst. commr., Jan., 1897.

HASTED, JOHN ORD, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—2nd lieut., Madras engrs., Dec., 1853; served in the dept. from 30th Jan., 1856, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1865, and suptg. engr., Feb., 1877; chief engr. for irrign. and joint sec. to govt., April, 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1883; sec., gen. and buildings and road branches, 1886; ret'd., July, 1890.

HASTINGS, CHARLES GODOLPHIN WILLIAM, C.I.E. (inspr.-genl. of police, N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Entered the service as asst. dist. supt., Sept., 1873; dist. supt., Dec., 1882; on special duty in Kabul, Feb.-Oct., 1880; with Chitral relief force, June-Sept., 1895; special duty in Kurram from Mar., 1896; C.I.E., June, 1897;

dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1901; inspr.-genl. of police, N.W. Frontier Prov., 1901.

HASTINGS, FRANCIS THEOPHILUS CLINTON, India Office (asst. private sec. to sec. of state for India).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll.; admiralty writer, 8th Oct., 1868; civil service copyist, Jan., 1874; clerk specially attached to the pol. and secret dept., India office, 7th March, 1877; rest. clerk, July, 1888; asst. private sec. to the sec. of state, June, 1892; précis writer, Nov., 1896.

HASTINGS, WARREN, Lieut.-Col. I.S.C. Haidarabad Admn. (inspr.-gen. of police).—Educ. at Brighton Coll.; first commn., 28th Feb., 1874; served in Bengal as cantonmt. mag. and small cause court judge from Jan., 1881; special asst. commr., Berar, Sept., 1887; dep. commr., July, 1891; in charge of provl. census operations, 1890-92; insp.-gen. of police, &c., Oct., 1895.

HATCH, FREDERICK HENRY, Ph.D., A.M.I.C.E., F.G.S., Geological Survey of India (gold mining expert).—Appointed gold mining expert to the Government of India, Mar., 1900.

HATCH, GEORGE WASHINGTON, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acting under sec., rev. and finl. depts., Jan., 1899; 2nd asst., Aug., 1900.

HATCH, HENRY FREDERICK, late Bombay Survey Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1873, and served as asst. supt.; supt. of surveys and asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, April, 1896; ret'd., Aug., 1899.

HATCH, WILLIAM KEITH, M.B., C.M., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (principal, Grant Med. Coll., Bombay).—Arrived, 10th Nov., 1877; second surgn. of the Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., and profr. of anatomy, &c., Grant med. coll., May, 1884; principal, Grant med. coll., senr. med. officer and senr. surgn. of the hosp., and profr. of surgery at the coll., April, 1893.

HATCHELL, CHARLES, Surgn.-Major, late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 23rd July, 1858; served in N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn. from Dec., 1859, to Oct., 1864, and subsequently under the mil. dept.; took part in the Bhutan expedn.; served again under govt. of N.W. Prov. from Nov., 1869, as civil asst. surgn., jail supt., and civil surgn.; ret'd., Oct., 1888.

HATCHELL, DAVID GEORGE, Revenue Survey Dept., Madras (superintendent).—Joined the service, 14th Sept., 1885; asst. supt., Feb., 1887; dep. supt., Feb., 1899; supt., Jan., 1901.

HATHERLY, JOHN RODNEY, late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as asst. engr., and was employed on various irrign. works; exec. engr., Feb., 1876; ret'd., Nov., 1899.

HATHORNTWAITE, JAMES THOMAS, M.A., late Educl. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 3rd Oct., 1872, and served from that date as profr. of mathematics at the Elphinstone coll., Bombay; also principal of the coll., April, 1894; ret'd., June, 1900.

HATTEN, JOHN JAMES, B.A., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at

Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 12th Oct., 1875, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883.

HAVELL, ERNEST BINFIELD, Bengal Educ. Dept.—Apptd. supt. of the Calcutta school of art and art gallery, July, 1896.

HAVELOCK, SIR ARTHUR ELIBANK, G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., late Govr. of Madras.—Entered the army, Jan., 1862; private sec. and A.D.C. to the officer administering the govt. of Mauritius, 1873-74; acting chief civil commr., Seychelles Islands, 1874-75; member of council, Fiji, and for a time colonial sec. and receiver-gen., 1875; prest. of Nevus, 1877; administering the govt. of St. Lucia, 1878-79; chief civil commr., Seychelles, 1879; govr. of W. Africa settlts., 1881, and assisted in Anglo-French negotiations at Paris regarding that territory; consul for Liberia, 1881; K.C.M.G., 1884; govr. of Trinidad, 1884; govr. of Natal and special commr. for Zulu affairs, 1885; also govr. of Zululand, 1887; attended Brussels conference on the suppression of the slave trade, 1889; govr. of Ceylon, 1890; G.C.M.G., 1895; govr. of Madras, March, 1896, to Dec., 1900; G.C.I.E., March, 1896.

HAWKINS, ROBERT WILLIAM LYONS, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 3rd Oct., 1873, and employed on irrig. works in N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1884.

HAY, GEORGE WILLIAM ROBERTSON, M.D., Surg. Gen., Indian Med. Service (principal med. officer, Madras).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1868; arrived, 24th Oct., 1868; served in the mil., civil, and jail branches of the service, and was apptd. port surg. at Aden, June, 1876; sec. to the principal med. officer, Poona, Oct., 1890; principal med. officer, Bombay and Aden dist., Aug., 1897; principal med. officer, Madras, April, 1900.

HAYDON, WILLIAM HENRY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1862; arrived, 1st March, 1865; exec. engr., Jan., 1870; employed on Bombay reclamation works and defences, Tapti bridge, and with the Kandahar field force; employed under mil. works dept., 1890-91; ret., Dec., 1894.

HAYWARD, MAURICE HENRY WESTON, LL.B., Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Aug. 1895; jud. asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1896; acting dist. judge, Karachi, Nov., 1899; confd., June, 1900.

HAZLETT, HENRY JAMES, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.—Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1867, and was employed on mil. and jail duty; analyst to salt commn., Madras, 1876; surgn., 4th district, Jan., 1887, to May, 1888; also profir. in med. coll., Aug., 1887; employed in mil. accts. dept. of govt. of India, 1888 and 1889; surgn., 3rd district, Feb., 1893; ret., July, 1897.

HEAD, EDGAR ALFRED (second-class clerk, Store Dept., India Office).—Educ. at King Edward's Gr. Sch., Bath; clerk, 2nd class, 30th May, 1895.

HEADLEY, ROBERT HOLLOWELL, LL.B. (second-class clerk, Military Dept., India Office).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., London; admitted to the Middle Temple, 1900; Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 9th March, 1896; LL.B. (honours) Lond. Univ., 1901; transfid. to mil. dept., Nov., 1901.

HEALD, BENJAMIN HERBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Nottingham High Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

HEANEY, JOHN SAMUEL, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at the Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 4th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transfid. to Bombay on famine duty, July, 1900; 1st asst., May, 1901.

HEARLE, NATHANIEL, late Indian Forest Dept., Cent. Provs.—Joined the service, Dec., 1878, as asst. conservr., dep. conservator, July, 1886; ret., Sept., 1901.

HEATON, JOSEPH JOHN, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 24th Dec., 1881, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and acting under sec.; asst. judge and sessions judge, July 1889; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, July, 1895; judge and sess. judge, July, 1898.

HEAVISIDE, WILLIAM JAMES, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—Joined Addiscombe, Aug., 1857; lieut., Bengal engrs., June, 1859; served in the public works dept. as asst. engr. from Sept., 1862; asst. field engr. with the Bhutan force, 1865-66 (medal and clasp); joined the great trigonl. survey, Jan. 1866; dep. supt., April, 1872; served in Afghanistan, 1878-79 (medal), and subsequently in Khandesh and Bombay native states; ret., July, 1883.

HEBBERT, FREDERICK BENBOW, M.C.I.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874; exec. engr., Sept., 1883; offg. dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lucknow, April, 1887; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., March, 1888; under sec. and dep. dir.-gen. of railways, May, 1892; offg. manager, Burma state railway, Jan.-May, 1896; suptg. engr., Nov., 1896; chief engr., Jan., 1901.

HEINIG, JOSEPH, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 26th July, 1867, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Oct., 1877; suptg. engr., Oct., 1895; ret., June, 1901.

HĒMANGA CHANDRA BOSE, Bengal Judi. Dept. (subordinate judge).—Joined the service as munsif, 14th April, 1872; sub. judge, July, 1887.

HEMING, DEMPSTER, Lieut.-Col., late Police Dept., Madras.—First commn., 16th Nov., 1861; proby. asst. supt. of police, Dec., 1865; dep. commr., Oct., 1874; asst. with the govt. of Madras, 1888, and 1890; supt. of police, Jan., 1892; ret., Feb., 1894.

HEMINGWAY, FREDERICK RICKETTS, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HEMINGWAY, HENRY EDMUND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr.

HEMINGWAY, PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Oundle, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 14th Dec., 1896, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

HENDERSON, CLAUDE GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 24th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

HENDERSON, EDWARD PIERCY, B.L., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and St. Peter's Coll., Radley; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 10th Oct., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; govt. advocate, Feb., 1878; ret'd., Sept., 1897.

HENDERSON, GEORGE, M.D., Surgn.-Major, late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the Indian med. dept. in 1859; civil asst. surgn., Shahpur, May, 1862; supt. of Lahore central jail, April, 1868; offg. supt., botanical gardens in Bengal, Dec., 1872; civil surgn., Gujranwala, Dec., 1875, and subsequently at other stations in the Punjab; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

HENDERSON, HARRY JOSEPH HUME, Bombay Police Dept. (dist. supt. of police).—Joined the service, May, 1872, as asst. supt. of police; supt., Nov., 1878; dist. supt., Dec., 1898.

HENDERSON, JAMES PERCY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Feb., 1891.

HENDERSON, PHILIP DURHAM, C.S.I., Maj.-Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras cavalry, Feb., 1857; attaché, foreign dept., May, 1871; under sec. to the govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1872; on special duty in Kashmir, 1874; pol. officer on staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Sept., 1875; C.S.I., March, 1876; gen. supt. of operations for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, March, 1878; rest. in Mysore, and chief commr., Coorg, April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Feb., 1895.

HENDERSON, WILLIAM GEORGE HUME, Lieut.-Col., Indian M.S. (presy. surgn., Bombay).—Joined the service, 21st Nov., 1876; in mil. employ till April, 1878, since when has held several civil surgeries; was on field service in Upper Burma, 1886-88; presy. surgn., April, 1901.

HENDERSON, WILLIAM SCOTT, India Store Dépôt (and class clerk).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1900.

HENDLEY, THOMAS HOLBEIN, C.I.E., Col., Indian Med. Service (inspr.-gen. of civil hospls., Bengal).—First commn., Oct., 1869; apptd. inspr.-gen. of civil hospls., Calcutta, April, 1898.

HENNESSY, SEPTIMUS HOPE, late Jud. Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the service as acting extra asst. commr., 13th July, 1864, and was confd., 8th July, 1867; judge, small cause court, Sept., 1889; acted also as cantonmt. mag. from Nov., 1888; ret'd., Jan., 1892.

HENNIKER, FREDERICK CHANDOS, Indian C.S. (dir. of land records and agric., Assam).—

Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 26th Nov., 1887, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1897; dir. of land records and agriculture, Dec., 1898.

HENRIQUES, QUENTIN QUIXANO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HENRY, EDWARD RICHARD, C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at St. Edmund's Coll., Ware, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, June, 1882; on famine duty, Tirhut, 1873; private sec. to lieut.-govr. in 1879 and 1880; mag. and collr., May, 1890; offg. sec. to board of rev., 1890-91; inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1892; C.S.I., May, 1893; commr., Mar., 1900; ret'd., March, 1901.

HENSLEY, JOHN WILLIAM, India Telegraph Dept. (supt. of telegraphs).—Apptd. asst. supt., Jan., 1879; supt., April, 1888; served with Chin-Lushai expdn., 1889-90 (medal and clasp).

HENSLOWE, CECIL WILLIAM EDWARD, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1866, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, and in the Central Prov.; transfed. to railway establt., Jan., 1880; engr.-in-chief and manager, Cawnpore-Farukhabad and Muttra-Hathras railways, Dec., 1880; manager, Cawnpore-Achnera state railway, 1884; transfed. to Burma state railway, Dec., 1886; ret'd., 1890.

HENSLOWE, FRANCIS JOHN DELORAINE, I.S.C. (comdt., Zohb levy corps).—First commn., Aug., 1888; joined the staff corps, Oct., 1889; apptd. comdt., Zohb levy corps, May, 1900.

HENVEY, FREDERICK, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., under sec. to govt., offg. sec. to board of rev., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on special duty in 1871 for purpose of compiling a narrative of the famine of 1869-70; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., July, 1874; offg. rest., Nepal, 1877-78; on special duty, Kashmir, April, 1878; commr., Haidarabad assigned districts, Jan., 1883; rest., E. Rajputana states, Dec., 1885; agent to the govr.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1889; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

HERALD, JOHN LUMGAIR, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Arbroath High Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 24th Dec., 1883; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1894; mag. and collr., Aug., 1895; dep. commr., March, 1896.

HERBERT, CHARLES, Lt.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resdt. Gwalior).—First commn., June, 1874; served under the mil. dept. to 1882, including Afghan war of 1879-80; extra A.D.C. to viceroy, 1878 and 1882; offd. as asst. to the govr.-gen., Rajputana, June, 1882; mag. of Abu, Nov., 1882; on depn. to Mewar states to settle boundary disputes, 1883; offg. cantonmt. mag., Nairabad, 1884 and 1885-86; asst. commr., Ajmir,

April, 1889; asst. to the agent, Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmir-Merwara, March, 1891; offg. pol. agent in E. states of Rajputana, 1891-92; 1st asst. to agent, Central India, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Kotah, March, 1894; ditto, Eastern States of Rajputana, 1900; resdt., Gwalior, Feb., 1901.

HERBERT, DAVID WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1880; exec. engr. Dec., 1893.

HERBERT, DOUGLAS, Capt., Indian S.C. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Tunbridge Sen.; 1st commn., 25th Nov., 1885; joined the Assam commn., March, 1891, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., March, 1899.

HERBERT, HERBERT, F.R.C.S., Major, I.M.S. (prof'r., Grant Med. Coll., Bombay).—1st commn., 31st March, 1887; on mil. duty till Dec., 1892; civil surgn., Kaira, April, 1893; ophthalmic surgn., J. J. Hospital, and prof., Grant Med. Coll., June, 1897.

HERBERT, PATRICK HERBERT COGHLAN (senr. clerk, Jud. and Public Dept., India Office).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; junr. clerk, accts. branch, 11th March, 1872; jud. and public dept., 1877; rest. clerk, 1885-88; senr. clerk, May, 1891; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state (Mr. G. W. E. Russell, M.P.), 1892-94; editor of India Office List, 1887-91; compiled jointly with Mr. Newmark the India office cypher code.

HERRON, HERBERT GEORGE WHITLEY, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 27th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to Burma as asst. commr., Nov., 1888, and offg. under sec.; returned to Bengal, Nov., 1890; joint mag. and collr. Dec., 1896.

HERSCHEL, JOHN, F.R.S., Col. R.E., late Survey of India.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1855; first commn., Dec., 1856; arrived at Calcutta, Jan., 1859; served in the great trigon. survey from Nov., 1859, in the grades of asst. surveyor and dep. supt.; ret'd., March, 1886.

HERVEY, HAMILTON LAW, Indian C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Giggleswick Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 27th March, 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., May, 1894; 1st asst., April, 1897; judge and sessions judge, July, 1897.

HEWETSON, JOHN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. ju'ge, Madras).—Educ. at Repton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; served in Burma, from March, 1886, as asst. commr., judge, civil court, Mandalay, and district officer; returned to Madras, Oct., 1888; senr. asst., Ganjam, July, 1889; head asst., Aug., 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1894; district and sessions judge, May, 1897; collr. and mag., S. Canara, Aug., 1898; dist. and sess. judge, Tinnevelly, Oct., 1899.

HEWETT, JOHN PRESCOTT, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt. of India, home dept.).—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as 1st mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; in charge of provl. gazetteer, Aug., 1883; acted as asst.

acctnt.-gen., Jan., 1884; junr. sec., board of rev., April, 1884; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1886; confd., Aug., 1887; offd. as private sec. to the viceroy in 1888 and 1892, and as sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1890; offg. census commr. for India, July, 1890; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1891; dep. commr., March, 1893; sec. of royal commn. on opium, Oct., 1893; mag. and collr., April, 1894; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1895; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; member of plague commn., Nov., 1898; author of contributions to provl. gazetteer.

HEWETT, WILLIAM SELWOOD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Jud. Dept. (cantonmt. mag., Benares).—First commn., 30th Dec., 1871; arrived, 13th March, 1872, and was employed on mil. duty, including service in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and clasp); cantonmt. mag., March, 1887.

HEWISH, JOHN (dep. acctnt.-gen., India Office).—Junr. clerk, 1st Feb., 1869; senr. clerk, Sept., 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen. and assessor of income tax, May, 1900.

HEWITT, JAMES FRANCIS KATHERINUS, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1858; arrived, 12th Feb., 1859, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and settl. officer; dep. commr., wards' estate in Behar, Feb., 1873; offg. sec., board of rev., Sept., 1874; mag. and collr., Oct., 1878; commr., Chota Nagpur, March, 1879; ret'd., July, 1885; author of 'The Ruling Races of Pre-Historic Times in India,' &c.

HEXT, REAR-ADMIRAL SIR JOHN, A.D.C., K.C.I.E., R.N. (Retd.).—Apptd. Director of the Royal Indian Marine, 26th Feb., 1883; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; ret'd., Feb., 1898.

HEYCOCK, WILLIAM BISSELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Oakham Sch., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HEYWOOD, JOHN MAGEE, Col. R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined Addiscombe, Aug., 1854; 1st commn., Bengal engrs., June, 1856; served in the Bengal public works dept. from April, 1858, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., May, 1881; offg. chief engr., and joint sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., 1883-84; chief engr. and joint sec., irrign. branch, Punjab, 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1889.

HEZLETT, JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Coleraine Academy, Queen's Coll., Galway, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HIGGINS, ALFRED WILLIAM BUCKLE, Indian C.S. (member of board of rev., Madras).—Educ. at Haileybury; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 6th Dec., 1876, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, 1877-78; sec. to the draft forest bill committee, 1882; dep. conservator of forests, Cuddapah, Dec., 1882; also forest settl. officer, July, 1886; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1890; principal asst. to collr., Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Jan., 1892; member, rev. board, and dir. of land records and agric., Dec., 1898.

HIGGINS, ANDREW FRANK, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at

Thomason Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Oct., 1873, and posted to Hyderabad; exec. engr., May, 1881; suptg. engr., Feb., 1901.

HIGGINS, JOHN JAMES, late Police Dept., Central Prov.—Apptd. acting adjutant, mil. police, Narsinghpur, for services rendered during the Mutiny under Sir Henry Havelock, Dec., 1858; from March, 1861, served in the police dept. of the Central Prov., and was promoted to district supt., 1st class, June, 1879; ret'd., March, 1894.

HIGHAM, THOMAS, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (insp. - gen. of irrigation).—Apptd. proby. asst. engr., July, 1867; arrived, Oct., 1867, and served in the irrign. dept., Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1873; asst. sec. to govt., irrign. branch, Nov., 1882; supt. of works, Derajat, Sept., 1883; suptg. engr., Aug., 1886; offd. as chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., Punjab, July-Oct., 1891; chief engr. and sec., irrign. branch, Oct., 1894; on special famine relief duty, May to July, 1897; insp.-gen. of irrigation and joint sec. to govt. of India, Nov., 1897; author of 'Hydraulic Tables'; C.I.E., May, 1896.

HIGHT, ARTHUR EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; entered the service as asst. engr., Nov., 1878; exec. engr., March, 1890; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

HIGNELL, SIDNEY ROBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Malvern and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 8th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.
