NEWBERY, HENRY JAMES, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. dept., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Dec., 1885; ret'd., April, 1887.

NEWBOULD, BABINGTON BENNETT, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Bedford Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 28th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1894; dep. commr., Oct., 1895.

NEWCOMBE, ALFRED CORNELIUS, B.A., A.M.I.C.E. (exec. engr.), India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab and the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; served in the superior accts. establ., 1878-81 and 1886-90, and in Assam, 1881-86; services lent to Nizam's govt., 1891-95; transf. to railway branch, Dec., 1896; transf. to Hyderabad admn., Oct., 1900.

NEWILL, JOSEPH HENRY, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 4th July, 1865; arrived, 25th Dec., 1865; served under the foreign dept. in the thagi and dakaiti branch, from May, 1872; cantonmt. mag., Jan., 1882; 2nd asst. to rest. at Hyderabad, March, 1884; from March, 1887, served as pol. agent at Bhopawar, and afterwards in S.E. Baluchistan; pol. officer in charge of Sardar Ayub Khan, Nov., 1889; acting pol. agent, Jhalawar, Nov., 1892; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, Feb., 1895; offg. rest., W. states of Rajputana, March, 1895; rest. at Gwalior, March, 1898; reverted to mil. duty, Sept., 1899.

NEWMAN, JOHN HENRY, M.D., Col., late Indian Med. Service—First commn., 1867; served in the Abyssinian war, 1868, and Afghan war, 1878-79; civil surgn., Ajmer, April, 1879; offd. as agency surgn., &c., in Rajputana in 1884 and 1889; residency surgn. and chief med. officer in Rajputana, Aug., 1892; offg. inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Bengal, July-Nov., 1893;

administrative med. officer and sanitary commr., Central Prov., March, 1895; inspr.-gen. of civil hosps., Bengal, Nov., 1897; retd., July, 1900.

NEWMARCH, FRANCIS WELLES, B.A. (senr. clerk, Mil. Dept., India Office).—Matriculated at Balliol Coll., Oxford, Jan., 1873; scholar of Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, 1873-78; junr. clerk, mil. dept., India office, 18th May, 1877; senr. clerk, March, 1892; rest. clerk, 1885-94; sec. to Lord Onslow's departmental commn., 1896-97; compiled, jointly with Mr. Herbert, the India office cypher code.

NEWMARCH, LINDSAY SHERWOOD, Major, I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Sehore).—Educ. at Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; first commn., 13th Aug., 1879; from Feb., 1885, served as asst. agent at Indore, Ajmir, and Quetta; asst. rest., Mysore, Oct., 1889; first asst. to agent, Rajputana, Oct., 1892; and in same capacity, Central India, Aug., 1893; pol. agent, Bhopal, May, 1897; ditto, Sehore, Nov., 1899.

NEWMARCH, SIR OLIVER RICHARDSON, K.C.S.I., Major-Gen., late I.S.C.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, March, 1855; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, Feb., 1861; 3rd asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., 1871; dep. acctnt.-gen., mil. dept., 1875; acctnt.-gen. and ex-officio dep. sec. to the govt. of India, April, 1878; sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., July, 1886; retd., Nov., 1887; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; sec., mil. dept., India office, May, 1889, to May, 1899; K.C.S.I., May, 1894.

NEWNHAM-SMITH, EDWARD DEEDES, B.L., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Bombay Jud. Dept. (cantonment mag., Poona).—First commn., 27th Feb., 1860; served through the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and clasp); cantonmt. mag., Disa, Oct., 1887; and at Poona and Kirkee, Oct., 1888.

NEWTON, JOHN (2nd class clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879.

NEWTON, WILLIAM GYLICH, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (sup'tg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and employed on state railways and in the Central Prov.; on famine duty in Madras, 1877; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; transfed. to Burma, Aug., 1887; suptg. engr., Aug., 1900.

NICHOLETTS, HENRY SAMUEL, late Haidarabad Administration.—Arrived, 20th Jan., 1858, and served in the telegraph and police depts.; district supt. of police, Berar, 1868; asst. commr., May, 1872; judge, small cause court, Sept., 1882; dep. commr., Nov., 1888; retd., April, 1896.

NICHOLL, BERNARD VINCENT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Beaumont Coll., Windsor, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 9th Nov., 1889, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1897.

NICHOLLS, GEORGE JASPER, LL.B., B.L., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Exeter Gr. Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 29th Nov., 1864, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and in various acting appts.; transfed. temporarily to Bengal on famine duty, 1874-75; small cause court judge, Jabalpur, Sept., 1880; dep. commr., March, 1883; from July, 1883, served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as manager of the

Awa state, and district and sessions judge; retd., June, 1895; author of 'The Law of the Central Prov.,' 'Digest of the Circulars and Standing Orders of the Chief Commissioner, Central Prov., 1862-80,' 'An Economic Gazetteer of the Central Prov.,' &c.

NICHOLSON, ÉUSTACE FERRERS (solicitor to govt., high court, Bombay).—Offd. as solicitor to govt. and public prosecutor, May-Nov., 1897; confid., April, 1898.

NICHOLSON, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (member, rev. board, Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Med. Coll., Epsom, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 4th Dec., 1869, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., acting sub-sec. and sec., board of rev., and principal asst.; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1889; collr. and mag., Oct., 1889; member of viceroy's legisl. council, Oct., 1897; acting resdt., Travancore and Cochin, Aug., 1898; C.I.E., June, 1899; member of rev. board, Nov., 1899; addl. member of viceroy's legisl. council, Oct., 1900; compiled district manual of Coimbatore.

NICHOLSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, M.D., F.R.C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service, Punjab.—Entered the service, March, 1878; in mil. employ till March, 1881, since when he has held various civil surgeoncies; civil surgn., Mooltan, Nov., 1897; retd. Feb. 1899.

NICHOLSON, SIR WILLIAM GUSTAVUS, K.C.B., Major-Gen., R.E.—First commn., March, 1865; arrived, Dec., 1871; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1872, and served as asst. engr., Haidarabad, and in the Punjab irrign. branch; transfed. to mil. works branch, 1873; exec. engr., March, 1876; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and 3 clasps, bronze star); sec. to defence committee, April, 1882; served in Egyptian expdn., 1882 (medal and clasp, bronze star, 4th class Osmanieh); ex-officio asst. adjutant-gen., 1885; suptg. engr., March, 1886; served in Burma expdn., 1886-87; mil. sec. to commander-in-chief, July, 1890, to April, 1893, when he reverted to the mil. works dept.; chief engr., April, 1894; dep. adjutant-gen., Punjab, April, 1895; adjt.-gen. in India, Nov., 1898; mil. sec. to field-marshal comdg. in South Africa, Dec., 1899; dir. of transport, S. Africa, Feb., 1900.

NICOLLS, JOSEPH RICHARD CLINTON, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. 23rd Sept., 1878, as asst. engr., irrigation branch; exec. engr., April, 1892.

NIGHTINGALE, WALTER HAWKINS, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Apptd., 23rd July, 1868, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., March, 1873; divl. supt., district works 1882 and 1887; inspr. of local works in various divns., 1887-91; suptg. engr., March, 1892; sec. to chief commr. of Assam, public works dept., June, 1895, and again May, 1899, chief engr., Jan., 1899.

NILAMBARA MUKERJI, M.A., B.L.—Apptd. vice-chairman of the Calcutta corporation, Jan., 1896.

NISBET, JOHN, late Indian Forest Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. as asst. conservator, Dec., 1875, and served in Purma; dep. conservator, May, 1882; conservator, Feb., 1899; retd., Aug., 1900.

NISBET, ROBERT PARRY, C.I.E., B.L., Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 20th Nov.,

1859; from Aug., 1862, served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. officer; jud. asst., July, 1876; dep. commr., April, 1877; commr., Rawal Pindi, Nov., 1886; rest. in Kashmir, Nov., 1888; ret'd., May, 1892; author of Gujranwala settlt. report.

NOBLE, CHARLES SIMEON, Col., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived as ensign, Bengal native infantry, 14th Nov., 1858; from April, 1862, served in the police dept., Oudh, as asst. district supt., and district supt.; and from June, 1866, in the commn. as asst. commr., and dep. commr.; ret'd., Nov., 1890.

NOETLING, FRITZ, Ph.D.—Apptd. palaeontologist to the geological survey of India, Feb., 1887.

NOLAN, PHILIP, Indian C.S. (member, board of rev., Calcutta, and Lieut.-Gov.'s. Council, Bengal).—Educ. at Stonyhurst Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in Bengal; joint mag., Feb., 1880; mag. and collr., July, 1884; offg. sec., board of rev., March, 1885; sec., govt. of Bengal, gen., rev., and statistical dept., March, 1887; commr., Rajshahi divn., Dec., 1891; acting member, board of rev., Calcutta, May to Aug., 1898, and from Nov., 1900; member Lieut.-Gov.'s. Council, Bengal, Dec., 1900.

NORFOR, CECIL HENRY THOMAS, late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 30th Aug., 1870; exec. engr., May, 1883; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

NORMAN, SIR HENRY WYLIE, G.C.B., G.C.M.G., C.I.E., Gen., I.S.C.—Ensign in Bengal army, March, 1844; served throughout the Punjab campaign in 1848-49; brigade-major and asst. adjutant-gen. in various expeds. on the Peshawar frontier in the years 1850-54; commanded a detachment in the Sonthal campaign, 1855; served throughout the Mutiny, 1857-58, as asst. or dep. adjutant-gen., and the greater part of the time as adjutant-gen. to the army in the field; also in the campaigns in Rohilkhand and in Oudh, in 1858-59, including several actions; in 1860 was apptd. asst. mil. sec. at the horse guards; sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., 1862-70; member of the council of the govr.-gen. of India, June, 1870, to March, 1877; member of the council of the sec. of state for India, Feb., 1878, to Nov., 1883, when he was apptd. govr. of Jamaica; govr. of Queensland, 1888-95.

NORMAN, THOMAS, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Royal Sch., Enniskillen, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 21st Jan., 1863; served in Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Oct., 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1887.

NORRIS, JOHN FREEMAN, K.C., B.L., late Calcutta High Court.—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 17th Nov., 1865; Q.C., Jan., 1882; judge of the high court, Calcutta, 17th June, 1882; ret'd., Nov., 1895.

NORTHBROOK (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. THOMAS GEORGE BARING, P.C., G.C.S.I., D.C.L.—Born 1826; educ. at Christ Church, Oxford, where he graduated in 1846; was successively private sec. to Mr. Labouchere at the board of trade, to Sir George Grey at the home office, to Sir Charles Wood at the India board, and at the admiralty till 1857; M.P. for Penryn and Falmouth, 1857-66; a lord of the admiralty, 1857-58; under sec. of state for India, June, 1859, to Jan., 1861; under

sec. for war, Jan., 1861; again under sec. for India, July, 1861, to April, 1864; under sec. for home affairs, 1864-66; sec. to the admiralty, 1866; succeeded as 2nd baron in the same year; again under sec. for war, 1868-72; viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, Feb., 1872, to Feb., 1876; G.C.S.I., May, 1872; created Viscount Baring, and Earl of Northbrook, 1876; first lord of the admiralty, 1880-85; high commr. on a special mission to Egypt, 1884; a royal commr. for the London exhibn. of 1886.

NORTHCOTE (BARON), RIGHT HON. HENRY STAFFORD, C.B. (govr. of Bombay).—Educ. at Eton and Merton Coll., Oxford; priv. sec. to Lord Salisbury, 1876-77, and to chancellor of the exchequer, 1877-80; M.P. for Exeter, 1880-99; finl. sec. to War Office, 1885-86; surveyor-gen. of ordnance, 1886-87; created a baronet, 1887; apptd. govr. of Bombay, Feb., 1900; and created a Baron of the United Kingdom.

NORTHCOTT, JOHN PERCY CYRIL SALE-MARSH (2nd class clerk, political dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 17th Dec., 1894; transf. to pol. dept., Nov., 1897.

NORTON, DAVID, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma).—Educ. at Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; transf. for service in Burma as dep. commr., Jan., 1887; prest. of the Rangoon municipality, May, 1890; commr., April, 1891; C.S.I., Jan., 1898.

NUGENT, JOHN, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Stonyhurst Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 6th Nov., 1872, and served in Bengal, and the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; senr. asst. commr., Kumaon, Jan., 1887; supt., Dehra Dun, May, 1888; dep. commr., July, 1891; mag. and collr., July, 1893; ret'd., Sept., 1899.

NUNN, HAROLD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Ripon Gr. Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

NURAL HUDA, SYUD, B.A., LL.B., Statutory Civilian (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Joined the service 4th August, 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1893; mag. and collr., Oct., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1899.

OAKDEN, RALPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Cranbrook Gr. Sch., and at Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 16th Dec., 1895; and served in N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

OATES, EUGENE WILLIAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 26th July, 1867, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Feb., 1877; suptg. engr., Dec., 1891; ret'd., March, 1899.

OBARD, OWEN JAMES, Capt., I.S.C., Burma commn. (asst. commr.)—First commn., 29th Aug., 1885; joined the Burma commn., May, 1890, as asst. commr.

OBARD, ROBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 27th Dec., 1873, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; on famine duty in Madras, 1877-78; special asst. commr., Berar, March, 1887; dep. commr., July, 1890; civil and sessions

judge, April, 1891; jud. commr., Oct., 1895; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

O'BRIEN, BARTHOLOMEW, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, Med. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh (civil surgn., Allahabad).—Apptd. asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 1st Oct., 1872; joined the dept. as acting civil surgn. and jail supt., Sept., 1873; confd., Oct., 1875.

O'BRIEN, EDMUND CORNELIUS, Postal Dept., Govt. of India (inspr.-gen., railway mail service in India).—Entered the service, Oct., 1878; apptd. asst. dir.-gen. of Post Office, April, 1888; inspr.-gen., railway mail service in India.

O'BRIEN, JOSEPH, M.D., Lieut.-Col., late India Med. Service.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1870, and was in mil. employ until May, 1881; from that date served as civil surgn. and offg. prof'r., Calcutta med. coll.; apptd. prof'r. at the coll., and surgn. at the coll. hosp., April, 1892; ret'd., Aug., 1898.

O'BRIEN, PETER HENRY, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 6th Nov., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1891; transf'd. to Assam as dep. commr., Jan., 1892; mag. and collr., March, 1898.

O'CALLAGHAN, FRANCIS LANGFORD, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1862, as asst. engr., Central Prov.; exec. engr., April, 1866; transf'd. to the railway branch, 1870, and served as engr.-in-chief on various railway surveys; suptg. engr., Jan., 1880; C.I.E., May, 1883; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; chief engr., April, 1889; consulting engr. to govt. of India for state railways, May, 1889; sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Aug., 1892; ret'd., July, 1894.

O'CALLAGHAN, IGNATIUS, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., Feb., 1873; traffic supt., Rajputana state railway, Dec., 1874; gen. traffic manager, Rajputana-Malwa railway system, April, 1885; ret'd., 1896.

O'CONNELL, HUGH HAMLYN, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Entered the dept., Sept., 1878, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.

O'CONOR, JAMES EDWARD, C.I.E., M.I.S.I., Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (dir.-gen. of statistics).—Registrar, dept. of agric. and commerce, June, 1873; asst. sec. in statistical branch of dept. of rev., agric., and commerce, Feb., 1875; asst. sec., dept. of fin. and commerce, Dec., 1879; on special duty in connection with the Melbourne exhibn., 1880-81; C.I.E., Jan., 1891; dir.-gen. of statistics, retaining his post as asst. sec., May, 1895; author of reports on the production of tobacco, vanilla, and lac, and on the cultivation of the ground nut, the carob tree, and the eucalyptus in India.

ODDIE, HENRY JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1876, and posted as asst. engr. to state rlys.; exec. engr., May, 1888.

ODLING, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.S.I., M.E., M.I.C.E. (chief engr. and sec., N.W.P. and Oudh), India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 20th Oct., 1865; exec. engr., Sept., 1870; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, Feb., 1883; suptg. engr., Nov., 1889; chief engr. for irrign. and joint sec. to govt. of Bengal, March, 1892; sec., irrign. branch, Jan., 1893; transf'd. to

N.W. Prov. and Oudh as chief engr. and sec. in buildings and roads branch, Dec., 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1898.

O'DONNELL, CHARLES JAMES, M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at St. Ignatius' Coll., and Queen's Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 14th Oct., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, Jun., 1874; asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics, Feb., 1875; joint mag., July, 1884; mag. and collr., Aug., 1889; provl. supt. of census, Bengal, 1890-91; commr., Dec., 1898; ret'd., April, 1900.

O'DONNELL, GEORGE BOODRIC, B.A., LL.B., Major, Indian Staff Corps, Foreign Dept. Govt. of India (administrator of Radhanpur).—First commn., 18th Jan., 1880; on mily. duty till Feb., 1887, when he offd. as asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha; confd., March, 1889; manager of Malia State, Kathiawar, Sept., 1892; asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, June, 1896; ditto, South Mahratta country, Feb., 1900; administrator of Radhanpur, July, 1900.

O'DONNELL, SAMUEL PERRY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Repton, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

O'DONOGHUE, WILLIAM FREEMAN, India Public Works Dept. (examr. of accounts).—Apptd. acctnt. in subord. accts. estab., pub. wks. dept., 4th April, 1873; asst. examr., Sept., 1876; dep. examr., July, 1880; examr., May, 1886.

O'DWYER, MICHAEL FRANCIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (rev. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at St. Stanislaus Coll., Tullamore, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after open exam. of 1882; arrived, 19th Nov., 1885, and was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; settl. collr., 1889-94; dir. of dept. of land records and agric., Punjab, Dec., 1896; settl. officer for Ulwar and Bhurtpore, July, 1897; special duty in connection with the scheme of the new frontier, April, 1901; rev. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901.

O'FARRELL, HORACE HANDLEY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Westminster; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1888; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Jan., 1894; offg. judge, high court. April, 1899.

OGDEN, WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Apptd. to Indian Tel Dept., July, 1868; transf'd. to accts. estab. as acctnt., 6th Oct., 1873; asst. examr., Sept., 1881; dep. examr., Oct., 1882; examr., Aug., 1889.

OGILVIE, GEORGE MACARTNEY, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and jud. asst.; dep. commr., Oct., 1881; commr. and supt., Nov., 1888; in 1893-94 and 1894-95 acted as finl. commr.; ret'd., Jan., 1896.

O'HARA, WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (inspr.-gen. of gaols, Madras).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1874; arrived, 18th April, 1875, and served in Madras under the mil. dept., and as civil-surgn. and jail supt. at various stations; district med. and sanitary officer,

Trichinopoli, May, 1895; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Nov., 1899.

O'KELLY, HILARY MARTIN, B.A., M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept. —Joined the service as asst. supt., 21st June, 1866; arrived, 14th Sept., 1866; supt., Jan., 1886; offg. dir., traffic branch, July-Oct., 1894; ret'd., May, 1898.

O'KINEALY, JAMES, late Indian C.S. (Bengal). —Educ. at Queen's Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst. supt. of survey, asst. commr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; apptd. addl. sessions judge of Bhagalpur, Sept., 1874; district and sessions judge, May, 1875; offg. sec., govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1877; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Jan., 1878; member of legisl. council, 1880; judge of the high court, Calcutta, Feb., 1883; also prest. of the board of exmrs., April, 1885; ret'd., June, 1899.

O'KINEALY, PETER, B.L. (standing counsel, Calcutta high court). —Called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1876; apptd. standing counsel, Bengal, 19th April, 1895.

OLDFIELD, FRANCIS DU PRÉ, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras). —Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 11th Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Nov., 1897.

OLDFIELD, SIR RICHARD CHARLES, Kt., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh). —Apptd. from Haileybury, 1848; arrived, 9th Nov., 1848; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; asst. sec. to govt., and asst. acctnt., N.W. Prov., March, 1856; mag. and collr., Farakhabad, March, 1864; from May, 1869, served in the jud. dept., and was apptd. civil and sessions judge, Jan., 1872; addl. judge, high court, N.W. Prov., Dec., 1874; puisne judge, high court, Nov., 1882; ret'd., March, 1887.

OLDHAM, ABEL JOHN, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept. —Apptd., 1st March, 1873, and posted to Bengal; employed in Madras on famine relief works, 1877-78; and in N.W. Prov. and Oudh on public works accts., 1881-82; exec. engr., Aug., 1881; transfid. to state railways, Sept., 1886; offg. dep. consulting engr. for railways, Calcutta, 1890; and at Lucknow, 1892-94; public works under sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1891-92; ret'd., Oct., 1895.

OLDHAM, CHARLES EVELYN ARBUTHNOT WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal). —Educ. at Galway Gr. Sch., and, Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 12th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., jud., &c., depts., March, 1895; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1898; mag. and collr., Sept., 1900.

OLDHAM, FRANCIS GEORGE, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept. —First commn., Dec., 1858; joined the dept., Feb., 1873; compt. of accts., Haidarabad, April, 1875; examr. of guaranteed railway accts., March, 1878; examr. of telegraph accts., 1883; examr. of accts., Allahabad, Aug., 1885; examr. of accts., mil. works, Jan., 1887; ret'd., Jan., 1892.

OLDHAM, RICHARD DIXON, A.R.S.M., F.G.S. (supt., Geological Survey of India). —Joined the survey, 6th Nov., 1879, as asst. supt.; dep. supt., Sept., 1885; on special duty in connection with Tibet mission, 1886; supt., Oct., 1891; has revised the 'Manual of the Geology of India,'

and contributed largely to the survey publs.; author of a report on the Assam earthquake of June 12, 1897.

OLDHAM, WILLIAM BENJAMIN, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. —Educ. at Kingstown Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1865, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1874; dep. commr., Nov., 1879; on famine relief duty, 1866 and 1874, and at Bellary, 1877-79; mag. and collr., Aug., 1888; commr. of Chittagong, Oct., 1891; C.I.E., Jan., 1893; member of rev. board, Dec., 1898; ret'd., Nov., 1900.

OLIPHANT, HENRY LAURENCE, late Bengal C.S. —Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived 8th April, 1858; from July, 1859, served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and dep. commr.; jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, April, 1877; ret'd., Oct., 1883.

OLIPHANT, JOHN S., late Funds Dept., India Office. —East India house writer, old establ., 22nd June, 1853; junr. clerk, minuting branch, 1855; private sec. to chairman and dep. chairman, 1857-58; India office, secretariat dept., 1859; asst. private sec. and précis writer to the sec. of state, 1863-65; senr. clerk, funds dept., April, 1866; asst. sec., mil. dept., 1872, but continued doing duty in funds dept.; also agent to administrators-gen. of India, 1877; acted as jud. and public sec. in 1879; dir. of funds, July, 1879; ret'd., Dec., 1893.

OLIVER, ARTHUR KNAPP (master and registrar in equity, Bombay high court). —Joined the service, Jan., 1876, as deputy registrar; asst. regr., May, 1877; asst. commr., Sept., 1893; officiated as master and registrar in equity, April, 1897; confid., Jan., 1899.

OLIVER, GILBERT THOMAS INGLIS, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.). —Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as apprentice engineer, Oct., 1878; asst. engr., Feb., 1879; exec. engr., Sept., 1892.

OLIVER, JAMES WILLIAM (conservator, forest dept., N.W. Prov.). —Arrived, 4th Dec., 1874, and was apptd. asst. conservator; dep. conservator, July, 1880; conservator, May, 1891; dir. of forest school, Dehra Dun, Aug., 1896.

OLIVIER, HENRY DACRES, Lieut.-Col., R.E., A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (agent to Bombay, Baroda, and Central India Railway Co.). —Entered the service, 15th Dec., 1871; joined the dept., 1st June, 1875; employed in Baluchistan, 1878-80; exec. engr., and dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, March, 1884; employed on Soudan railway, 1885; under sec. to govt., public works dept., 1889-91; agent to the Bombay, Baroda, and Central India railway co., Aug., 1894; suptg. engr., Sept., 1900.

OLLIVANT, ALFRED, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. —Arrived as ensign, 20th April, 1857; medal for services during Mutiny, 1857; apptd. to Jhansi mil. police, July, 1858; from May, 1861, served as district supt. of police; dep. inspr.-gen., June, 1885; inspr.-gen., Jan., 1890; returned to Europe, 1891.

OLLIVANT, SIR EDWARD CHARLES KAYLL, K.C.I.E., Indian C.S. (member of Council, Bombay). —Educ. at Marlborough; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty at Baroda in connection

with estate of Mir Zulfiqar Ali, Jan., 1879; asst. commr., branch inspr.-gen. of registrn. in Sind, and Sindi translr. to govt., Sept., 1880; also dep. supt. of census in Sind, Jan., 1881; municipal commr. for Bombay, May, 1881; on special duty in England in this connection, 1883; C.I.E., May, 1888; pol. agent and collr. of stamp rev., Kathiawar, April, 1889; K.C.I.E., May, 1892; offd. as commr. in Sind, April, 1895, to March, 1896; offd. as chief sec. to govt. of Bombay, March to July, 1896; member of council, Bombay, April, 1897.

O'MALLEY, LEWIS SIDNEY STEWARD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at K. Edward's Sch., Norwich, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 7th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

OMMANNEY, EDWARD LACON, C.S.I., Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 12th June, 1855; asst. to commr. in charge of ex-king of Delhi and state prisoners, Sept., 1857; took ex-king of Delhi and family to Rangoon, and served in Pegu commn., 1858-59; served subsequently in the Punjab as asst. commr., personal asst. and dep. commr.; commr. and supt., Derajat, Dec., 1883; on special duty with Hazara field force, Nov., 1888; C.S.I., May, 1889; reverted to mil. duty, 1891.

OMMANNEY, HENRY TRAVERS, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 12th Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., railway mag., cantonmt. mag., and second asst.; acting under sec. to govt., rev., &c., depts., April, 1886; and acting inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1888; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1891; senr., Feb., 1895; member of hemp drugs commn., 1893-94; ret'd., Oct., 1896.

ONSLLOW (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. WILLIAM HILLIER ONSLLOW, G.C.M.G. (parly. under sec. of state, Colonial Office).—Born in 1853; educ. at Eton and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. parly. under sec. of state for the colonies, Feb., 1887, and again, Nov., 1900; acted as vice-prest. of the colonial conference, 1887, and as a delegate to the conference on sugar bounties; K.C.M.G., 1887; parly. sec., board of trade, Feb., 1888; govr. and commr.-in-chief, New Zealand, 1889-92; G.C.M.G., 1889; parly. under sec. of state for India, July, 1895 to Nov., 1900.

OPPENHEIM, ERNEST FERDINAND, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rugby Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 5th Dec., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

OPPERT, GUSTAV, Ph.D., late Educn. Dept., Madras.—Profr. of Sanscrit, presidency coll., and curator of govt. oriental manuscript library, 14th Nov., 1872; fellow of Madras univ., 1873; Telugu translr. to govt., Sept., 1878; ret'd., April, 1893.

ORMOND, ERNEST W., B.L. (judge, Calcutta small cause court).—After serving temporarily in 1891-92 as asst. sec. to the govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., was apptd. to act as 2nd judge, Calcutta small cause court, March, 1893; confd., Dec., 1894.

ORMROD, JOHN BAXTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived,

16th Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.

ORR, JAMES PETER, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 12th Oct., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and forest settlt. officer; 2nd asst., March, 1896; 1st asst., Sept., 1897; acting under sec., rev. and finl. depts., Nov., 1897; acting collr. and mag., April, 1898.

OSBORN, WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—First commn., 18th Dec., 1861; asst. engr., Nov., 1864; served in the Abyssinian campaign, 1867 (medal); exec. engr., Jan., 1869; suptg. engr., Jan., 1893; ret'd., May, 1894.

OTTEWILL, WILLIAM THOMAS (second-class clerk, registry and record dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 12th Sept., 1898.

OTTLEY, JOHN WALTER, C.I.E., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept. (presdt., Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.).—First commn., Jan., 1864; arrived, Nov., 1868; joined the dept., Feb., 1870; asst. to chief engr. for irrign., Punjab, April, 1870; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; asst. to chief engr. for irrign., Bengal, Feb., 1874; re-transfd. to Punjab, irrign. branch, June, 1875; asst. sec., irrign. branch, Punjab, July, 1881; supt. of works, Bari Doab circle, April, 1882; suptg. engr., Dec., 1884; chief engr. and joint sec. to govt. of Punjab, public works dept., Nov., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1892; sec., irrign. branch, Sept., 1892; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Oct., 1894; ret'd. from the dept., 1896; served in the campaign on the N.W. frontier in 1897-98 (medal and two clasps); ret'd. from the army, July, 1898; apptd. presdt. Royal Indian Engrg. Coll., Cooper's Hill, Oct., 1899.

OWEN, EDWARD, B.L. (staff clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; staff clerk, July, 1896; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Jan., 1900.

OWEN, WILLIAM, M.D., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., Indian Medical Service (opium factory supt., Behar).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1877, and was employed under the mil. dept. and as second med. officer, Port Blair; from 24th May, 1882, served in Bengal as supt. of vaccn., dep. sanitary commr. and civil surgn.; principal asst. to opium agent, Behar, April, 1889; factory supt., Behar, May, 1891.

OWENS, JOHN, Postal Dept., Govt. of India (presy. postmr., Calcutta).—Entered the service, June, 1868; inspr. of post offices, April, 1873; supt., Sept., 1881; presy. postmr., Bombay, Feb., 1892; ditto Calcutta, April, 1893.

OZANNE, EDWARD CHARLES, M.R.A.C., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine relief duty, 1874, 1877, and 1879; dir. of agric., July, 1883; survey and settlt. commr., May, 1891; survey commr. and dir. of land records and agric., April, 1893; ret'd., July, 1897; was a delegate to the Brussels Conference on sugar bounties, 1898, and again 1901.

OZZARD, HUDSON HENRY, Major, I.S.C. First commn., 13th June, 1874; arrived, 1st Nov., 1874, and was employed on mil. duty, including

service in Perak expdn. (medal and clasp) and Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); acting cantonmt. mag., Feb., 1888; confd., April, 1890; ret'd., April, 1899.

PADDISON, GEORGE FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Richmond, Yorks., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

PAGE, HORACE, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 19th Dec., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag; 2nd asst., April, 1900.

PAGE, WILLIAM HUMPHREY, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Nottingham Gr. Sch., and Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 10th Dec., 1868, and served in Bengal; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1879; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1885; ret'd., April, 1895.

PAINTER, HAROLD LISCOMBE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 9th Dec., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

PALLE CHENTSAL RAO PANTULU, C.I.E., late Supt. of Stamps and Stationery, Madras.—Served from Feb., 1851, in the jud. and rev. depts., Madras; from July, 1859, served as dep. collr., special asst. to the inam commr., and dep. collr. of salt rev.; sheristadar, board of rev., May, 1872; fellow of Madras univ., 1875; supt. of stamps and stationery, July, 1882; C.I.E., Feb., 1887; ret'd., April, 1887; member of Madras legisl. council, July, 1887.

PALLISER, HARRY GEORGE, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Educ. at Chiswick Sch.; joined the dept., 28th Oct., 1867; exec. engr., Nov., 1872; suptg. engr., Feb., 1894; chief engr., June, 1899.

PALMER, ARTHUR LAURENCE HAMILTON, M.I.E.E. (supt., India Telegraph Dept.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., Sept., 1878; supt., Feb., 1894.

PALMER, CHARLES EDGAR, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at the Mathematical Sch., Rochester, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 5th Dec., 1896, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

PALMER, CHARLES GEORGE, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 7th Oct., 1868, and employed on irrig. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1876; suptg. engr., Jan., 1898; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; chief engr., Feb., 1900.

PALMER, SIR ELWIN MITFORD, K.C.B., K.C.M.G., late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the finl. dept., Nov., 1871; asst. to acctnt.-gen., British Burma, Dec., 1873; transf. to Bombay, April, 1876; asst. to compt.-gen., Oct., 1878; served under the Egyptian govt., 1878-79; from Jan., 1880, served in Madras as offg. asst. and dep. acctnt.-gen.; compt., Haidarabad, Dec., 1882; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1884; asst. compt.-gen., Dec., 1884; transf. for service under Egyptian govt., Aug., 1885; K.C.B., Jan., 1897.

PANTIN, ROBERT GERALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr Central Prov.).—Educ. at

Bradfield Coll., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 21st Dec., 1894, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and acting under sec.

PANTON, EDWARD BROOKS HENDERSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich, and at Christ's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

PARGITER, EDWIN HERMANN, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883.

PARGITER, FREDERICK EDEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Taunton Coll. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 6th Nov., 1875; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., settl. officer, commr. of the Sundarbans, and offg. under sec.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1887; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1893; author of 'The Revenue History of the Sundarbans from 1765 to 1870,' and of a transln. of the Markandeya Purana.

PARKER, AUBREY HASTINGS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., Melbourne Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 13th Dec., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

PARKER, EDWIN WOODALL, late Registrar of Chief Court, Punjab.—From 20th Oct., 1863, served in the Punjab as extra asst. commr., and small cause court judge; district judge, Simla, May, 1887, to Jan., 1888; registrar of chief court, Oct., 1888; author of 'Outlines of the Constitutional Law of India,' and 'Outlines of Jurisprudence'; ret'd., May, 1900.

PARKER, JOSEPH, C.S.I., late Dir.-Gen. of Stores, India Office.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; East India house writer, old establ., 9th July, 1851; junr. clerk, marine dept., Aug., 1858; store dept., 1860; senr. clerk, Nov., 1866; asst. to dir.-gen. of stores, Jan., 1890; dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1891; ret'd., Aug., 1896.

PARKER, WILLIAM HENRY, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Bengal.—Apptd., 24th Aug., 1860; from Nov., 1873, served in the railway branch, and was employed on various lines; offg. chief engr. and joint sec., Bengal, railway branch, July-Oct., 1885; served subsequently as engr.-in-chief on various railway surveys; ret'd., Jan., 1892.

PARKER, WILLIAM JACKSON, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 16th Dec., 1858; from July, 1867, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and jud. asst.; district judge, Nov., 1884; dep. commr., Sept., 1887; divl. judge, Oct., 1891; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1894.

PARKES, BENJAMIN, M.I.C.E., M.E., F.C.H., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to state railways; transf. to Punjab provl. establ., Jan., 1879; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; suptg. engr., July, 1899.

PARKIN, HENRY, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C. (dep. inspr.-gen. of mil. police, Burma).—First commn., 11th May, 1878; served in the Zhoob expdn., 1884, and in Burma, 1886-87 and 1887-89 (medal); asst. dir. of police supply and

clothing, Burma, 15th Aug., 1888; dep. inspr.-gen. of police for supply and clothing, June, 1890; dep. inspr.-gen. of mil. police, Dec., 1896; offz. inspr.-gen., April, 1901.

PARLETT, LEONARD MONTAGUE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Bradford Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 21st Dec., 1891, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer.

PARRATT, CHARLES (clerk, Indo-European telegraph, Public Works dept., India Office).—Apptd. to the London office of the Indo-European telegraph dept. as clerk, 26th March, 1881; transf. to India office, 1st Jan., 1889.

PARROTT, BENJAMIN ALEXANDER NAPIER, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn., July, 1868; arrived, Nov., 1868; served in the Lushai expdn., 1871; from April, 1872, served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., asst. town mag., and settl. and boundary officer; dep. commr., Feb., 1886; commr., Oct., 1894; ret'd., May, 1898.

PARRY, R., late Educ. Service, Bengal.—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1865; served as head-master, prof'r., and offg. principal at various colls., and as inspr. of schools (temporary); ret'd., 1890.

PARRY, ROBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

PARRY, WILLIAM ENSOR, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomson Coll.; apptd., 23rd Dec., 1859, and posted to state railways; transf. to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1860; exec. engr., Sept., 1868; ret'd., Dec., 1895.

PARSONS, CLEMENT GEORGE, Major, I.S.C. (inspr.-gen. of registration, Punjab).—Arrived 28th April, 1880; apptd. to Punjab commn. as asst. commr., Sept., 1885; dep. commr., April, 1896; inspr.-gen. of registration, commr. of excise, and supt. of stamps, July, 1898.

PARSONS, EDWARD REID (dep. inspr.-gen. of police, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Entered the service, 17th July, 1875; offg. asst. dist. supt., June, 1878; confid., Feb., 1881; dist. supt., July, 1885; dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1901.

PARSONS, FRANCIS COTTON, Indian C.S. (head asst. collr., Madras).—Educ. at Amersham Hall Sch., Reading, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Jan., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; special settl. officer, July, 1894; head asst. coll. and mag., Aug., 1897; special settl. officer, Malabar, Jan., 1898; head asst. collr., S. Canara, 29th May, 1901.

PARSONS, HENRY JAMES, B.L., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 21st Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., asst. judge, and judge and sessions judge; acted as jud. commr. and judge of the sadar court in Sind, in 1881-82, and again in 1887; judge of high court, Nov., 1887; acted as chief justice for several periods since June, 1898; ret'd., 1900.

PARSONS, JAMES EDMUND BACON, B.L., Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 4th Feb., 1849; from Feb., 1856, served

in the Punjab as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; reverted to mil. duty, Nov., 1885.

PARSONS, OCTAVIUS SYDNEY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 28th Nov., 1891, and served in Burma as asst. commr., cantonmt. mag., small cause judge, and settl. officer.

PARTHASARATHI AIYANGAR, M.O., M.A., M.L. (judge, small cause court, Madras).—Apptd. judge, court of small causes, Madras, April, 1896.

PARTRIDGE, JOHN GEORGE DENMAN, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Sherborne, and at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 15th Jan., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. to mag. and collr.; senr. asst., Ganjam, Aug., 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1898.

PARTRIDGE, WILLIAM REGINALD, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, Sherborne Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1883, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., May, 1888; senr. asst. commr., Garhwal, Nov., 1890; dep. commr., Oct., 1891; also settl. officer, Nov., 1893; joint mag., Nov., 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; mag. and collr., April, 1896.

PASSMORE, JOHN REGINALD JUTSUM (2nd class clerk, acct.-general's dep., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 10th June, 1899.

PATCH, JAMES, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 17th May, 1863, as asst. supt. of police; transf. to Assam, Jan., 1869; district supt., Sept., 1870; served with Lushai expdn., 1871-72; returned to Bengal, July, 1884; ret'd., April, 1894.

PATERSON, GEORGE ALFRED, Indian C.S. (dep. supt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Blair Lodge Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Oct., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, Sept., 1899.

PATTABHIRAMA AIYAR, C.R.A. (judge, Madras City Civil Court).—Apptd. judge, Madras City Civil Court, April, 1899.

PATTERSON, ALEXANDER BLEAKLEY, C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Belfast Royal Acad. Inst., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 20th Dec., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settl. officer, and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Dec., 1883; acting commr. of Jhansi, July-Oct., 1890; commr. of N. India salt rev., April, 1891; C.I.E., May, 1895; ret'd., Dec., 1898; author of Fatehpur and Karwi settl. reports.

PATTERSON, WILLIAM HENRY, late Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1851; served as a volunteer during the Mutiny; asst. supt., Jan., 1883; dep. supt., Feb., 1889; ret'd., Sept., 1893.

PATTLE, ARTHUR JOHN (staff clerk, acct.-gen.'s dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; staff clerk, July, 1896.

PATUCK, PESTONJEE SRABJEE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Provs.).—Educ. at Elphinstone Coll., Bombay, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived

12th Dec., 1898, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.

PAUL, ALFRED WALLIS, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and acting inspr.-gen. of registrn.; on special duty in connection with the Thibet mission, April, 1886; dep. commr., Aug., 1887; C.I.E., Jan., 1889; on special duty on Thibetan frontier, April, 1890; British commr. for Anglo-Chinese convention regarding Sikkim and Thibet, Feb., 1891; mag. and collr., May, 1891; on special duty at Darjiling, March, 1892; ret'd., Dec., 1895.

PAUL, JOSIAH EDWARD, late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875; asst. to chief engr., and under sec. to govt., irrign. branch, in 1880; exec. engr., June, 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1900.

PAYNE, CHARLES FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 6th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

PEACOCK, EDWARD BOVILL, late Punjab Judicial Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd April, 1862; apptd. asst. commr., 25th Feb., 1867; offd. as judge of small cause court in 1874 and 1875; district judge, April, 1889; ret'd., March, 1899.

PEACOCK, HENRY PHIPSON, Major-Gen., late Pol. Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Bengal cavalry, Sept., 1856; served during the Mutiny (medal), and subsequently with the govr.-gen.'s body guard; acted as agent to the govr.-gen. with King of Oudh, and supt. of pol. pensions, on several occasions; pol. agent, Murshidabad, 1878; pol. agent, Alwar, 1882; acting rest., W. Rajputana, March, 1886; offg. rest. at Jaipur and offg. agent in Central India, May, 1888; pol. rest. in Turkish Arabia, Nov., 1891; rest., Jaipur, May, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., March, 1894.

PEARS, STEUART DURAND, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880; exec. engr., Jan., 1894.

PEARS, STEUART EDMUND, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 7th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

PEARS, THOMAS CALDWELL, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resident in Nepal).—First commn., 29th May, 1872; served in the Afghan war, 1878-79, and with the Thal Chotiali field force (medal); on special pol. duty in Afghanistan in 1880; apptd. asst. commr., Merwara, Nov., 1882; served in the thagi dept. in Rajputana, and on boundary duty on the Mewar and Tonk border in 1884; pol. asst., March, 1886; settlt. officer under the Tonk durbar, Nov., 1886; pol. agent, Nov., 1891; settlt. officer, Tonk, Dec., 1891; pol. agent, Alwar, March, 1893, Johdpur, March, 1898; offg. resdt., Gwalior, May, 1899; ditto, Nepal, Feb., 1901.

PEARSE, HERBERT GEORGE, B.L., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 7th Nov., 1871, and served in the

N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; judge, small cause court. Allahabad, Dec., 1887; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1890; ret'd. May, 1898.

PEARSON, CHARLES, M.A., late Educ. Dept., Punjab.—Served in the dept. from March, 1861, as prof. and principal, Agra coll.; inspr. of schs., Nov., 1865; offg. dir. of public instrn. in 1873, 1876, and 1883; ret'd., March, 1886.

PEARSON, JAMES RAE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 10th Nov., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., May, 1898; dep. commr., Mar., 1900.

PEARSON, TINDAL ARTHUR, B.A., B.L. (chief presidency mag., Bengal).—Joined the service, 3rd April, 1878, as offg. reporter, Indian law reports; confd., Jan., 1880; acted subsequently as dep. supt. and rememberer of legal affairs, registrar of the diocese, and receiver of the high court; apptd. chief presidency mag. and judge of the court for trial of pilots, May, 1894.

PEASE, HENRY THOMAS, Veterinary Captain (principal, Veterinary Coll., Lahore).—Joined the home (service) 27th Aug., 1834; principal, Veterinary Coll., Lahore, Oct., 1896.

PECK, FRANCIS SAMUEL, Lieut.-Col., Indian Medl. Service (prof., Calcutta Medl. Coll.).—1st commn., 2nd April, 1880; in mily. employ till April, 1883; resdt. surg., Calcutta Medl. Coll., Oct., 1883; civil surg. at various stations, Oct., 1887, to Mar., 1899; prof. of midwifery, Calcutta Medl. Coll., Mar., 1900; is author of a work on ovariotomy.

PEDLER, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., F.R.S. (dir. of public instrn., Bengal).—Apptd. to the educn. dept., 22nd Jan., 1873; arrived, 8th May, 1873, and served as prof. of agricl. chemistry, presidency coll.; on special duty, eclipse expdn., March, 1875; meteorological reporter to govt. of Bengal, May, 1889, in addn. to his duties as prof.; principal, Presidency Coll., Nov., 1896; dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1899; C.I.E., Nov., 1901.

PEET, ARTHUR WILLIAM, late Forest Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1871, as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, April, 1872; conservator, Oct., 1891; ret'd., July, 1901.

PEILE, SIR JAMES BRAITHWAITE, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Bombay C.S. (member of the Council of India).—Educ. at Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. to the Bombay civil service after exam. of 1855; arrived 8th Oct., 1856, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty for inquiry into claims of thakurs of Bhaunagar on account of land and sea customs, May, 1859; specially apptd. to inquire into condition of talukdars, Ahmadabad, Jan., 1860; alienation settlt. officer, Gujarat, April, 1861; asst. to rev. commr., Dec., 1861; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, rev. dept., April, 1862; on special duty for carrying out provisions of bill for relief of the talukdars, Ahmadabad, Dec., 1862; commr. for revising subord. civil establs., April, 1866; registrar-gen. of assurances, July, 1866; dir. of public instrn., April, 1869; acting municipal commr., Bombay, May-Oct., 1872; acting pol. agent, Kathiawar, Jan., 1873; on duty at Delhi in connection with imperial assemblage, Dec., 1876; acting commr. in Sind, June, 1878; member of famine commn.,

1878-79; acting sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., March, 1879; from April, 1879, served as sec. in the rev., finl., and gen. depts., and acted as chief sec.; on special duty on famine commn. in England, 1879-80; on special duty in India, 1880 and 1881; member of council, Bombay, Dec., 1882; acted as govr. and prest. in council in March, 1885; temporary member of govr.-gen.'s council, Oct., 1886; retd., Nov., 1887; member of council of India, 12th Nov., 1887; vice-prest., 1894-95; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1888; member of royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895.

PEILE, SOLOMON CHARLES FREDERICK, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Police (inspr.-gen. police).—First commn., 9th Aug., 1874; served in the Afghan war of 1878-79 (medal), and in the Burmese expedn. of 1886-87 (medal and clasp); apptd. dir. of police supply and clothing, Upper Burma, March, 1887, the office being subsequently extended to the whole of Burma; dep. inspr.-gen., mil. police, June, 1890; inspr.-gen. of police, Feb., 1892; C.I.E., Jan., 1901.

PEMBERTON, ROBERT CHARLES BOILEAU, C.S.I., Major-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Lieut., Bengal engrs., 1853; joined the dept., May, 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and two clasps), and subsequently in the road dept., Oudh, as exec. engr.; asst. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., March, 1865; offg. suptg. engr., railway branch, Hyderabad, Sept., 1870; dir. of stores, railway branch, May, 1877; consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lucknow, 1880; chief engr., 1st class, March, 1886; dir.-gen. of railways and dep. sec., govt. of India, railway branch, Feb., 1887; sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1887; reverted to mil. dept., 1890; offg. member of viceroy's council, Feb., 1891, to April, 1892; retd. from the army, Nov., 1892; C.S.I., May, 1894.

PENNELL, CHARLES STUART, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Edinburgh univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 10th Dec., 1897, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

PENNINGTON, HENRY FRANCIS DUMARESO, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Westminster; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 21st Oct., 1875, and served in Oudh as asst. commr. and sub-judge; joint mag., April, 1887; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1890; also addl. judge, Moradabad, 1893-94; retd., April, 1901.

PENNINGTON, JAMES BURN, B.L., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Sedbergh Gr. Sch., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., sub-collr., sub-sec. to the board of rev., and acting collr. of sea customs at Madras; collr. and mag., July, 1875; retd., Sept., 1888.

PENNY, EDMUND, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to state railways; transfed. to Central Prov., Aug., 1875; exec. engr., May, 1883; planned and carried out scheme for Nagpur water-works, 1890; acted as asst. sec. to chief commr. in 1891; suptg. engr. and sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1899; C.I.E., May, 1900.

PENNY, JAMES CHAMPION, M.D., Dep.-Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Arrived, 3rd June, 1857; from Feb., 1863, served in the Punjab

at various stations as civil asst. surgn., and civil surgn. and supt. of jail; retd., June, 1887.

PENNYCUICK, JOHN, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Lieut., Madras engrs., 1863, 1858; arrived, 11th Nov., 1860; served in the dept. from April, 1862; exec. engr., Dec., 1866; served in the Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (medal); suptg. engr., Oct., 1881; dep. chief engr., and under sec. to govt., Jan., 1883; supt. of works, tank maintenance scheme, April, 1884; on special duty to England to select machinery for Periyar works project, July, 1888; chief engr. and sec. in the public works dept., July, 1890; member of legisl. council, Nov., 1893; C.S.I., Oct., 1895; retd., Jan., 1896; presdt. of Royal Indian Engrg. Coll., Cooper's Hill, Sept., 1896, to Sept., 1899.

PERCIVAL, PHILIP EDWARD, B.A., Incian C.S. (asst. coll. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

PERKINS, NORMAN CHICHESTER, Major, I.S.C., late Burma commn.—First commn., 13th Aug., 1879; served in Afghan campaign of 1880-81, and in Burma, 1886-89 (medal and clasp); joined the commn. as asst. commr., Aug., 1886; dep. commr., Oct., 1896; retd., April, 1900.

PERRAM, GEORGE JAMES, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Apptd. asst. engr., 21st Dec., 1868, and posted to Mysore; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transfed. to Central Prov., March, 1881; to Coorg, Jan., 1887; and to Burma, Feb., 1893; suptg. engr., Jan., 1896.

PERRIN, CHARLES, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 10th Oct., 1874, and employed on irrig. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1892; sanitary engr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, with rank of suptg. engr., July, 1893; supt. of practical training of passed students at Cooper's Hill Coll., 1896-7; retd., April, 1899.

PERROTT, FINCH (senr. clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office).—Junr. clerk, 24th Dec., 1880; senr. clerk, May, 1900.

PERROTT, HENRY RONALD TAYLOR SIMPSON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 13th Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

PERRY, FRANCIS FREDERIC, F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, (prof'r., Mayo hosp., Lahore).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1879; from March, 1882, served in the Punjab as civil surgn., prof'r., Lahore med. coll., and chemical examr. to govt.; prof'r. of surgery, and surgn. to Mayo hosp., Lahore, Dec., 1888; acted as prof'r., Calcutta med. coll., in 1894; hon. surgn. to Viceroy, Nov., 1901.

PERT, FREDERICK JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint sec. to rev. board, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 15th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Feb., 1893; settl. officer, Nov., 1894; dep. commr., July, 1898; joint sec. to rev. board, March, 1899.

PESTONJI, HORMASJI PATAK, B.A., L.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Entered the dept. as asst. engr., Feb., 1877; exec. engr., Nov., 1891.

PETERS, CHARLES EDWARD DANSEY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1820; arrived 21st Nov., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

PETERS, CHARLES JASPER, M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, as proby. asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1875; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

PETERS, EDWARD NICOLLS, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the Bombay public works dept. as exec. engr., 11th March, 1879; transf'd. to state railways, Feb., 1880; served in the Afghan war, 1880-81 (medal); posted to Burma, Sept., 1881; employed on Sind-Pishin railway, April, 1885; transf'd. to Central Prov., March, 1888; in 1891 acted as public works sec.; ret'd., Nov., 1895.

PETERSON, FRANCIS WILLIAM, late Mint Dept., Bombay.—Joined the mint dept., Calcutta; apptd. dep. assay master, Oct., 1881; dep. assay master, Bombay mint, Oct., 1887; ret'd., Nov., 1892.

PETERSON, FREDERICK WILLIAM VOYSEY, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Leicester Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 7th Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and dep. commr.; from May, 1877, served in the jud. dept., and was apptd. district and sessions judge, 1st grade, Sept., 1885; ret'd., April, 1889.

PETERSON, HERBERT PARSONS, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Loughborough Gr. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 10th Nov., 1875; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as district and sessions judge, Sylhet; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1887; district and sessions judge, April, 1893; ret'd., Feb., 1895.

PETERSON, JOHN CARLOS KENNEDY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 6th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

PETHERAM, SIR WILLIAM COMER, Kt., K.C., B.L.—Called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1869; chief justice of the high court, N.W. Prov., 1st Nov., 1884; chief justice, Calcutta, 24th March, 1886; ret'd. Nov., 1896; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Calcutta, 1887-90.

PETLEY, EATON WALLACE, C.I.E., Commander R.N. (dep. conservator, port of Calcutta).—Dep. conservator of the port of Calcutta, and river surveyor, Nov., 1881; port officer and master attendant, April, 1888; hon. A.D.C. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, and commander, Calcutta naval volunteers; C.I.E., Jan., 1897; hon. A.D.C. to Viceroy, Nov., 1901.

PETRE, FRANCIS LORAINNE, B.L., late Indian C.S., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Oscott Coll., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 15th Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; off. under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Aug., 1882; junr. sec., board of rev., N.W. Prov.,

Nov., 1882; from April, 1885, served as asst. agent to govr.-gen. in Central India; asst. commr., March, 1888; joint mag., Dec., 1888; joint sec., board of rev., Jan., 1891; mag. and collr., July, 1891; ret'd., March, 1900.

PETTY, ALBERT JAMES (second class clerk, store dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 16th July, 1883.

PHEAR, SIR JOHN BUDD, Kt., M.A., B.L., F.G.S.—Educ. at Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; graduated 6th wrangler in 1847, and was elected fellow, and afterwards apptd. mathematical lecturer and asst. tutor of Clare Coll.; called to the bar at the Inner Temple in 1854; judge of the high court, Calcutta, 1864-76; knighted in 1877; chief justice of Ceylon, 1877-79; unsuccessfully contested the Honiton divn. of Devon in 1885, and the Tavistock divn. in 1886; author of 'The Aryan Village in India and Ceylon,' 'International Trade,' and other works.

PHELAN, ALFRED BERNARD, B.A., L.C.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (supig. engr.).—Apptd., 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrig. work in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1878; suptg. engr., March, 1898.

PHELIPS, HENRY VIVIAN MEJENDIE, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; entered the service 23rd Sept., 1881, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1895.

PHELPS, EDWIN ASHLEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Clifton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.

PHELPS, WILLIAM EDMONDSON (asst. supt., India Store Depot).—Entered the royal artillery, 30th Oct., 1869; from Feb., 1876, served in the Bombay and Bengal ordnance depts., including duty with the Quetta field force, 1878-80; supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depot, 17th June, 1884; asst. supt., 25th Dec., 1893.

PHIBBS, ALFRED (second class clerk, Mil. Dept., India Office).—Educ. at Stourbridge Gr. Sch.; clerk, and class, 16th April, 1894.

PHILLIMORE, JOHN ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 12th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. officer; joint mag. and collr., Nov., 1896.

PHILLIPS, AUGUSTUS MARSHALL, Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Ensign, Bombay infantry, June, 1855; arrived, 13th Dec., 1855, and served with the Persian expeditry. force, 1856-57 (medal and clasp); from March, 1863, served as asst. pol. supt., Savantvadi, Mahi Kantha, and Kathiawar, and held various acting appts.; pol. supt., Savantvadi, March, 1889; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

PHILLIPS, FRANCIS ANGELO THEODORE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 11th Nov., 1880, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and personal asst. to the chief commr.; judge, small cause court, Aug., 1891; commr. of excise, Oct., 1891; dep. commr., March, 1892.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM WATKIN, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888;

arrived, 25th Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., July, 1897.

PHIROZE HOSHANG DASTUR, M.A. (and presy. mag., Bombay).—Entered the service 3rd May, 1880, and served as dep. collr. and mag.; presy. mag., March, 1892.

PIGGOTT, THEODORE CARO, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sessions judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893; dep. commr., July, 1900; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1901.

PIGOT, JONES QUAIN, B.A., B.L., late Calcutta High Court.—Advocate, high court, Bombay, 1870–82; puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, 17th July, 1882; vice-chancellor, Calcutta univ., 1892–93; ret'd., March, 1896.

PIKE, HERBERT WATSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Honiton Gr. Sch., Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 17th Nov., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; under sec. to govt., Nov. 1890; joint mag., April, 1893; dep. commr., March, 1896; mag. and collr., Jan., 1900.

PILCHER, JESSE GRIGGS, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1860; from March, 1866, served as jail supt. at Allahabad and Agra, offg. sanitary commr. for Bengal, and civil surgn. at various stations; dep. surgn.-gen., Central Prov., March, 1890; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Bengal, April, 1893; ditto, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1894; ret'd., March, 1895.

PILGRIM, HERBERT WILSON, M.B., Major, Indian Med. Service (supt., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta).—First commn., Sept., 1886; in mil. employ to June, 1890; then as acting and substantive civil surgn. at various stations; rest. surgn., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, April, 1892; surgn.-supt., presidency gen. hosp., June, 1898.

PIM, ALAN WILLIAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. settlt. offr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Leipzig Univ. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 4th Dec., 1895, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., May, 1898; asst. settlt. offr., Nov., 1898; author of a monograph on woollen fabrics.

PINHEY, ALEXANDER FLEETWOOD, C.I.E., Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resdt., Udaipur).—First commn., 10th May, 1882; from Feb., 1886, served as asst. pol. agent, Banswara, asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti, and supt. for control of Moghias; pol. agent, Haraothi, March, 1885; acting pol. agent, Baghelkhand, May, 1895; confid., April, 1897; resdt., Udaipur, April, 1900; C.I.E., Nov., 1901.

PINHEY, ARTHUR FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1884, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and in Burma as asst. commr. (1888–90); head asst., June, 1891; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1896; collr. and mag., March, 1901.

PINHEY, EDWARD, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Roy. Indian Engrg. Coll.; joined the service 1st Oct., 1876, and served as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1886.

PINHEY, HUGH THEODORE, M.I.E.E. (supt., India Telegraph Dept.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., Jan., 1879; in charge of telegraphs in Manipur expdn., April, 1891; supt., June, 1891; India medal and three clasps.

PINKERTON, JOHN, M.D., Surgn.-Major-Gen., late Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 4th Aug., 1855; served with Indian navy, during the Persian war in 1857; on civil and mil. duty, 1858–61; acting supt. of vaccn., Sind, March, 1863; supt.-gen. of vaccn., Bombay presidency, April, 1863; surgn. in European gen. hosp. and dep. sanitary commr.; dep. surgn.-gen., Sind, Aug., 1882; surgn.-gen. of Bombay, Feb., 1888; member, legisl. council, June, 1892; ret'd., Feb., 1893.

PIPON, PHILIP JAMES GRIFFITHS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

PITCHER, DUNCAN GEORGE, Col., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, as cornet, Bombay light cavalry, 3rd Sept., 1859, having previously served in the royal marine light infantry and artillery, 1856–59; served from Aug., 1868, in the Oudh commn. as asst. commr., and small cause court judge, and registrar, Lucknow; dep. dir. of agric. and commerce for Oudh, June, 1882, and for N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1886; dep. commr., June, 1887; dir. of land records, Gwalior, Nov., 1890; returned to Europe, Sept., 1897.

PITMAN, CHARLES EDWARD, C.I.E., M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. supt., 16th Jan., 1868; on special duty in connection with Lushai expdn., 1871, and with Afghanistan field force, 1878–79; C.I.E., 1879; supt., April, 1880; chief supt., Punjab, April, 1891; dir.-genl., Jan., 1899; ret'd., May, 1900.

PITTAR, CHARLES WILLIAM ERSKINE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 31st Oct., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam (1889–95) as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1895; district and sess. judge, Dec., 1896.

PLACE, GEORGE WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B., B.L., late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 29th Nov., 1875, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; from Jan., 1886, served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, March, 1893; ret'd., Nov., 1900.

PLANCK, CHARLES, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Gen.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. estabt., 4th Aug., 1855; civil asst. surgn., Fatehgarh, July, 1859; supt., central prison, Agra, Feb., 1862; inspr.-gen. of prisons, British Burma, Oct., 1864; sanitary commr., N.W. Prov., April, 1868; also in Oudh, April, 1877; also supt.-gen. of vaccn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan., 1879; ret'd., Aug., 1886.

PLATEL, JOHN JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Calcutta

Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 14th Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1900.

PLAYFAIR, ARCHIBALD LEWIS, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, Aug., 1856; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); and with the Haidarabad contingent, 1862-73; served subsequently in civil employ as cantonmt. mag., small cause court judge, and jail supt.; ret'd., Feb., 1887.

PLOWDEN, CHARLES HENRY, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., Feb., 1854; arrived, 28th April, 1854; served during the Mutiny, 1858-59; served from May, 1860, in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., commr. of excise, &c., and dep. commr.; offd. as commr., Jabalpur divn., 1892; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1893.

PLOWDEN, SIR HENRY MEREDYTH, Kt., B.A., B.L., late Jud. Dept., Punjab.—Served in the Punjab from 17th March, 1871, as govt. advocate and offg. judge of the chief court; confd. as judge, chief court, Jan., 1878; knighted, Feb., 1887; ret'd., April, 1894.

PLOWDEN, SIR TREVOR JOHN CHICHELE, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 31st Oct., 1868, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under-sec.; offg. under-sec., govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1873; insp.-gen. of police, Assam, Nov., 1876; sec. to the prison conference, Jan., 1877; sec., govt. of India, foreign dept., Dec., 1878; rest., and pol. agent, Turkish Arabia, March, 1880; consul-gen., Baghdad, June, 1880; commr. of Ajmir-Merwara, June, 1885; rest. in Mewar, Nov., 1885; rest. in Kashmir, Nov., 1887; commr., Haidarabad assigned districts, Nov., 1888; rest., Haidarabad, March, 1892; C.S.I., Jan., 1893; K.C.S.I., May, 1898; author of several reports on journeys in Kurdistan, &c.; ret'd., Sept., 1900.

PLOWDEN, WALTER FRANCIS COURTENAY CHICHELE, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh (cantonmt. mag., Fyzabad).—First commn., 19th Oct., 1872; served in the Jowaki expdn., and Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and clasp); offg. cantonmt. mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Sept., 1888; confd., May, 1890.

PLOWDEN, SIR WILLIAM CHICHELE, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury Coll., 1852; arrived, 20th Sept., 1852; from Nov., 1853, served in the rev. and jud. dept. in the N.W. Prov., Oudh, and Punjab; dep. commr., Aug., 1858, and joint mag. and dep. collr., first grade, March, 1863; sec., board of rev., N.W. Prov., July, 1864; on special duty on the census report, April, 1866, and again, May, 1872; mag. and collr., March, 1870; civil and sessions judge, March, 1877; on special duty at Calcutta as prest. of census committee, Jan., 1878; commr., Meerut divn., Sept., 1879; offg. member, board of rev., Aug., 1880; census commr. for India, March, 1881; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1881; ret'd., July, 1885; M.P. for Wolverhampton (West), 1886-92.

PLOWMAN, ALBERT CHARLES, India Office (second class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Educ. at Bedford Modern Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 12th June, 1901.

POCOCK, REYNOLDS JOHN MICHEL, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Served as a volunteer during the Mutiny; joined the dept., 26th Jan., 1861; asst. district supt., Oct., 1866; district supt., Sept., 1872; ret'd., April, 1895.

POGSON, CHARLES ALBERT, Salt Dept., Bombay (asst. collr.).—Entered the service, 1st Dec., 1875, and served in the marine postal dept.; asst. collr. of salt rev., Dec., 1877.

POLLEN, ARTHUR DANIEL, B.A., LL.D., B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Koyal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Oct., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge and joint sessions judge; special judge under the Deccan agriculturists relief act, Jan., 1880; judge and sessions judge, Jan., 1884; ret'd., March, 1894.

POLLEN, JOHN, B.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (commr. of customs, Bombay).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1871, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine relief duty, 1874 and 1878; on special settlt. duty, March, 1879; acting under sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Dec., 1880; asst. commr. and branch insp.-gen. of registration and stamps in Sind, April, 1881; also Sindi translr. to govt., Nov., 1881; acting collr. and mag., Haidarabad, May, 1887; junr. collr. and mag., March, 1892; senr. collr., June, 1896; commr. of customs, &c., April, 1900; author of 'Rhymes from the Russian,' &c.

PONTIFEX, SIR CHARLES, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Legal Adviser, India Office.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, May, 1854; judge of the high court, Calcutta, May, 1872; ret'd. in 1882 on appt. as legal adviser to sec. of state for India; legal adviser and solicitor, May, 1887; ret'd., April, 1892; K.C.I.E., May, 1892.

POPE, ALFRED WILLIAM UGLOW, India Public Works Dept. (traffic supt.).—Apptd. to the Public Works Dept., May, 1878, and posted to state railways; asst. traffic supt., July, 1879; traffic supt., 1880.

POPE, JOHN VAN SOMEREN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., Burma).—Educ. at Trinity Hall, Cambridge (scholar); joined the Bengal educl. service, 17th June, 1876, and served as prof. at the Patna Coll. and presidency coll., Calcutta, and as principal, Dacca coll., Jan. 1878; inspr. of schs., Behar circle, June, 1883; fellow of the Calcutta univ., 1886; apptd. dir. of public instrn. in Burma, May, 1889; presdt. of the Educl. Syndicate, Jan., 1897.

POPE, RICHARD RODNEY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Oratory Sch., Edgbaston, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 1st Nov., 1879; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag., and in Assam as asst. commr., and district and sessions judge; district and sessions judge, Jessor, March, 1895; Dinajpore, May, 1896.

POPE, THOMAS ARCHDALE, Survey of India (asst. surv. genl.).—Joined the service, July, 1880; 2nd asst. supt., Sept., 1881; asst. supt., Survey of India, Oct., 1886; dep. supt., Mar., 1891; supt., Oct., 1899.

POPE, THOMAS HENRY, M.D., C.M., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (supt., ophthalmic

hospl., Madras).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1878, and served in Afghanistan, 1879-81 (medal); acting residency surgn., Travancore, April, 1885; civil surgn., Negapatam, Oct., 1885; asst. physician, gen. hosp., Jan., 1888; also prof. at the med. coll., to Dec., 1888; district surgn. and supt. of jail, S. Arcot, March, 1892; supt., ophthalmic hosp., Madras, April, 1894.

POPERT, EDWIN PHILIP, late Forest Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 16th Dec., 1872, and was employed in Burma as asst. conservator of forests; dep. conservator, July, 1880; conservator, March, 1887; transf. to Madras, Jan., 1892; ret. d., March, 1900.

PORTEOUS, ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Harrow; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 16th Jan., 1879, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1890; offg. pol. agent and supt., Manipur, Dec., 1892, and again, July, 1897; offg. pol. officer, N. Lushai, July, 1894.

PORTEOUS, CHARLES ARKKOLL, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 26th Nov., 1856, and was employed in the suppression of the Mutiny, 1857-59 (medal and two clasps); served in the dept. from May, 1860, as asst. supt., and supt.; in charge of the Madras police at the imperial assemblage, Delhi, Dec., 1876; dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1883; inspr.-gen. of jails, Jan., 1889; inspr.-gen. of police, March, 1891; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1894; C.I.E., May, 1895.

PORTEOUS, WILLIAM, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and pol. agent; from Jan., 1874, was employed in Mysore as asst. commr., and in various offg. appts.; from April, 1878, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and acting collr. of salt rev.; junr. collr. and mag. Nov., 1886; senr. collr., April, 1890; acted as commr. in 1894; ret. d., Nov., 1896.

PORTER, ALEXANDER, M.D., F.R.C.S.I., M.R.I.A., Surgn.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn, 31st March, 1865; held the civil surgery of Akola, 1866-74, and acted as sanitary commr., Berar, 1870-71; from April, 1874, served in Madras, as chemical examr. to govt., surgn., 4th district, and prof. in the med. coll.; fellow of Madras univ., 1883; principal and prof. of medicine, med. coll., Dec., 1886; reverted to mil. duty, July, 1890; ret. d. July, 1895; author of 'Notes on the Pathology of Famine Diseases.'

PORTER, FORTESCUE WILLOUGHBY, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Felsted Gr. Sch., and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 21st Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settlt. officer, and joint mag.; dep. commr., Aug., 1885; mag. and collr., Nov., 1886; inspr.-gen. of police, Aug., 1891; ret. d., May, 1894.
