WHITLOCK, CHARLES JAMES TOLLER, Col., I.S.C., late Madras Police Dept.—First commn., 20th Feb., 1858; served in the suppression of the Mutiny (medal); joined the dept., April, 1867, as proby. asst. supt.; confd., Nov., 1868; resumed mil. duty, Aug., 1871; rejoined the dept., April, 1874; supt., Nov., 1881; reverted to mil. duty, April, 1895.

WHITMORE, JOHN, B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 31st Dec., 1868; served in rev. and jud. depts., Bengal, and was apptd. joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1878; offg. jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, Dec., 1881; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1885; ret'd., March, 1895.

WHITTEN, RICHARD JOHN, late N. India Salt Rev. Dept.—Joined the service in Feb., 1862, as asst. patrol; dep. inspr., internal branch, Jan., 1867; personal asst. to commr. of inland customs, Oct., 1869; collr. of customs, Sept., 1871; employed temporarily under govt. of Bombay in salt inquiries in Cutch and Kathiawar; dep. commr. of inland customs, Dec., 1879; at various periods has offd. as commr. of N. India salt rev.; ret'd., Aug., 1898.

WHITTLE, GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 28th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., Dec., 1895.

WHITTY, JOHN TARLTON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., New Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 29th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

WHITTY, RICHARD FRENCH LAWRENCE, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Felsted Sch. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

WHITWORTH, GEORGE CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (judge and legal remembrancer, Bombay).—Educ. at Sandicroft Sch., Northwich, and Queen's Coll., Liverpool; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 5th Dec., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acting under sec. to govt. of Bombay in the pol., &c., depts., Jan., 1876; under sec. to govt. in the public works dept., for relief purposes, 1877-78; second asst. collr., Feb., 1881; jud. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, Nov., 1886; judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1887; acting sec., pol., &c., depts., 1893-94; acting jud. commr., Sind, April, 1897; confd. July, 1898; judge and legal remembrancer, March, 1901; author of 'The Theory of Relativity for the purpose of Judicial Evidence,' an 'Anglo-Indian Dictionary,' 1885, the 'Rajkumar Law Lectures,' 1888; 'A Letter to Magistrates,' 1896, and 'Presumptions and Inferences,' 1899.

WHYMPER, FRANK, postal dept., Govt. of India (dep. postmr.-gen., Allahabad).—Entered the service, 20th March, 1872; dep. postmr.-gen., April, 1894.

WICKHAM, WILLIAM ALLEN (supervisor, store dept., India Office).—Apptd. 2nd grade supervisor, India store depot, 23rd Sept., 1889.

WIGHT, JOHN KNOX, B.A., late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Educ. at Queen's Coll. Sch., Trinidad, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 4th Dec., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, 1874; transfed. to Assam, Dec., 1874, and served as asst. commr. and asst. inspr. of immigrants; dep. commr., March, 1884; returned to duty in Bengal, May, 1890; district and sessions judge, June, 1890; ret'd., July, 1900.

WIGNER, JOHN MURCH, B.A., B.Sc. (senr. clerk, Store Dept., India Office).—Educ. at King's Lynn Gr. Sch., and London Univ.; temporary clerk, 17th July, 1862; junr. clerk, Jan., 1867; senr. clerk, July, 1885.

WILBERFORCE, SAMUEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 17th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

WILCOX, FREDERICK, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 25th May, 1862, as asst. supt. of police; district supt., June, 1873; ret'd., March, 1895.

WILD, ALBERT EDWARD (conservator, India Forest Dept.).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1869, as asst. conservator, and served in Burma and the Punjab; dep. conservator, Oct., 1876; conservator, Jan., 1892; in charge, central circle, N.W. Prov., April, 1892; dir. of forest sch., Nov., 1893; conservator of forests, Bengal, April, 1894.

WILD, ARTHUR CLEMENT, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 24th Nov., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr.

WILD, CHARLES EDMUND, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 17th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., July, 1898; dep. commr., Aug., 1900; mag. and collr., Jan., 1901.

WILKIE, DAVID, M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (statistical offr. to govt. of India, sanitary and med. depts.).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1873, and was employed as civil surgeon, and jail supt. at various stations in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; statistical officer to govt. of India in sanitary and med. depts., Nov., 1886.

WILKINS, CECIL ANSDELL, late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 26th Nov., 1868; served in rev. and jud. depts., Bengal; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1884; registrar, high court, March, 1885; district and sessions judge, April, 1886; member of Bengal legisl. council, March, 1894; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Dec., 1894; judge, high court, Calcutta, Sept., 1899; compiled revised edns. of the high court's rules and orders, in 1881 and 1883; ret'd., April, 1900.

WILKINS, THOMAS JAMES HACKETT, Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (dist. surgn. Malabar).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1873; arrived, 29th April, 1874, and was employed in Madras under the mil. dept. (including service in the Burma campaign) and as acting civil surgn.; district surgn., Anantapur, Sept., 1887; Ganjam, Nov., 1894; Malabar, May, 1895; and superintendent of lunatic asylum, Calicut.

WILKINS, WILLIAM HENRY, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India.—Joined the Bengal artillery, Dec., 1857; arrived, Jan., 1858, and served with the Cawnpore field force; studied at Thomason Coll., 1861-62; served in the rev. survey from April, 1863, as asst. surveyor; dep. supt., Oct., 1878; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; asst. surveyor-gen., Sept., 1892; offg. dep. surveyor-gen. and offg. dir. of Bengal surveys, April, 1895; ret'd. from the survey dept., Dec., 1895; placed on unemployed supy. list, Oct., 1898.

WILKINSON, CHARLES ROBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

WILKINSON, FRANCIS HENRY, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Milton Abbas Sch., Blackheath Proprietary Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 10th Nov., 1863; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting under sec. to govt.; on special duty as arbitrator of boundary disputes, 1870; sub-collr. and joint mag., Salem, April, 1878; district and sessions judge, S. Malabar, June, 1882; judge of the high court, Madras, Nov., 1887; ret'd., Feb., 1893.

WILKINSON, THOMAS ORMEROD, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Chudleigh Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. registrar of the chief court; jud. asst., Dec., 1883; district judge, 1st grade, Nov., 1884; divl. judge, Jan., 1887; ret'd., Sept., 1890.

WILKINSON, WALTER HUGH JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 29th Nov., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

WILLCOCKS, ALEXANDER JOHN, M.D., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service as asst. surgn., 30th Sept., 1873; served on mil., civil, and jail duty, and was apptd. civil surgn., April, 1877; offg. profir., Calcutta med. coll., 1892; hon. surgn. to vice-roy, Jan., 1894; depd. to Dholpur state in 1894; civil surgeon, Agra, Nov., 1894; Naini Tal, March, 1897; ret'd., April, 1901.

WILLCOCKS, JOHN, India Public Works Dept. (sec. to govt., Bombay).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the service, Nov., 1879, and posted to state railways; asst. engr., Feb., 1880; exec. engr., Nov., 1891; under sec. to govt., Bombay, Oct., 1897; sec. to govt., July, 1899.

WILLIAM, JAMES HENRY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Nov., 1874, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; asst. engr., March, 1875; exec. engr., July, 1884.

WILLIAMS, ALBERT, LL.M., Indian C.S. (dep. sec. to govt. of India, Home Dept.).—Educ. at Uppingham, Clifton, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3th Jan., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and under sec. to govt.; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., May, 1894; pol. agent, Hyderabad, Nov., 1895; pol. agent, Quetta, May, 1897; under-sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Jan., 1899; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1901.

WILLIAMS, VEN. ARTHUR ACHESON, M.A., Eccles. Dept., Madras (archdeacon of Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. junr. chaplain, 18th Oct., 1880; chaplain, Nov., 1880; archdeacon of Madras, Sept., 1900.

WILLIAMS, SIR EDWARD CHARLES SPARSHOTT, K.C.I.E., Gen., R.E.—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., New Cross, and Addiscombe; first commn., 1848; adjutant of engrs., Fort William, and studying machinery in Calcutta mint, 1850; extra asst. sec., mil. board, 1851; supt., iron bridge yard, Calcutta, 1851-52; served with the Pegu expdn., 1852-53 (medal and clasp); supt., Pegu topogr. survey, 1854-56; principal, civil engrg. coll., Calcutta, 1856-60; member of senate, Calcutta univ.; acted as under sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., during the Mutiny; principal, Thomason Coll., 1861-62; under sec., govt. of India, public works dept., 1863-69; member of Bengal sanitary commn.; in charge of office of inspr.-gen. of forests for one year; acting mint-master and commr. of paper currency, Bombay, 1867; dep. sec., govt. of India, public works dept. (railway branch), 1869-74, and 1877-78; dir. of state railways, 1874-77; C.I.E., Jan., 1878; dep. govt. dir. of Indian railway companies, India office, March, 1880; govt. dir., March, 1892 to Aug., 1897; K.C.I.E., June, 1893.

WILLIAMS, GEORGE ROBERT CARLISLE, B.A., late Indian C.S., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 21st Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; dep. commr., Jhansi, March, 1883; mag. and collr., Dec., 1886; commr., Feb., 1894; author of 'An Historical and Statistical Memoir of Dehra Dun,' and 'Gazetteer of Dehra Dun' (part); retd., Aug., 1900.

WILLIAMS, HENRY CLISSOLD, Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Haileybury; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 25th Oct., 1871, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; on famine duty in Bengal, 1874; from Nov., 1878, served in Assam as asst. commr.; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, &c., Assam, Sept., 1885; returned to Bengal as mag. and collr., Nov., 1891; acting commr., April, 1894; prest. of mining commn., May, 1895; prest. of labour enquiry commn., Dec., 1895; chairman of Calcutta Corporation, April, 1896; commr., Jan., 1898.

WILLIAMS, RALPH KING, late India Public Works Dept.—From Nov., 1869, served in the office of the consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, and was apptd. asst. examr. of guaranteed railway accts. in 1872; served in Madras from Feb., 1873, and was apptd. dep. examr., May, 1876; examr., Indus valley state railway accts., Aug., 1880; transf. to Nagpur, March, 1885; examr., guaranteed railway accts.,

Lahore, April, 1885; examr., N.W. railway accts., Jan., 1886; retd., June, 1898.

WILLIAMS, WILLIAM, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. supt., 16th Jan., 1868; arrived, 29th Feb., 1868; supt., April, 1880; held charge of various divns., including Indore, Bombay, and Nagpur; retd., 1890; author of 'Manual of Telegraphy.'

WILLIAMS-WYNN, ARTHUR W., B.A., B.L.—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1882; apptd. asst. private sec. (unpaid) to the sec. of state (Viscount Cross), Aug., 1886; asst. private sec., Dec., 1889; private sec., June to Aug., 1892.

WILLIAMSON, FREDERICK LENNOX JERVIS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 5th Jan., 1895, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; J.P., Sept., 1896; transf. to Mandalay, Feb., 1899.

WILLIS, JOHN (clerk, funds dept., India office).—Writer, 3rd Nov., 1862; junr. book-keeper, Nov., 1868; asst. book-keeper, Dec., 1881; clerk, Oct., 1892.

WILLOCK, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 12th March, 1870, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to the salt commn., Jan., 1876; from March, 1879, served in the salt dept. as dep. commr., and was also employed in suppressing the disturbances in the Gudum Hills, 1879; collr. and mag., Dec., 1888; collr. and agent, Vizagapatam, July, 1890; acting member of rev. board, July, 1895; collr., Vizianagram Zemindari, Nov., 1897; retd., 1898.

WILLOUGHBY, MICHAEL WEEKES, C.S.I., Lieut.-Gen., I.S.C., late Bombay Secretariat.—First commn., Bombay army, June, 1849; acted as sec. to govt. of Bombay in the mil., marine, and ecclesi. depts., from May, 1880, and was confd., Feb., 1881; returned to Europe, 1888.

WILSON, JAMES, M.A., late Indian Educ. Dept.—Educ. at Ballinasloe Coll., Ranelagh Inst., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; prof., cathedral mission coll., Calcutta, Jan., 1870; asst., geological survey of India, Sept., 1871; prof., Hugli coll., March, 1873; Dacca coll., Jan., 1874; Patna coll., May, 1875; from Jan., 1879, served in Assam as inspr. of schools, and was apptd. dir. of public instrn., Assam, Jan., 1889; inspr. of Sch., Patna, Mar., 1899; has edited Wilson's 'Dynamics'; retd., Feb., 1900.

WILMER, JOHN RANDAL, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India.—Joined the service as lieut., royal artillery 22nd June, 1859; arrived, 27th Oct., 1860; served in the mil. dept., and in Aug., 1869, was apptd. asst. supt., topogr. survey; dep. supt., July, 1882; in charge of Baluchistan survey party, Oct., 1885, to Oct., 1886; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Jan., 1896.

WILSON, SIR ARTHUR, K.C.I.E. (legal adviser, India office).—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 17th June, 1862; puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, 1878; vice-chancellor, Calcutta univ., 1880-88; prest. of the Crawford enquiry commn. at Poona,

1888-89; legal adviser and solicitor, India office, 27th April, 1892; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1898.

WILSON, CHARLES ROBERT, M.A. (principal, Patna coll.).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; entered the service, April, 1887, apptd. profir., Dacca coll., May, 1887; apptd. profir. presidency coll., Calcutta, April, 1895; principal, Patna coll., Jan., 1900; editor of 'Early Annals of the English in Bengal' and of 'Inscriptions on Tombs and Monuments in Bengal.'

WILSON, DAVID, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Hutcheson's Gr. Sch., Glasgow, Univ. of Glasgow, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 4th Feb., 1886, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Oct., 1894; judge, small cause court, Moulmein, Jan., 1898.

WILSON, FREDERICK ALEXANDER, Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., as licut., Bengal artillery, 8th June, 1860; extra A.D.C. to viceroy, Dec., 1869; from Nov., 1870, served under the foreign dept. as guardian and tutor to the Nawab of Jaora, and as pol. asst. in Jaora, in Central India, and at Tehri; tutor to the Maharaja of Mysore, June, 1876; 1st asst. rest., Haidarabad, Oct., 1879; pol. agent, Oct., 1881; agent with the king of Oudh, and supt. of pol. pensions, March, 1882; boundary settlt. officer and asst. to pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Jan., 1883; offd. as rest., Nepal, and pol. agent, Bhartpur and Karauli; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Oct., 1886; commr., Ajmir, Feb., 1894; rest., Persian Gulf, May, 1894; served in the Bhutan campaign of 1864-65 (medal and clasp), the Hazara campaign of 1868, and the Black Mountain expdn. (clasp); returned to Europe, 1897.

WILSON, FREDERICK JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Entd. the dept., Sept., 1879, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr. under sec. to govt., irrigation branch.

WILSON, GEORGE, Commander, R.I.M., Marine Dept. (dep. director).—Commander, Indian marine, Aug., 1878; port officer, Bassein, April, 1882; acting asst. dir. of Royal Indian marine, May, 1889; port officer, Rangoon, Sept., 1890; port officer, Bombay and offg. dep. dir. of Royal Indian marine, Aug., 1896, to March, 1900.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (director of railway traffic and dep. sec. to govt. of India).—Apptd., May, 1875, and posted to state railways; asst. dir., N.E. system, 1877 and 1879; asst. sec., railway branch of public works dept., Bengal, 1878; exec. engr., May, 1883; served in Soudan expdn. (medal), and was employed on Soudan railway, 1884-85; asst. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1888; dep. manager, N.W. railway, April, 1891; on special duty in Ceylon, Oct.-Dec., 1895; manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand Railway, Dec., 1898; director of railway traffic and dep. sec. to govt. of India, June, 1901.

WILSON, JAMES, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (settlt. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Perth Acad. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam., of 1873; arrived, 14th Nov., 1875, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; offg. under sec. to govt., 1881 and 1884; offg.

senr. sec. to finl. commr. in 1885 and 1886; dep. commr., Sept., 1890; settlt. commr., Nov., 1899; member of legisl. council, Punjab, Jan., 1900; C.S.I., Nov., 1901; author of works on tribal customs in the Gurgaon and Sirsa districts, and of the Sirsa settlt. report.

WILSON, JOHN HATTON, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Bengal.—Proby. asst. engr., 5th March, 1856; served in the public works dept. in the Central Prov., and temporarily in Oudh (1865-67), as asst. engr. and exec. engr., also in mil. works branch, 1879; suptg. engr., and transfld. to Bengal, Dec., 1881; ret'd., 1890.

WILSON, ROBERT HENRY, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; offg. under sec. to the govt. of Bengal, Dec., 1870; inspr. of registrn. offices, July, 1872; offg. sec., board of rev., 1877 and 1879; offg. commr., Bardwan divn., Aug., 1884; employed under the public works dept. on the commn. in connection with the Orissa canals, 1884; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., Feb., 1886; ret'd., Sept., 1887.

WILSON, THOMAS CORBY, Indian C.S. (settlt. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 26th Nov., 1883, and served as asst. commr., Central Prov.; asst. settlt. officer, June, 1886; settlt. officer, May, 1888; transfld. to Burma as dep. commr., Feb., 1891; dir. of land records and agric., Aug., 1892; settlt. commr., Jan., 1900.

WILSON, WILLIAM, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Marischal College, Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1859; served in the Madras rev. and jud. dept., and was apptd. district and sessions judge, Kistna, Feb., 1874, and collr. and mag., June, 1877; dir. of rev. settlt. and agric., Nov., 1882; addl. member, board of rev., April, 1883; fellow of Madras univ., 1885; acting sec. to govt. of Madras in the rev. dept., April, 1885; member of legisl. council, Sept., 1885; acting chief sec. to govt. of Madras, Oct., 1885; member of board of rev., April, 1887; 1st member and commr. of land rev. settlt., &c., Jan., 1889; ret'd., Oct., 1889.

WILSON, WILLIAM HENRY, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 22nd Jan., 1857, and was employed on mil. duties till April, 1864, when he was apptd. acting asst. pol. agent, Sadra; from Nov., 1864, served as asst. supt. of police, subord. mag., supt. of railway police, and district supt.; commr. of police, Bombay, July, 1888; reverted to mil. duty, Sept., 1894.

WILSON, WILLIAM HERBERT, Ph.D. (Heidelberg) (profir., presy. coll., Madras).—Served from 4th Feb., 1875, as profir. of physical science, presidency coll.; fellow of Madras univ., 1880; in addn., registrar of Madras univ., March, 1886; profir. of chemistry, presidency coll., Nov., 1892.

WILSON-JONSTON, JOSEPH, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 6th Dec., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

WILTSHIRE, CHARLES PALMER BLUETT, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Proprietary Sch., Islington, City of London Sch., King's Coll., London Univ., and Middle Temple;

apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served as asst. collr. and mag., acting port officer at Karwar, and supt., timber depot, Kanara; boundary commr. between Kanara and Mysore, May, 1873; on famine duty, Dharwar, in 1876, 1877, and 1878; in charge of Huzur treasury in 1880 and 1881; 1st asst. collr., Sept., 1886; also forest settl. officer, Poona, June, 1891; ret'd., Aug., 1894; transld. the preliminary chapter of Mill's 'Political Economy' into Marathi.

WIMBERLEY, REGINALD JUSTUS, B.L., Col., late I.S.C.—Ensign, 3rd Bengal European regt., 1859; served in the Punjab police force, 1861-67; commissariat dept., March-Aug., 1867; from Aug., 1867, served in Bengal as district supt. of police; in command of police force to quell insurrection in the tributary mehals, May, 1868; 1st asst. supt., Port Blair and Nicobars, April, 1872; dep. supt., Dec., 1884; ret'd., Dec., 1887.

WINCHESTER, CHARLES BLAKE, B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, 1877; 2nd asst. collr., Nov., 1886; 1st asst., March, 1890; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1893; senr. collr., Oct., 1896; ret'd., Dec., 1898.

WINCHESTER, ERNEST CHARLES (staff clerk, registry and record dept., India Office).—Writer, India office, 16th Dec., 1872; clerk, 2nd class, Nov., 1878; staff clerk, May, 1901.

WINGATE, SIR ANDREW, K.C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Bombay).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, Kensington Gr. Sch., Univ. of London, and Univ. of Heidelberg; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; in pol. charge of the Jath state, 1876; addl. sec. in the famine dept., Mysore, Aug., 1877; asst. sec. to famine commn., Aug., 1878; C.I.E., June, 1879; settl. officer in Udaipur state, Aug., 1879; offg. rest., Mewar, and settl. officer, Kashmir state, Feb., 1887; junr. collr. and mag., Kanara, July, 1890; acting collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Oct., 1890; also acting commr. of customs, &c., April, 1891; settl. officer, Udaipur state, 1891-93; senr. collr., Sept., 1893; addl. mem. of legisl. council, July, 1896, and again, Feb., 1898; offd. as commr. in Sind, Dec., 1896-June, 1897; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1898; sec. to govt., plague dept., and plague commr., April, 1900; commr., Aug., 1900; addl. member of legisl. council, Oct., 1900.

WINGATE, JOHN BRUCE, Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., Burma).—Educ. at Old Aberdeen Gymnasium, Aberdeen Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 28th Nov., 1890, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1895; under sec. to govt. of Burma, May, 1897; sec. to govt., Jan., 1900.

WINGFIELD, COMMANDER G. T., R.N. (supt., India store depot).—Apptd. supt. of the India store depot, 10th Sept., 1898.

WINMILL, GEORGE, India Public Works Dept. (locomotive supt., North-Western railway).—Joined the service as second sub-engr., Indus valley railway, 17th Nov., 1875; transfld. to N.W. railway, May, 1888; and to Oudh and Rohilkhand line, Oct., 1888; locomotive supt.,

Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, Jan., 1889; ditto, North-Western railway, Oct., 1893.

WINNY, THOMAS HERBERT, A.R.I.B.A. (surveyor, India Office).—Apptd. asst. to the surveyor and clerk of the works, India office, 17th Aug., 1885; surveyor, 26th April, 1897.

WINTER, EDGAR FRANCIS LATIMER, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow, Trinity Hall, Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 20th Nov., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1889; dep. supt., family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April, 1893; supt., April, 1894; dep. commr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., Dec., 1896; sec. to govt., Oct., 1898 to March, 1899.

WINTERBOTHAM, HENRY MARTIN, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (member of council, Madras).—Educ. at Portland Gr. Sch., and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived 19th Nov., 1869, and served in Madras as asst. collr. 2nd mag., principal asst., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; collr. and mag., Malabar, Aug., 1888; Tanjore, Nov., 1893; member of rev. board, Sept., 1895; member of council, Madras, Dec., 1898; C.S.I., May, 1900.

WODEHOUSE, THE HON. ARMINE, C.B.—Asst. private sec. (unpaid) to sec. of state for colonies (Earl of Kimberley), 1880; private sec. (unpaid) to sec. of state for India (Earl of Kimberley), Dec., 1882, to 1885; private sec., Feb. to Aug., 1886, and again, from Aug., 1892; private sec. to sec. of state for foreign affairs (Earl of Kimberley), March, 1894, to July, 1895; C.B., July, 1895.

WOLFE-MURRAY, FRANCIS D'ARCY OSBORNE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Harrow, and St. Andrew's Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 19th Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. sessions judge and forest settl. officer, sub-collr. and joint mag., March, 1890; collr. and mag., March, 1893; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1897.

WOLLASTON, ARTHUR NAYLOR, C.I.E. (registrar and supt. of records, India Office).—Educ. at Stockwell Gr. Sch.; entered the India office by open competition as writer, old establt., 3rd March, 1859; junr. clerk, pol. and secret dept., 1860; senr. clerk, Jan., 1873; asst. sec., rev., statistics, and commerce dept., Jan., 1884; in charge of the dept., May to Oct., 1886, and April, 1887, to March, 1888; registrar and supt. of records, July, 1898; in 1877 publ. a transl. of the 'Anvar-i-Suhaili, or Fables of Bidpai'; in 1879 edited Sir Lewis Pelly's miracle play of 'Hasan and Husain'; succeeded Sir F. J. Goldsmid as examr. in Persian at the royal staff coll., Sandhurst, 1880; selected in 1881 by the science and art dept. to superintend the transl. of the oriental inscriptions in the S. Kensington Museum; in 1882 publ. an English-Persian dictionary, and in 1889 a larger work of the same kind; C.I.E., Jan., 1886.

WOLLEY-DOD, F., A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to state railways; services lent to Kashmir state, 1888-89; exec. engr., Oct., 1889; dep. consulting engr. to govt. of India for state

railways, Nov., 1892; on special duty with govt. of India, May, 1897; ret'd., Oct., 1899.

WOMACK, ARTHUR SHAW, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 5th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Central Prov., July, 1885, and served as asst. commr., and registrar of the jud. commr.'s court; judge, small cause court, March, 1892; commr., of excise, Feb., 1893; dep. commr., April, 1893; also pol. agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, May–Nov., 1893, and from April, 1901.

WOOD, ARTHUR LIDBETTER MARTIN, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 13th Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; chief inspr. factories, Feb., 1897; 2nd asst., July, 1897; offg. pol. agent, Jawhar, July, 1899.

WOOD, EDWARD SEPTIMUS, Capt., late Forest Dept., Oudh.—Arrived as lieut., 93rd Sutherland highlanders, 20th Sept., 1857; from Dec., 1859, served in the public works dept. on irrign. works, N.W. Prov.; offd. as conservator of forests, 1864–66; reverted to his regt., Feb., 1866; apptd. exec. engr., March, 1867; from April, 1867, served in the forest dept., Oudh and was apptd. conservator, Oct., 1868; ret'd., May, 1891.

WOOD, JOHN BARRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 11th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. of India, Foreign dept., Nov., 1899.

WOOD, JULIUS JOHN, M.B., Surgn.—Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1868, and served on gen. and jail duty, and as supt. of vaccn.; on famine relief duty in 1874; dep. sanitary commr., Ranchi circle, Sept., 1888; ret'd., Feb., 1892.

WOOD, RICHARD BOARDMAN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; temply. transf'd. to Bombay on famine duty, July, 1900.

WOOD, SAMUEL GEORGE, India Public Works Dept. (examr. of accts.).—Apptd. to the accounts estabt. of the public works dept. as accountant, Sept., 1865, and posted to Central Provinces; dep. examr., Jan., 1882; examr., May, 1887.

WOOD, WALTER GUNNELL, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. eugr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to N. W. Provs. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1894.

WOODBURN, ARCHIBALD FRANCIS, B.L., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Ayr Acad., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; second asst. collr., June, 1885; 1st asst., Sept., 1888; junr. collr. and mag., Haidarabad (Sind), Feb., 1893; collr. and mag. and dist. registrar, March, 1897; acting commr., Jan., 1898; acting chief sec., plague dept., April, 1899; ret'd., Dec., 1900.

WOODBURN, SIR JOHN, K.C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (lieut.-govr. of Bengal).—Educ. at Ayr Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Oudh, Feb., 1865, and served as asst. commr., asst. settlt. officer, and offg. sec. to chief commr.; dep. commr., Faizabad, Aug., 1879; sec. to govt. of the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, in the Oudh rev. dept. and forest branch, Jan., 1882; also in the Oudh pol. dept., March, 1882; sec. to govt. of the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, jud. dept., May, 1884; chief sec., Nov., 1888; member of provl. legisl. council, Dec., 1886; member of viceroy's legisl. council, Feb., 1891; resumed his post as chief sec., April, 1891; again member of viceroy's legisl. council, 1891–92, and 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1892; chief commr. of the Central Prov., Dec., 1893; member of govr.-gen.'s exec. council, Dec., 1895; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1897; lieut.-govr. of Bengal, April, 1898.

WOODCOCK, ELBOROUGH MARTIN, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived as ensign, 9th March, 1856; served with the Persian expedy. force, 1856–57 (medal and clasp); apptd. to Oudh mil. police, 27th Nov., 1858; from March, 1860, served in the dept. as district supt.; reverted to mil. dept., Jan., 1892.

WOODMAN, HENRY CHARLES, B.A., India C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Durham Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 18th Jan., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

WOODROFFE, JAMES TISDALL, B.L.—Apptd. advocate-gen., Calcutta, Dec., 1899.

WOODS, ALBERT EDWARD, Major, I.S.C. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., New Cross, and Sandhurst; entered the army, 22nd Oct., 1881; joined the Assam commn., 12th Aug., 1889, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1893.

WOODWARD, HILLERSDEN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Oakham Gr. Sch., and Birmingham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 28th Oct., 1871, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; forest settlt. officer, Sholapur, Nov., 1883; 2nd asst. collr., May, 1885; 1st asst., July, 1888; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1892; senr. collr., July, 1896; ret'd., July, 1898.

WOODWARD, WILLOUGHBY, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Oakham Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 26th Nov., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag. and district registrar, Panch Mahals, and pol. agent, Rewa Kantha, Aug., 1881; senr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Satara, March, 1885; ret'd., March, 1890.

WOOTTON, JOHN JOSEPH (staff clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 20th Feb., 1872; staff clerk, May, 1901.

WORDSWORTH, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), late Educl. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the educ. service, May, 1861; arrived, 10th July, 1861, and served as head-master of the Elphinstone instn., Bombay, acting profr. in the Elphinstone coll., and asst. inspr. of schs.; from May, 1862, served as principal of the Deccan coll., Poona; principal and profr., Elphinstone

coll., Nov., 1874; also sheriff of Bombay, Dec., 1885; C.I.E., Feb., 1887; retd., 1890.

WORGAN, JOHN BERNEY, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1856; arrived, 16th Dec., 1856; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; from May, 1876, served in the jud. dept., and was apptd. district and sessions judge, 1st grade, July, 1879; retd., Dec., 1891.

WRAY, JOHN WILLOUGHBY, Lieut.-Colonel, Indian Staff Corps (pol. agent, Cutch).—First commn., 12th Nov., 1870; entered pol. employ as asst. pol. agent, Cutch, May 1877; acting asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, Jan., 1885; pol. agent, Kolhapur, and southern Mahratra country, April, 1897; pol. agent, Cutch, June, 1900.

WRIGHT, FREDERICK CHARLES BUCKLEY (senr. clerk, store dept., India Office).—Junr. clerk, 4th July, 1867; senr. clerk, June, 1891.

WRIGHT, HENRY NELSON, Indian C.S. (registrar, high court, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 29th Oct., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1895; under sec. to govt., April, 1897 to Jan., 1899; registrar, high court, Feb., 1901.

WRIGHT, JAMES MCCALLUM, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Fordyce Sch., Univ. of Aberdeen, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov. 1890; asst. commr., April-Oct., 1894; dep. commr., Sept., 1896; mag. and collr., April, 1898.

WRIGHT, JAMES WALKER, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. in July, 1859; served in the Punjab as asst. and exec. engr.; transf. to the Punjab provl. list, Nov., 1879; suptg. engr., July, 1886; offg. chief engr., and sec. to chief commr. of Burma, April, 1890; offg. chief engr., buildings and roads branch, and joint sec. to govt. of Punjab, Sept., 1890; sec. to chief commr. of Assam, June, 1891; retd., Jan., 1893.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM CHARLES, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. proby. asst. engr., Oudh, 23rd Sept., 1864; confd., 1st April, 1865; exec. engr., March, 1871; lent to Cawnpore municipality, Sept., 1884; services lent to Rampur state, Jan., 1890; suptg. engr., May, 1891; retd. 1899.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., late Educ. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd., 7th Aug., 1867, and served as profir., Benares and Muir colls.; principal, Bareilly coll., Dec., 1873; profir., Muir coll., Jan., 1877; principal, Benares coll., Oct., 1888; retd. July, 1897; has publ. a handbook of the educen. dept., an account of the Muir coll., and various educ. works.

WROUGHTON, ROBERT CHARLES (conservator, Bombay Forest Dept.).—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1871; served as asst., and dep. conservator of forests; conservator, Dec., 1895.

WYBROW, GEORGE DIGBY, A.M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, and served in Madras as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Jan., 1892.

WYER, FREDERICK, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Dec., 1861; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, May, 1884; offd. as commr. in 1883 and 1885; retd., May, 1887.

WYER, THOMAS ROWLAND, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. of Glasgow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. officer; asst. commr., Nov., 1884; joint mag., March, 1888; settl. officer in addn., March, 1890; dep. commr., Nov., 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1891; mag. and collr., May, 1894; retd., Nov., 1900.

WYLIE, HENRY, C.S.I., Major-Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Oct., 1861; arrived, 15th Nov., 1861; and took part in the N.W. frontier campaign, 1863, the 2nd Yusufzai expdn. (medal and clasp), the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (clasp), the Abyssinian expdn., 1868 (medal), and the Hazara campaign, 1868 (clasp); on special duty in Baluchistan, with Major R. Sandeman, 1875-77; asst. to govr.-gen. agent, Baluchistan, 1877; served in the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal); C.S.I., Feb., 1881; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, 1882; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1885; Jhalawar, April, 1885; Bhopal, March, 1887; rest., Nepal, Oct., 1893; offg. agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Feb., 1898; retd., April, 1900.

WYLLIE, WILLIAM HUTT CURZON, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. aide-de-camp to the sec. of state for India).—Ensign 106th Foot, Oct., 1866; arrived, Feb., 1867; served in Oudh, 1870-79, as asst. commr.; acted as inspr.-gen. of registrn. in 1875, and as junr. sec. to govt., 1876-77; from Jan., 1879, served under the foreign dept., as cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, asst. commr. in Merwara and Ajmir, and as asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan; served in Madras, 1880-81, as mil. sec. to the govr., and acted as private sec.; C.I.E., May, 1881; pol. agent, in charge of ex-Gackwar of Baroda, Mulhar Rao, 1881; asst. rest., Haidarabad, 1882; asst. commr., Ajmir, Nov., 1883; 1st asst. agent, Rajputana, 1884; addl. pol. agent, Kotah, April, 1885; boundary settl. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Nov., 1886; pol. agent, Kotah, Jan., 1889; offg. commr., Ajmir, July, 1891; offg. pol. agent, Jhalawar, in 1891 and 1892; offg. rest., states of Rajputana, May, 1893; rest., Mewar, March, 1894; rest., West Rajputana states, June, 1898; pol. aide-de-camp to the sec. of state, July, 1901.

WYNCH, LIONEL MALING, B.A., Indian C.S. (private sec. to govr. of Madras).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 7th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to land rev. commrs., Feb., 1894; sec. to commrs. of salt, Abkari, and sep. rev., Oct., 1896; sub. collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1897; collr. and mag., Dec., 1899; private sec. to govr. of Madras, March, 1901.

WYNDHAM, PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Giggleswick Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 13th

Dec., 1839, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April, 1894; dep. commr., Sept., 1899; mag. and collr., March, 1900.

WYNNE, SYDENHAM HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 21st Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. to the collr. and mag., S. Arcot, Oct., 1882; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1887; collr. and mag., Jan., 1890; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1898; ret'd. Aug., 1898.

YATE, CHARLES EDWARD, C.S.I., C.M.G., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (agent to gov.-gen., Baluchistan).—First commn., 9th Nov., 1867; arrived, 27th Feb., 1868; from Aug., 1871, served under the foreign dept.; on special duty, Sind border, 1872 and 1873; boundary settlt. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Sept., 1874; and Mewar-Tonk border, Dec., 1875; offd. for various periods as pol. asst., asst. to supt. for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, &c.; supt. of operations for control of Moghias, Sept., 1879; served in Afghanistan, 1880-81 (medal and clasp, and bronze star); subsequently served in the pol. dept. in Bhopal, Central India, and Bhopawar; attaché with Afghan boundary commn., July, 1884; on duty at Herat in connection with the fortifications, 1885; employed on various special services in connection with the Afghan boundary commn.; pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, March, 1890; offg. rev. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Zhor, March, 1893; on special duty on Russo-Afghan frontier, April, 1893; acting gov.-gen.'s agent and consul-gen., Khorassan and Seistan, Sept., 1893; confd. 1897; rest., Mewar, June, 1898; offg. resdt., Western States, Rajputana, April, 1899; agent to gov.-gen. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1900; author of gazetteers of Mewar, Banswara and Partabgarh, 'Northern Afghanistan,' &c.

YELD, HORACE PARR, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 1874; was employed in the mil. dept. up to Aug., 1884, when he was apptd. prob. in the assay dept., Bombay mint; transf. to Calcutta mint, Sept., 1884; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1886, and Calcutta mint, Jan., 1888; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1892; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

YEWALLD, FRANK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Leys Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 10th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

YOUNG, FRANK POPHAM, Capt., C.I.E., I.S.C. (pol. agent, Phulkian State).—Entered the service, Aug., 1884; in mil. employ till Dec., 1888; apptd. to the Punjab commn. as asst. commr., Dec., 1888; dep. commr., April, 1898; C.I.E., June, 1899; settlt. officer, Patiala State, and pol. agent, Phulkian State, Jan., 1900.

YOUNG, GEORGE EDGAR (2nd class clerk, finl. dept., India Office).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., 22nd May, 1888; transf. to finl. dept., 14th May, 1901.

YOUNG, GEORGE GORDON, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 23rd Oct., 1856; from June, 1858, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and asst. settlt. officer; dep. commr.,

Jan., 1873; commr. and supt., Jan., 1881; offg. and finl. commr., 1888 and 1890; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

YOUNG, HARRY GAVIN (dep. inspr.-gen., Madras Police).—Entered the service, April, 1874, and served as inspr., asst. supt., and supt. of police; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1895.

YOUNG, JAMES, late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., July, 1879; ret'd., 1899.

YOUNG, JAMES DAVID (dist. supt. of police, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1879, and acted as asst. dist. supt.; confd., March, 1881; dist. supt., April, 1885.

YOUNG, MATTHEW, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., May, 1900.

YOUNG, WILLIAM EDLIN, Sind commr. (collr. of customs, Sind).—Entered the service 17th Aug., 1872, and served as extra asst. commr. of customs, Bombay; asst. collr., Feb., 1881; collr. of customs and salt rev. in Sind, Aug., 1900.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM MACKWORTH, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. settlt. officer, and offg. sec. to finl. commr.; offg. under sec. to govt., Oct., 1873; settlt. sec. to finl. commr., March, 1875; dep. commr., Jan., 1878; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, Feb., 1878; sec. to govt., Punjab, Jan., 1880; commr. and supt., May, 1887; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Jan., 1888; commr., Jalandhar divn., April, 1888; 2nd finl. commr. of the Punjab, April, 1889; 1st finl. commr., Feb., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1890; member of viceroy's legisl. council, June-Nov., 1893; prest. of the Indian hemp drugs commn., Oct., 1893; rest., Mysore, and chief commr. of Coorg, Sept., 1895; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, March, 1897; K.C.S.I., June, 1897; ret'd., 1902.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ARTHUR DELAVAL, K.i-H., Indian C.S. (commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby, and Clifton Coll.; apptd. to the Bombay civil service after exam. of 1875; arrived, 9th Dec., 1877, and served as asst. collr. and mag., talukdari settlt. officer, and asst. pol. agent; acted as private sec. to govr. in 1890; and as administrator, Rajpipla state, in 1888 and 1890-91; transf. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., Jan., 1892; also pol. agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, Nov., 1893; commr., April, 1897; and acting pol. agent, April, 1897.

YOUNGHUSBAND, FRANCIS EDWARD, C.I.E., Captain, I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent).—First commn., 10th May, 1882; apptd. to the foreign dept., July, 1889; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, April, 1892; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Oct., 1892; asst. to gov. genl.'s agent, Rajputana, Nov., 1897; pol. agent in Haraoti and Tonk, Sept., 1898.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ROMER EDWARD, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, Clifton Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 14th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer;

junr. sec. to finl. commrs., March, 1886; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1888, to April, 1890; offg. junr. sec., May, 1893; dep. commr., Sept., 1894; chief pol. offr., Tochi Field Force, July, 1897; judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1899.

YUSUF, MOHAMMAD, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Patna Coll. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892,

and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. mag. and collr., Jan., 1896; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1899.

ZORAB, JOHN MANOOK, M.B., C.M., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; served in the mil. dept., and as civil surgn., Balasor, March, 1887, Cuttack, March, 1891, Bahaglpur, April 1896, Cuttack, June, 1897; retd., Dec., 1901.

ADDENDA.

COLESTON, MOST REV. REGINALD STEPHEN, D.D., Bengal Ecclesi. Dept. (Bishop of Calcutta).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Merton Coll., Oxford; Bishop of Colombo, 1875-1902; apptd. Bishop of Calcutta and Metropolitan of India, Jan., 1902.

CRICKMAY, JOSEPH GEORGE, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 17th Jan., 1902.

DAHM, RICHARD HENRY, India Office (2nd class clerk, store dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 1st Jan., 1902.

GRIST, FREDERIC EDWIN, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 23rd Jan., 1902.

KELLAWAY, HERBERT FRANK, India Office (2nd class clerk, store dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 27th Dec., 1901.

MARSHALL, JOHN HUBERT, B.A. (director of archaeology).—Educ. at King's Coll., Cam-

bridge (Porson prize); student at British Sch. at Athens, 1898-99; apptd. dir. of archaeology, Feb., 1902.

MASON, WILLIAM RANDLE, India Office (2nd class clerk, acct.-gen'l's. dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, acct.-gen'l's. dept., 30th Dec., 1901.

ORANGE, HUGH WILLIAM (Director-gen. of Education).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; junior examr., Board of Education, London, 1893-1902; apptd. director-gen. of education in India, 6th March, 1902.

RONCA, EDWARD HENRY, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 3rd Feb., 1902.

SWISS, WALTER EDMUND, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Audit Office, 30th Dec., 1901.

TOMKINS, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 24th Jan., 1902.

CASUALTIES.DEATHS.
Name. Appointment. Date.
Adams, C. A. E. G. .... Provincial Civil Service 20 Aug., 1901.
Afghanistan, H. H. Sir Abdur Rahman Khan, Amir of, G.C.B., G.C.S.I. ... 3 Oct., "
Arbuthnot, F. F. .... Bombay Annuitant 25 May, "
Arbuthnott, Hon. D. .... Madras Annuitant 27 July, "
Ashworth, Lieut. J. G. .... late Indian Medical Service 16 April, "
Badham, Rev. C. H. .... Bombay Ecclesiastical Establishment 19 April, "
Bahauddin Khan, Sardar Bahadur, C.I.E., Risaldar Maj. (Honry. Capt.) ... 18 Sept., "
Baker, M. B. .... Bombay Annuitant 22 June, "
Behrmann, E. A. .... late Public Works Department 11 May, "
Bernard, Sir C. E., K.C.S.I. Bengal Annuitant 19 Sept., "
Birch, Maj.-Gen. F. M., S.C. late Punjab Commission 1 July, "
Bridges, A. .... Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment 6 Sept., "
Brown, P. H. .... Madras Public Works Department 17 April, "
Burne, Gen. H. K., C.B. .... Bengal Staff Corps 7 Nov., "
Cama, Byramji Navroji .... Indian Civil Service Probationer 10 Jan., 1902.
Campbell, Maj.-Gen. J. W. P. .... late Indian Staff Corps 30 Oct., 1901.
Cappel, S. F. L. .... India Forest Service 3 Oct., "
Chamberlain, Field Marshal Sir N. B., G.C.B., G.C.S.I. Indian Army (retired) 17 Feb., 1902.
Chisholm, Col. W. .... late Madras Staff Corps 24 Oct., 1901.
Christie, A. .... Bengal Annuitant 16 March, "
Christie, J. T., C.I.E. .... late Punjab Police 4 Aug., "
Clarke, C. .... Funds Department, India Office 9 Jan., 1902.
Clarke, Lt.-Col. J., M.D. .... Indian Medical Service 15 Feb., 1901.
Collett, Col. Sir H., K.C.B. Bengal Staff Corps 21 Dec., "
Colvin, C. S., C.S.I. .... late Secretary, Public Works Department, India Office 26 Aug., "
Comber, A. B. C. .... Bengal Police 3 May, "
Cook, Lt.-Col. H. D. .... Indian Medical Service 16 Dec., "
Cotton, Maj.-Gen. F. C., C.S.I. Royal (late Madras) Engineers, retired 12 Oct., "
Daly, W. W. .... Bengal Police (retired) 17 May, "
Davidson, Lieut.-Col. D. C. .... Indian Medical Service 2 May, "
De Vismes, Major R.G.R. .... His Majesty's Consul, Pondicherry 24 July, "
Dholpur, Maharana of ... 20 July, "
Dodgson, C.G. .... Indian Civil Service, Bombay Establishment 16 Jan., "
Dufferin and Ava, Marquis of, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E. formerly Viceroy of India, &c., &c. .... 12 Feb., 1902.
Duncan, P. .... late India Public Works Department 24 Nov., 1901.
Elwes, Ven. W. W. .... Madras Ecclesiastical Establishment 22 May, "
Emslie, H. H. .... Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment 27 Oct., "
English, J. .... late Bombay Public Works Department 19 July, "
Ensor, Commander E. H. .... Royal Indian Marine 7 June, "
Fabre-Tonnerre, L. .... Assam Police Department 18 Dec., "
Forbes, G. F. G., I.C.S. Bengal Establishment 10 March, "
Furnivall, W.C. .... late India Public Works Department 16 May, "
Name. Appointment. Date.
Garrett, E.P. ... .. Indian Civil Service, Bombay Establishment 5 April, 1901.
Gee, H. W. ... .. Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment 4 Feb., "
Germany, H.I.M. Victoria, Dow-
ager Empress of, C.I.		 ... .. 5 Aug., "

Griffith, J. T., I.C.S. ... .. Bengal Establishment ... .. 27 May, "
Griffiths, W. ... .. late Bengal Education Department ... .. 23 Jan., 1902.
Hacket, C. A. ... .. late Geological Survey ... .. 23 Feb., 1901.
Haddon, H. E. ... .. late India Public Works Department ... .. 7 Dec., 1900.
Hallen, Vet. Lieut.-Col., J. H. B.,
C.I.E.		 late India Civil Veterinary Department ... .. 1 Aug., 1901.

Harvey, Surg.-Gen. R., C.B. ... Indian Medical Service ... .. 1 Dec., "

Hood, Gen. J. C., C.B. ... .. late Bengal Staff Corps... .. 2 April, "

Jackson, W. G. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 1 May, "

Jones, A. W. ... .. Bombay Annuitant ... .. 16 April, "
Jones, P. ... .. Purser, late Indian Navy ... .. 13 Dec., "
Kean, H. F. J. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 7 Nov., "
Ketchen, Capt. S. D. B. ... .. and in command, Kurram Militia ... .. 9 Dec., "
Kindersley, J. R. ... .. Madras Annuitant ... .. 27 Aug., "
King, W. ... .. late Geological Survey ... .. 5 Dec., 1900.
Krafft, A. V., Ph.D. ... .. Geological Survey of India ... .. 22 Sept., 1901.
Kumarapuram, Sir K. Sheshadri
Aiyar, K.C.S.I.		 ... .. 13 Sept., "

Police		 20 Dec., "
Lemarchand, W. J. ... .. Punjab Police ... .. 18 Mar., "
Loch, Lt.-Col. W., S.C. ... .. late Resident, Nepal ... .. 8 Aug., "
Lungley, Capt. R. B. ... .. Royal Indian Marine ... .. 3 Sept. "
Maclaran, F. B. ... .. Bombay Public Works Department ... .. 1900.
Manderson, Col. T. C. ... .. Royal (late Bengal) Engineers, retired ... .. 3 Nov., 1901.
Mathias, Maj.-Gen. H. V. ... .. late Bengal Staff Corps... .. 3 Feb., "
Maxwell, John... .. Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establish-
ment		 27 April, "
Meherjibhai Kuvarji Tarapurvala,
C.I.E., Khan Bahadur		 Bombay Political Department... .. 14 Feb., "

ministration		 21 Aug., "
Moran, Lt.-Col. J., M.D. ... .. Indian Medical Service... .. 20 Sept., "

Norman, Lieut.-Gen. Sir F. B.,
K.C.B.		 Bengal Staff Corps ... .. 25 June, "

DEATHS—continued.
Name. Appointment. Date.
Parsous, Maj. J. H., S.C. ... .. Burma Commission ... .. 19 Feb., 1901.
Pelly, C. R. ... .. Madras Annuitant ... .. 15 Oct., "
Pemberton, Col. W. W., S.C. ... .. late Burma Commission ... .. 15 Dec., "
Perkins, Gen. Sir Æ., K.C.B., R.
(late Bengal) E. ... ..		 late India Public Works Department ... .. 22 Dec., "
Pughe, R. F. H. ... .. late Bengal Police Department ... .. 15 Oct., "
Ramsay, W. ... .. Bombay Annuitant ... .. 30 Nov., "
Read, R. ... .. Burma Public Works Department ... .. 16 June, "
Reader, G. F., F.G.S. ... .. Geological Survey of India ... .. 12 March, "
Reid, Gen. Sir C., G.C.B. ... .. Bengal Staff Corps ... .. 23 Aug., "
Saiyid Akbar Hasan ... .. Provincial Civil Service, North-Western
Provinces ... ..		 13 April, "

C.S.I. ... ..		 ... .. 27 April, "

Sirdar Bhagat Singh, C.I.E. ... .. Punjab Legislative Council ... .. 30 Oct., "
Smart, T. D. ... .. late Indian Navy... ... 2 May, "
Smith, E. W. ... .. Archæological Surveyor, North-Western
Provinces and Oudh ... ..		 21 Nov., "
Smith, Midshipman S. ... .. late Indian Navy... ... 12 Nov., "
Spankie, Lt.-Col. J. P. W. ... .. Indian Staff Corps ... .. 9 Dec., "
Steinberg, A. F. ... .. Indian Civil Service, Bengal Establishment ... .. 21 Oct., "
Strachey, Sir Arthur, Knt. ... .. Judge, High Court, North-Western Provinces
and Oudh ... ..		 14 May, "
Thompson, J. G. ... .. Madras Annuitant ... .. 6 Aug., "
Toussaint, C. L. ... .. Burma Forest Department ... .. 30 Aug., "
Trotter, E. C. ... .. India Public Works Department ... .. 22 June, "
Turnbull, R., C.I.E. ... .. late Secretary, Calcutta Municipal Council ... .. 25 Nov., "
Wake, H. C. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 9 Dec., "
Wall, R. ... .. Bengal Annuitant ... .. 11 Sept., "
Way, A. D. C. ... .. Inspector of Police, Burma ... .. 15 Dec., "
Wodehouse, Hon. Armine, C.B. ... .. formerly Private Secretary to Secretary of
State for India (Lord Kimberley) ... ..		 1 May, "
Youngusband, A. ... .. Punjab Public Works Department ... .. 20 Dec., "
